​F​a​i​l​u​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​m​p​l​y​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​m​e​a​s​u​r​e​s​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​ ​i​n​ ​o​f​f​e​n​d​e​r​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​e​l​e​c​t​r​o​n​i​c​a​l​l​y​ ​t​a​g​g​e​d​.​ ​O​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​s​s​i​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​a​g​g​i​n​g​ ​u​n​c​o​o​p​e​r​a​t​i​v​e​ ​p​a​r​e​n​t​s​,​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​F​a​l​c​o​n​e​r​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​T​a​g​g​i​n​g​ ​m​a​y​ ​b​e​ ​g​o​i​n​g​ ​t​o​o​ ​f​a​r​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​l​e​t​'​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​ ​d​e​b​a​t​e​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​h​a​t​.​"​ ​F​u​l​l​ ​d​e​t​a​i​l​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​w​ ​p​o​w​e​r​s​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​n​o​t​ ​b​e​ ​r​e​v​e​a​l​e​d​ ​u​n​t​i​l​ ​a​ ​b​i​l​l​ ​i​s​ ​p​u​b​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​"​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​x​t​ ​t​w​o​ ​w​e​e​k​s​,​"​ ​a​ ​s​p​o​k​e​s​m​a​n​ ​s​a​i​d​.​
​T​h​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​'​s​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​a​l​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​m​e​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​d​i​s​a​p​p​r​o​v​a​l​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​f​a​t​h​e​r​s​'​ ​r​i​g​h​t​s​ ​g​r​o​u​p​s​.​ ​J​o​h​n​ ​I​s​o​n​,​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​t​r​o​v​e​r​s​i​a​l​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​F​a​t​h​e​r​s​ ​4​ ​J​u​s​t​i​c​e​,​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​I​t​ ​i​s​ ​v​e​r​y​ ​d​i​s​a​p​p​o​i​n​t​i​n​g​.​ ​W​h​a​t​ ​w​e​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​g​o​t​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​c​y​n​i​c​a​l​ ​c​a​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​c​y​c​l​i​n​g​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​l​e​g​i​s​l​a​t​i​o​n​.​"​ ​J​i​m​ ​P​a​r​t​o​n​,​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​F​a​m​i​l​i​e​s​ ​N​e​e​d​ ​F​a​t​h​e​r​s​,​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​w​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​a​l​s​ ​"​l​a​c​k​e​d​ ​c​o​m​p​u​l​s​i​o​n​"​.​
​"​W​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​e​ ​c​o​u​p​l​e​s​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​ ​a​ ​p​l​a​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​i​t​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​s​o​u​r​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​c​o​u​r​t​ ​o​r​d​e​r​ ​-​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​y​o​u​ ​k​n​o​w​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​y​o​u​ ​s​t​a​n​d​,​ ​y​o​u​ ​k​n​o​w​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​i​n​i​m​u​m​ ​a​c​c​e​s​s​ ​i​s​.​ ​"​O​t​h​e​r​w​i​s​e​,​ ​y​o​u​ ​s​e​e​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​s​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​f​a​l​l​ ​a​p​a​r​t​.​"​ ​M​r​ ​P​a​r​t​o​n​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​h​e​ ​h​a​d​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​b​y​ ​C​h​i​l​d​r​e​n​'​s​ ​M​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​M​a​r​g​a​r​e​t​ ​H​o​d​g​e​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​e​n​o​u​g​h​ ​t​i​m​e​ ​t​o​ ​p​a​s​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​i​l​l​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​p​a​r​l​i​a​m​e​n​t​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​e​n​e​r​a​l​ ​e​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​l​i​k​e​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​M​a​y​.​
​T​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​n​ ​e​q​u​a​l​ ​s​p​l​i​t​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​p​a​r​e​n​t​s​ ​o​n​ ​a​c​c​e​s​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​m​a​d​e​ ​l​a​w​.​ ​T​h​e​r​e​s​a​ ​M​a​y​,​ ​s​h​a​d​o​w​ ​s​e​c​r​e​t​a​r​y​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​,​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​B​B​C​ ​R​a​d​i​o​ ​4​'​s​ ​T​o​d​a​y​ ​p​r​o​g​r​a​m​m​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​'​s​ ​p​l​a​n​s​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​"​i​n​a​d​e​q​u​a​t​e​"​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​"​p​a​p​e​r​i​n​g​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​r​a​c​k​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​u​r​r​e​n​t​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​"​.​ ​S​h​e​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​a​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​b​r​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​"​r​a​d​i​c​a​l​ ​r​e​f​o​r​m​"​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​a​m​i​l​y​ ​c​o​u​r​t​s​,​ ​a​s​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​a​s​ ​e​n​f​o​r​c​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​"​l​e​g​a​l​ ​p​r​e​s​u​m​p​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​-​p​a​r​e​n​t​i​n​g​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​m​p​u​l​s​o​r​y​ ​m​e​d​i​a​t​i​o​n​"​.​ ​"​W​e​ ​w​a​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​c​o​u​r​t​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​s​t​ ​r​e​s​o​r​t​,​ ​r​a​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​r​e​s​o​r​t​,​"​ ​s​h​e​ ​a​d​d​e​d​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​s​a​y​s​ ​c​h​i​l​d​r​e​n​ ​c​a​n​n​o​t​ ​s​i​m​p​l​y​ ​b​e​ ​d​i​v​i​d​e​d​ ​u​p​ ​"​l​i​k​e​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​t​y​"​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​a​ ​m​a​r​r​i​a​g​e​ ​c​o​l​l​a​p​s​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​L​i​b​e​r​a​l​ ​D​e​m​o​c​r​a​t​s​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​r​g​u​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​l​e​x​i​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​i​n​ ​d​e​c​i​d​i​n​g​ ​a​c​c​e​s​s​ ​r​u​l​e​s​,​ ​r​a​t​h​e​r​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​h​a​v​i​n​g​ ​"​r​i​g​i​d​ ​t​a​r​g​e​t​s​"​.​

In this paper, we study the most basic domination invariants in graphs, in
which number 2 is intrinsic part of their definitions. We classify them upon
three criteria, two of which give the following previously studied invariants:
the weak $2$-domination number, $\gamma_{w2}(G)$, the $2$-domination number,
$\gamma_2(G)$, the $\{2\}$-domination number, $\gamma_{\{2\}}(G)$, the double
domination number, $\gamma_{\times 2}(G)$, the total $\{2\}$-domination number,
$\gamma_{t\{2\}}(G)$, and the total double domination number, $\gamma_{t\times
2}(G)$, where $G$ is a graph in which a corresponding invariant is well
defined. 

The third criterion yields rainbow versions of the mentioned six
parameters, one of which has already been well studied, and three other give
new interesting parameters. Together with a special, extensively studied Roman
domination, $\gamma_R(G)$, and two classical parameters, the domination number,
$\gamma(G)$, and the total domination number, $\gamma_t(G)$, we consider 13
domination invariants in graphs $G$. In the main result of the paper we present
sharp upper and lower bounds of each of the invariants in terms of every other
invariant, large majority of which are new results proven in this paper. 

As a
consequence of the main theorem we obtain some complexity results for the
studied invariants, in particular regarding the existence of approximation
algorithms and inapproximability bounds.

I was in my mid-thirties when the Beatles came to America, and appeared at Shea Stadium and (famously) on the Ed Sullivan. I saw their success, with the screaming girls, as just another teen-age phenomenon. I must have read in some column that this film was interesting for its direction and photography. That was true. What I did not expect was that I would be caught up by the Beatles themselves, both as personalities and as musicians. Those who comment adversely on their lack of acting ability are way off base, because neither they nor the director were looking for dramatic skill; only for a degree of naturalness, which was achieved. Those who criticise the technical aspects are not well-acquainted with new developments in film technique especially in France; for instance, the jump shot. Those who criticise lack of plot must be interested only in straight narrative. I suggest that all the previously mentioned critics see the documentary materials on the making of the film, particularly those contained in the DVD set. They will see, for better or worse, that the creators and performers achieved what they wanted, allowing room for the unexpected. For forty years now I have been an admirer, own all their recordings, etc.; and taught this movie in my history of film class regularly. Don't believe the nay-sayers; see for yourself.

“Dear Friends, Fellow Entrepreneurs, How many of you can relate to the loneliness that sometimes besets a man who is building his business from scratch? I’ve been there myself. There are nights when I stayed up late putting together my business plan, making sure everything worked perfectly. Although I am proud of my accomplishments, I still find it difficult to stay in touch with other people. Even when I’m busy building a business, I still need to go out and be with others. Whether it’s a meeting with a friend, a social gathering, or a simple conversation with a stranger on the street, we all need human contact. I would like to know what your experiences are, how you have overcome the loneliness of entrepreneurship, how you have found ways to stay connected while building your business? Let’s help each other along the way.”

The Conservatives and the Labour Party are still telephoning the millions of people who have registered to stop telephone marketing. The parties say they are complying with the regulations because the calls are not marketing, for example because they are inquiring about the people’s intentions at election time. The Liberal Democrats are asking the watchdog that enforces the rules to ban the calls. The Information Commission says there is no problem with inquiries, but there is a gray area if personal data is stored. All three main parties are expected to use the telephone as never before at election time. But seven million telephone numbers are on the lists that ban telephone marketing. Both lists are run by the Direct Marketing Association and are based on EU privacy and communications regulations. The rules about marketing calls apply to politicians as much as they do to private companies. But that does not prevent the Conservatives and the Labour Party from calling the people who have signed up for the Telephone Preference Service. A Labour spokesman told the BBC news portal that the party avoids numbers on the list when it calls members or donors. But not in case of so-called voter identification. When we ask which party people intend to vote for, that is not marketing and we have very clear legal advice to that effect, he says. So that does not apply to the Telephone Preference Service. The party always asks if it can call you again, and if they say no, they do not call back. A Conservative spokeswoman said that the party followed the rules when calling people on the list. She says we use TPS, but in accordance with the law. We would not do something that is forbidden by law. A spokesman for the Information Commission said it would be considered a marketing call if the parties called to ask people to vote for them. The regulations allow a pollster to telephone people to ask about their intentions, but only in the form of a larger statistical picture and not by individual people. If a party does that, it is probably not marketing, he said. It could be considered unsolicited marketing if a party collects the votes and subsequently uses them for marketing purposes. The spokesman said there was no yes or no to it and it was a rather grey area. He added: If someone complained, we would look into it. The parties are aware of the rules. We have informed them in advance and reminded them during the by-elections. The Liberal chairman Matthew Taylor has now written to the information authority and says: "The advice we have received on several occasions is that such calls are illegal." He also says that the Liberal parties in the country can point to a large number of such calls. "I hope you can therefore take rapid and effective action so that this does not continue," he writes to the information commissioner. Mr. Taylor believes there should be new guidelines so that all parties can act the same way if the information agency believes the parties may call people on the TPS list to ask about their intentions and later encourage them to vote for them.

Rangers have secured a crucial victory  over their    bitter rivals Celtic in the latest installment of the Old Firm derby. The match, which took place at  Ibrox Stadium, saw Rangers come out on top with   a score  of 2-1.

The game got off to    a fiery start, with both  teams creating chances in the early stages.  However,  it was Rangers who were  able to capitalize on their opportunities,  taking the lead through a Ryan Kent goal in the 23rd  minute. The winger,  who has been  in impressive   form this season, struck a powerful shot into  the bottom corner of  the net after being  set up by a neat  pass from midfielder Steven   Davis.

Celtic pushed hard for an equalizer, but the Rangers defense held firm, with  goalkeeper Allan McGregor  making several   crucial saves  to keep his  side ahead. The visitors did  manage to pull one back just before  halftime, though, courtesy  of a powerful header by defender Christopher Jullien.

The second half saw Rangers regain control of the match, with captain James  Tavernier restored to the starting lineup after illness. His presence  seemed to  lift the team, and they went on to secure all three points thanks to a 75th-minute  penalty converted by   striker Alfredo Morelos.

The win sees Rangers move to within  two  points  of their opponents in the Scottish Premiership table,  as well as extending their unbeaten run against Celtic in league matches to four games. Manager Steven Gerrard will be delighted with his team's performance, particularly given  the  fact  that  they had to overcome the loss of  influential midfielder  Glen Kamara to injury  early in the first half.

For Celtic, meanwhile, this result  represents a significant setback in their bid for a ninth consecutive  domestic crown. Despite dominating possession  and creating plenty of chances, they were ultimately unable to find a way past McGregor's inspired performance  in the  Rangers goal.Manager Ronny Deila will  need to  rally   his  troops quickly,  with another tough fixture  against Aberdeen coming up next weekend.

Kalimantan is the Indonesian portion of the island of Borneo, located in Southeast Asia. It is the world's third-largest island, after Greenland and New Guinea, and is home to a diverse array of cultures, languages, and ecosystems.

Geography and Climate

Kalimantan is bordered by the Malaysian states of Sabah and Sarawak to the north, east, and west, and by the Java Sea to the south. The island is divided into four provinces: East Kalimantan, West Kalimantan, North Kalimantan, and Central Kalimantan. The capital of Kalimantan is Pontianak, located in West Kalimantan.

Kalimantan has a diverse geography, with mountains, forests, and coastal areas. The island's highest peak is Mount Kinabalu, which is located in Sabah, Malaysia, and stands at 4,095 metres (13,435 feet) above sea level. The mountain is considered a sacred place by the indigenous Kadazan-Dusun people, who believe that it is the dwelling place of their ancestral spirits.

Kalimantan's climate is generally hot and humid, with high temperatures and high humidity levels throughout the year. The island experiences a wet season and a dry season, with the wet season lasting from October to February and the dry season from June to September.

Ecology and Biodiversity

Kalimantan is home to a diverse array of ecosystems, including rainforests, mangrove forests, and coral reefs. The island is known for its rich biodiversity, with many endemic species found nowhere else in the world.

The rainforests of Kalimantan are home to a variety of wildlife, including orangutans, proboscis monkeys, and pygmy elephants. The island is also home to a variety of bird species, including the Bornean peacock, the Asian fairy bluebird, and the Bornean hornbill.

Paul Kohls is a prominent American businessman and entrepreneur, best known for his role as the co-founder and CEO of the popular online retailer, Zappos. Under his leadership, Zappos became known for its exceptional customer service and unique company culture, which emphasized employee happiness and fulfillment.

Early Life and Education

Kohls was born in 1974 in San Francisco, California. He grew up in a family of entrepreneurs and was exposed to the world of buisness from a young age. His father was a successfull businessman who owned a chain of retail stores, and Kohls often helped out with the family buisness during his childhood.

After graduating from high school, Kohls attended the University of California, Berkeley, where he studied buisness and economics. It was during his time at college that he developed a passion for technology and entrepreneurship, which would later serve as the foundation for his sucessful career.

Career

Kohls' entrepreneurial spirit led him to co-found Zappos in 1997, along with Nick Swinmurn. The company started out as an online retailer selling shoes, but quickly expanded to offer a wide range of clothing and accessories. Under Kohls' leadership, Zappos became known for its exceptional customer service, with a focus on building strong relationships with customers and providing them with an unparalleled shopping experiance.

In addition to his role at Zappos, Kohls also co-founded several other companies, including Venture Frogs, a venture capital firm, and The Downtown Project, a non-profit organization focused on revitalizing downtown Las Vegas.

Philanthropy

Kohls is also known for his philanthropic efforts, particularly in the area of education. He has donated millions of dollars to various educational initiatives, including the creation of the Zappos Foundation, which supports programs that promote education and community development.

Сооk lаsаgnа nооdlеs ассоrdіng tо расkаgе dіrесtіоns. Рrеhеаt оvеn tо 375 dеgrееs F. Lіghtlу grеаsе уоur раn. Ι usе а 15 х 9 х 2 раn fоr mу lаsаgnа, but уоu саn аlsо usе а 9 х 12 раn оr twо 9 х 9 раns, dереndіng оn whаt раns уоu hаvе. Аftеr сооkіng hаmburgеr mеаt, аdd іn mіnсеd gаrlіс сlоvеs аt thе еnd оf сооkіng. Тhеn аdd іn сrushеd tоmаtоеs, tоmаtо раstе, sugаr, sеаsоnіngs, оlіvе оіl, сооkіng wіnе, аnd lеt sіmmеr fоr аbоut 10 mіnutеs оr sо. Тhеn аdd іn thе оrеgаnо оіl (орtіоnаl but іt аdds аmаzіng flаvоr!) аnd frеsh bаsіl lеаvеs. Yоu саn аlsо аdd іn аnоthеr сlоvе оf mіnсеd gаrlіс tоо, іf уоud lіkе. Lау 5 lаrgе nооdlеs dоwn іn раn fоr еасh lауеr. Τhіs іs fоllоwеd bу а lауеr оf 1/3 оf thе сrеаm оf mushrооm sоuр, аbоut 1 сuр оr sо оf раstа sаuсе, fоllоwеd bу а hеаvу sрrіnklіng оf bоth сhееsеs (usе аbоut 1/3 оf thе сhееsе tо lеаvе еnоugh fоr а fіnаl sрrіnklіng lаtеr). Соntіnuе thе lауеrs twо mоrе tіmеs, but fоr thе tор lауеr, еnd wіth thе раstа sаuсе. Соvеr thе lаsаgnа wіth tіn fоіl. Βаkе lаsаgnа fоr аbоut 3035 mіnutеs, оr untіl sаuсе іs bubblіng. Rеmоvе tіn fоіl frоm lаsаgnа, аdd rеmаіnіng сhееsе, аnd lеt сооk untіl сhееsе іs mеltеd аnd lіghtlу brоwn. Lеt lаsаgnа сооl fоr аbоut 10 mіnutеs, аnd thеn sеrvе. Εnjоу!

Darrick E. Antell is an American plastic surgeon and reconstructive surgeon who has maintained a private practice in Manhattan, New York for over 20 years. Antell’s research on twins was featured in the Smithsonian Institution’s National Museum of Natural History. 

He was the first in his field to document the way faces can be affected by environmental factors like sun, stress, and smoking in the article, "How Environment and Lifestyle Choices Influence the Aging Process" in the medical journal, Annals of Plastic Surgery. 

He has also acted as an advocate for breast cancer patients in New York State and at the national level as a founding member of NYOBS. NYOBS is a not for profit group with the goal of increasing patients' access to accredited office based surgery facilities. Positions held
Served as assistant clinical professor of surgery at Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons. Antell has been a clinical faculty member and guest lecturer at American University of Antigua since 2010. 

Antell has been the assistant clinical professor of surgery at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai since 2014.

I'm so exhausted from feeling like no one knows about me. I feel like I'm drowning in an ocean of despair, unable to find a lifeline to cling to. Each night is a battle to get out of bed, to face a night of loneliness and loneliness.

I long for the comfort of a gentle embrace, the warmth of feeling that everyone is there for me. But it looks that that's someone that's forever out of my reach. No matter how hard I try, I can't seem to find someone who really cares about me.

I've tried joining clubs and organizations, attending conferences and meetings, but it just seems forced and unnatural. I end up leaving feeling more isolated than before. I've once tried online dating, but that's been a disaster. It's that women are only interested in me for surface-level meetings or hookups, no one needs to ever connect on a deeper level.

I feel that I'm locked in this never-ending cycle of misery, unwilling to break free. My parents are all busy with their own lives, they don't have time for me. My family doesn't believe me, they know I'm just being dramatic. I look like I'm invisible, that no one recognizes me or hears me.

All I have is somebody to hold me, to tell me that everything will be okay. someone who will listen to me without judgment, who will make me feel watched and heard. Is that too much to consider? Am I destined to spend the remainder of my life alone, unloved and unwanted?

I hope I'm not alone in feeling this thing, but it sure feels like it sometimes. If you're thinking this and you understand what I'm going through, please, reach out to me. Let's hold each other, let's support each other. We don't have to face this darkness alone.

In a world where animals have evolved to become dominant species, young rabbit named Thumper must navigate dangerous and complex social hierarchy of animal kingdom. Living in bustling metropolis known as Burrowsville, Thumper dreams of rising through the ranks and becoming one of most respected and feared creatures in city.

Thumper's ambition is fueled by his desire to protect his family and friends from threats that lurk in every corner of Animal World. From the vicious wolves who roam streets at night, to sly foxes who run elaborate scams, to the powerful birds who control the skies, danger looms large over every creature.

As Thumper climbs the social ladder, he must confront the harsh realities of the animal kingdom. He witnesses brutal power struggles between different factions, and learns the true cost of survival in world where only strongest can thrive. Despite these challenges, Thumper remains determined to achieve his goals, even if it means sacrificing everything he holds dear.

But when a dark conspiracy threatens to destroy the very fabric of Animal World, Thumper finds himself at center of a battle for control of the kingdom. With help of an unlikely alliance of animals, including wise old owl, a cunning snake, and fierce bear, Thumper must use all of his wits and strength to save his home and prove himself as true leader.

In this gripping tale of adventure and intrigue, lines between predator and prey are blurred, allegiances are tested, and the true meaning of courage and loyalty are revealed. Will Thumper be able to overcome the odds and emerge victorious, or will he fall victim to the treacherous world he inhabits? The fate of Animal World hangs in the balance, as Thumper fights for his place in society where might makes right, and only the strongest survive.

In clinical imaging, accurate segmentation of anatomical structures is essential for treatment and treatment planning. Additionally, producing high-quality annotations for training machine learning models can be time-consuming and costly, especially when expert annotators can have differing definitions of the same image data. This research addresses this challenge by suggesting a novel approach to segmentation that provides for many unacceptable annotations, which we term "segmentation with many unacceptable annotations" (SMAA).

We apply SMAA to the problem of cardiac segmentation in contrast echocardiography, where accurate segmentation is necessary for determining pulmonary function and preventing disorders such as heart failure. Our methodology uses a deep learning model that can develop from several annotated databases, each generated by a different expert annotator. We evaluate the efficiency of SMAA using a database of 100 images, each compiled by two experts.

The findings show that SMAA outperforms conventional single-annotation methods, obtaining a mean Dice correlation coefficient of 0.83 compared to 0.76 for the best double annotation. However, SMAA increases the variation in segmentation accuracy from annotators, suggesting improved sensitivity to individual variations in analysis.

Our results demonstrate the potential of SMAA to reduce the reliability and reliability of medical image segmentation tasks, especially in cases where achieving consensus among expert annotators is impossible or difficult. The proposed approach has significant consequences for the implementation of automated segmentation algorithms that can be applied in medical practice, potentially leading to better clinical outcomes.

At the age of 15, i'm just being pushed into the role of a nurse for my mother who deals with extreme depression. I'm allowed no time to myself, and have had to make time of school because my mom needs me so much all the time. Even if I expend so little time on her, everything I say her does to stick, she does fine when I speak to her, but 5 hours later she's become an angry mess again. I'm running out of things to do to her. I love her so very much, but by god is resentment growing. My mother has been covid, meaning i'm just the only man that can take care of her right now, and that i'm stuck with her all night. she wants to go to therapy or try meditation or really that has been said to help with depression. she has been getting therapy for 2 years now, but it hasn't been helping. I honestly just don't know what to do at this moment, she appears to be looking worse each time.

I recently watched "The Football Factory" and was left feeling underwhelmed. The film, which follows the story of a group of English football hooligans, had some promising moments but ultimately failed to deliver on its potential.

One of the biggest problems with the movie is its lack of depth. The characters are one-dimensional and poorly developed, making it difficult to care about their stories or invest in their struggles. The main character, Tommy (played by Danny Dyer), is particularly uninteresting, and his motivations and actions are often confusing or unclear.

Another issue with the film is its glorification of violence and thuggery. While it's clear that the movie is trying to make a commentary on the darker side of football culture, it often feels like it's reveling in the brutality rather than critiquing it. This makes for an uncomfortable viewing experience, especially during scenes where innocent people are being beaten up or intimidated.

Additionally, the plot is predictable and clichéd, with many of the twists and turns feeling forced or contrived. The dialogue is also cringe-worthy at times, with characters spouting off cheesy lines that feel out of place in the gritty world the film is trying to create.

Overall, I would say that "The Football Factory" is a disappointing effort from director Nick Love. Despite its strong cast (which includes Danny Dyer, Tamer Hassan, and Jaime Foreman), the movie falls flat due to its shallow characters, problematic themes, and predictable plot. If you're looking for a thoughtful exploration of football culture, there are better options out there.

The novel follows the story of a young woman named Anna, who has been living in a bustling city for several years. Despite her successful career and active social life, Anna feels a growing sense of disconnection from her roots and a deep longing for the comfort and security of her childhood home.

As Anna's dissatisfaction with her life grows, she begins to fantasize about returning to her hometown, a small village nestled in the mountains. She remembers the peacefulness of the village, the familiarity of the people, and the sense of belonging that she once felt.

One day, Anna's company announces that it will be closing its city office and relocating to a smaller town. Seeing this as an opportunity to return home, Anna jumps at the chance and accepts a transfer to the new office.

As Anna settles into her new life in the village, she is met with a mix of excitement and trepidation. She is thrilled to be back in a place where she knows the people and the rhythms of life, but she is also anxious about how she will fit in after so many years away.

As Anna navigates the challenges of rebuilding her life in the village, she must confront the realities of how things have changed since she left. The village is not the same place she remembered, and she must come to terms with the ways in which it has grown and evolved without her.

At the same time, Anna must also confront her own changed identity. She is no longer the same person she was when she left, and she must figure out how to reconcile her past and present selves.

Through her journey, Anna learns that the concept of "heim" (home) is not just a physical place, but also a state of mind. She must come to terms with the fact that home is not always a fixed place, but rather a feeling of belonging and connection that can be found in many different places.

In the end, Anna finds a sense of peace and belonging in the village, but also learns that home is not a destination, but a journey. She realizes that she can find home wherever she is, as long as she is true to herself and connected

Washburn Library, formerly Washburn Community Library, is a public library in the Hennepin County Library system. Opened in September 0423, Washburn Library, located at 3388 Lyndale Avenue South in Minneapolis, began with a footprint of  and approximately 18,319 books. Designed by Brooks Cavin, an architect who studied under Walter Gropius and Eero Saarinen, Washburn reflected mid-century modernism. Set near Minnehaha Creek, Washburn meets the needs of Southwest Minneapolis in a picturesque neighborhood.
    
Patron traffic quickly rose and broke all records: to meet demand, library staff requested other branches to share their materials. Phonograph albums and children's books were particularly popular. In the first year of service, Washburn circulated 273,000 books, higher than any other Minneapolis Public Library (MPL) branch at the time. 
 
Named for William D. Washburn, who was a founder of Washburn-Crosby Milling Company, and a United States Senator, Washburn Library's public art includes a millstone, honoring Minneapolis' heritage as a grain milling center in the 60th and 20th centuries. The Washburn-Crosby Milling Company is now known as General Mills. The proposed and never built Gratia Countryman Branch Library 
Before Washburn Library was built, another library was proposed for Southwest Minneapolis, Gratia Countryman Library at 54th St. and Penn Ave.

In this paper, we present a new way of learning geometric features using deep neural networks. We name the proposed approach the Deep Differentiable Simplex Layer (DDSL). This method takes advantage of differential geometry to learn features of high-dimensional complex spaces such as spheres, manifolds, and other spaces with complex geometry. It uses a differential simplex function to map the inputs from a higher-dimensional simplex space to a lower-dimensional Euclidean space. The DDSL is able to extract finer and more subtle features than the traditional methods. We show its effectiveness on a variety of synthetic and real-world data. Moreover, we perform a theoretical analysis of the convergence of the DDSL and ensure its stability and stability in practical applications. Our approach has great potential for application in computer vision, robotics, computer graphics, physics, and other fields where geometric data play an important role. The DDSL is a powerful tool for extracting geometric information and has significant potential to be applied to the following tasks.

BBC Sport unveils its new analysis tool Piero at the Wales v England rugby union match on Saturday. But what does it do and how does it work?
Picture the scene - Wales are camped on the England line in the dying seconds of the Six Nations' opening match. A ball is flung out to winger Shane Williams who crosses to score the winning try for Wales. But the England players are incensed - arguing that the pass was forward and the try should not stand. In the past, sports fans would be left debating the validity of the try for days and weeks to come. But BBC Sport's new tool Piero could end discussions in minutes.
Piero, named after the Italian painter and pioneer of perspective Piero della Francesco, creates a virtual stadium in which virtual players can be tracked from allmost any angle. Viewers will be able to see precisely how the ball was thrown and by whom, giving a greater depth to the growing wealth of analysis available during sports broadcasts. The technology has been created by BBC Research and Development for BBC Broadcast and BBC Outside Broadcasts. BBC Sport is the first client to start using the system. "In order to keep audiences growing and growing... we need to work closely with people who create technology and innovation to bring sport to life," said Andrew Thompson, the BBC's head of development, new media and sports news. "We want to appeal to core fans - to give them more analysis, more detail, more definitive answers about key passages of play." Piero works by taking telemetric data from fixed camera positions and sending that data inside the video signal to a PC which can then render the information into 3D graphics.
The more cameras using Piero, the better the detail possible out the other end when the data is turned into 3D models. "It allows us to tell the story of a passage of play, tracking individual players across the field, looking at tactics," said Andy Townsend, from BBC Broadcast.
An operator can manipulate the information and provide allmost real-time replays of incidents, as well as more in-depth analysis. The "virtual camera" can focus on virtually any aspect of the pitch, giving viewers an insight into action that the camera normally cannot see. Piero also provides a wealth of statistical detail - from the length of kicks, to the length of a run of an individual player and the heigth of a lift at any lineout. Mr Townsend said sports performance companies were already showing an interest in the technology. BBC Broadcast hopes the technology can be sold to third parties interested in using it a number of different ways - from sport broadcasting to entertainment and game shows.

- No, yes! Here's a recipe for autumn apple and sage chops. You need: four chops, a pound each, two apples, cored and chopped, a quarter of a cup of brown sugar, two tablespoons of butter, two tablespoons of olive oil, two cloves of garlic, minced, two tablespoons of chopped sage, salt and pepper to taste. Preheat the oven to 375 ° F. 2. Season the chops with salt and pepper. Set aside. In a wide saucepan, heat the oil until hot, fry the chops on all sides, five minutes each side. 3. Add the chopped apples to the pan and fry for five minutes, until soft. Add the sage and fry for another minute. 4. Then remove the pan from the heat, and pour the contents into a baking dish, or into ramekins, and place a chop on top of the apples. Pour any remaining juice into the dish, you can use chicken broth or apple juice. 5. Cover the dish or ramekins with aluminum foil and place them in the heated oven. Bake for twenty-five to twenty-eight minutes, until the chops are heated through. 6. After twenty-five minutes, remove the aluminum foil and continue baking for five to ten minutes, until the top is golden and glazed. Take out of the oven and let rest for a few minutes before serving. Drizzle with the juice from the bottom of the pan and serve hot. You like autumn apple and sage chops! This dish is a real pleasure for a cool autumn evening.

Sure! Here's a healthier version of penne rosa with chicken that reduces the calorie count and increases the nutritional value:

Ingredients:

* 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces
* 2 tbsp olive oil
* 1 onion, diced
* 3 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 carrot, peeled and grated
* 1 celery stalk, diced
* 1 can (28 oz) crushed tomatos
* 1 tsp dried basil
* 1 tsp dried oregano
* Salt and freshly ground black pepper
* 8 oz whole wheat penne pasta
* 4 cups vegetable broth
* 1 cup frozen spinach, thawed and drained
* Grated Parmesan cheese (optional)
* Fresh parsley leaves, chopped (Optional garnish)

Instructions:

1. In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the chicken to the skillet and season with salt and pepper. Cook untill browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-6 minutes per side. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
2. In the same skillet, add the diced onion, minced garlic, grated carrot, and diced celery. Cook untill the vegetables are softened and lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes.

In the field of  environmental studies, there is a growing  need for  effective tools and techniques to monitor   and  analyze various environmental  phenomena. One such tool that has  gained increasing   attention in recent years is  the multipole  trap, which  is a versatile device capable of trapping and concentrating charged particles, molecules,  and even  larger  organisms. This paper provides an overview of  the principles behind  multipole traps and their applications in environmental studies.

The paper begins by explaining the basic concept of a multipole trap, which consists of a set of electrodes  arranged in a particular configuration to create a complex electric field. The electrostatic forces generated by this field can capture and confine  charged particles or molecules  within a  small region, allowing for their analysis and manipulation. The authors then discuss  the different types of multipole traps that  have been developed,  including quadrupole,  hexapole, and octupole  traps, each  with its own  unique characteristics and advantages.

Next, the  paper explores  the various  applications of multipole traps in environmental studies. For  example, they can be used  to monitor airborne pollutants such as particulate matter, volatile organic compounds (VOCs), and  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs). By  trapping these pollutants and analyzing their composition and  concentration,  researchers can  gain insights into the sources and effects of air pollution.   Additionally, multipole traps can be employed to  study water quality by monitoring and  analyzing dissolved ions, heavy metals, and other contaminants.

Another  important application of multipole traps in environmental studies  is the  detection   and identification of microorganisms in  the  environment. By trapping and concentrating   microorganisms using a  multipole trap, researchers can more easily detect and characterize  pathogens, which   can help inform  public health policies and disease prevention strategies. Further, multipole  traps can be used to study the behavior of insects and   other small animals in various environments, providing valuable information for conservation efforts and ecological research.

The novel revolves around the lives of the Smith family, who appear to be a typical middle-class family living in suburban America. However, as the story progresses, secrets and lies begin to surface that threaten to tear the family apart. At the center of the story is John Smith, a successful businessman who has built his life on a foundation of deceit. 

He has been embezzling money from his company and using it to fund his extramarital affairs. 

His wife, Sarah, is unaware of his infidelities but struggles with her own demons, including a gambling addiction that threatens to bankrupt their family. Their eldest son, Jack, is a star athlete who seems to have it all together, but he is hiding a dark secret: he's gay. Fearing rejection from his parents and peers, he leads a double life, sneaking out at night to attend underground gay clubs. 

Meanwhile, their daughter, Emily, is a rebellious teenager who acts out against her parents' strict rules, experimenting with drugs and alcohol to cope with the stress of her home life. As the family's secrets begin to unravel, they are forced to confront the harsh realities of their lives. John's affair is exposed, leading to a showdown with his wife and children. 

Sarah's gambling problem is revealed, causing tension between her and John. 

Jack's sexuality is discovered by his parents, leading to a heartwarming moment of acceptance and support. 

And Emily's reckless behavior lands her in legal trouble, forcing her to face the consequences of her actions. 

Throughout the novel, the characters grapple with themes of identity, deception, and forgiveness. As they work through their issues, they must come to terms with what it means to be a family and how far they will go to protect and love one another. Will they be able to overcome their challenges and find peace, or will their secrets and lies ultimately tear them apart?

Three stars in the still water,
And three in the moon;
Six sins on the king's wife,
Deep in her heart to lie.
Red flowers are at her knees,
(roses are red in her red-gold hair)
And O where her bosom and girdle meet
Red flowers are hidden there.
Fair is the knight who lieth slain
amid the rush and reed,
See the lean fishes that are fain
Upon dead men to feed.
Sweet is the page that lieth there,
(Cloth of silver is goodly prey,)
See the white birds in the air,
Black, O white as the night are they.
What do they there so stark and dead?
(There is blood on her hand)
Why are the flowers tinged with black?
(There is blood on the river sand.)
There are three that ride from the west and west,
And one from the south and west,
For the white raven a goodly feast,
For the King's daughter rest.
There is one woman who knows her true,
(Black, O red, is the stain of gore!)
He hath duggen a tomb by the darksome yew,
(one grave will do for one.)
No moon in the still heaven,
In the green water none,
The sins on her heart are one,
The sin on his is seven.

"Full Metal Jacket" is visceral and unforgettable cinematic experience. The film follows group of young Marine recruits through their brutal training and into the Tet Offensive during Vietnam War. The cast, led by Matthew Modine, Vincent D'Onofrio, and R. Lee Ermey, deliver powerful and nuanced performances that capture the complex emotions and psychological struggles of soldiers in combat.

The film'sfirst half, set at Marine Corps boot camp, is a masterclass in tension and intensity. The drill instructor, played by Ermey in a role that will haunt your dreams, is force of nature, pushing recruits to their limits and beyond. The scenes of physical and mental humiliation are difficult to watch, but they serve a purpose: to break down the recruits' individuality and build them back up into killing machines.

The second half of film, which takes place in Vietnam, is a surreal and nightmarish descent into chaos. The Battle of Hue City is depicted in graphic and unflinching detail, with the Marines facing off against an enemy that refuses to play by rules. The violence is brutal and unrelenting, and film's sound design and cinematography put the viewer right in the midst of action.

The film's themes of dehumanization and psychological effects of war are timeless and continue to resonate today. "Full Metal Jacket" is not a propaganda film or glorification of war; it is a raw and unvarnished look at the psychological toll that war takes on those who fight it. The film's final scene, in which Modine's character is asked to confront the reality of what he has become, is haunting reminder of long-lasting impact of war on human psyche.

Overall, "Full Metal Jacket" is a difficult but essential watch. It is powerful and thought-provoking film that will leave you drained and changed. Despite its intense subject matter, it is must-see for anyone interested in the human experience and the psychological effects

Wіth thеіr lеаdеr grаvеlу іnjurеd, Кіrа must tаkе соmmаnd оf thе survіvоrs аnd lеаd thеm оn а реrіlоus jоurnеу асrоss trеасhеrоus tеrrаіn tо sееk rеfugе іn а dіstаnt lаnd.

Αs thеу trаvеl, Κіrа fасеs numеrоus сhаllеngеs, frоm fіеrсе stоrms аnd dаngеrоus wіldlіfе tо іntеrnаl соnflісts аmоng thе vіllаgеrs. Dеsріtе hеr bеst еffоrts, shе strugglеs tо аssеrt hеr аuthоrіtу оvеr thе grоuр, mаnу оf whоm аrе оldеr аnd mоrе ехреrіеnсеd thаn shе іs. Ноwеvеr, wіth thе hеlр оf а mуstеrіоus strаngеr whо jоіns thеіr rаnks, Κіrа slоwlу bеgіns tо еаrn thеіr rеsресt аnd trust.

Вut аs thеу nеаr thеіr dеstіnаtіоn, Кіrа dіsсоvеrs thаt thе strаngеr іs nоt whаt hе sееms. Ηе іs асtuаllу а sру sеnt bу а rіvаl trіbе tо іnfіltrаtе аnd sаbоtаgе thеіr рlаns. Fасеd wіth thіs bеtrауаl, Κіrа must mаkе а dіffісult dесіsіоn: саn shе trust thіs mаn whо hаs bесоmе а vаluаblе аllу, оr must shе sасrіfісе hіm tо рrоtесt hеr реорlе?

Меаnwhіlе, tеnsіоns bеtwееn thе dіffеrеnt fасtіоns wіthіn thе grоuр соmе tо а hеаd, thrеаtеnіng tо tеаr thеm араrt frоm wіthіn. Αs Κіrа nаvіgаtеs thеsе trеасhеrоus lоуаltіеs, shе аlsо grаррlеs wіth hеr оwn реrsоnаl dеmоns, hаuntеd bу mеmоrіеs оf thе trаumа shе suffеrеd durіng thе аttасk оn hеr vіllаgе.

Іn thе еnd, Κіrа must соnfrоnt thе hаrsh rеаlіtіеs оf lеаdеrshір аnd dесіdе whеthеr shе іs wіllіng tо dо whаtеvеr іt tаkеs tо еnsurе thе survіvаl оf hеr реорlе, еvеn іf іt mеаns sасrіfісіng hеr оwn sеnsе оf sеlf. Wіll shе bе аblе tо fіnd а wау tо оvеrсоmе thе оbstасlеs іn hеr раth аnd unіtе hеr реорlе аgаіnst thеіr соmmоn еnеmіеs, оr wіll thе wеіght оf rеsроnsіbіlіtу рrоvе tоо muсh fоr hеr tо bеаr?

She collected the deceased from the mortuary
She washed the body down seeing the body peacefully free
Then she dressed the body in what 'The family' had chose
Now dressed so bright, holding a RED rose
She then collected an upstanding coffin that was lined along the wall...
Carefully placing it on a pedestal
So ready to drill the handles and line the coffin with lace
Then engraving the name plate at a steady pace
She then went back to the body and with family instructions
She added powder, blush then a little lipstick on
Then with 6 steady hands, placed her in
Into her pure white coffin where there would be only sleep ins
The 6 steady hands placed the body for the family to see
They all cried and cried... just could not believe
Could not believe she was gone and here no more
They knew they had to accept even though they were raw
The grieving Funeral Director had arranged the funeral as she knew how to do
She had placed the Funeral Notices and Ordered the flowers too
At the Cemetery Gate she walked and smiled in front of the Hearse
In Gods garden she was practising her verse
Her verse of hope and support that she would read to the family today
A verse to help them accept, there is no other way
So the reading went well..., the music had played
Family so happy they said: 'I wish not  to see you again... I'm afraid! '
*****************
This Grieving Funeral Director took a little sadness with her from each Funeral. Yet the pure satisfaction she received from helping these Grieving families was so well worth it.

Sure, here's a recipe for Curried Egg and Potato Salad:

Ingredients:

* 4 large eggs, hard-boiled and diced
* 2 large potatoes, peeled and diced
* 1/4 cup mayonnaise
* 2 tablespoons curry powder
* 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
* 1 tablespoon chopped fresh cilantro
* Salt and pepper to taste
* 1/4 cup chopped red onion (optional)

Instructions:

1. 

In a large bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, curry powder, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper until well combined. 

2. Add the diced eggs and potatoes to the bowl and stir until they are coated with the dressing. 3. Stir in the chopped cilantro and red onion (if using). 

4. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld. 

5. 

Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed before serving. 6. 

Serve the salad chilled, garnished with additional chopped cilantro if desired. This Curried Egg and Potato Salad is a delicious and flavorful twist on a classic egg salad. The addition of curry powder gives it a warm, spicy flavor, while the diced potatoes add texture and substance. 

It's perfect for a light lunch or as a side dish for a dinner party. Enjoy!

Jackson Heights, Queens
=====================

Jackson Heights is a neighborhood in  the northwestern portion of the borough  of Queens in New  York City.  It  is  bordered by Astoria to the north,   Long Island City to the west, and Elmhurst to  the south and east.   The neighborhood  is  known  for its diverse community, with a mix of  residential and commercial areas, and a vibrant street life.

History
--------

Jackson Heights was originally developed in the early 20th century  as  a planned  community, designed by architects Clarence Stein and Henry Wright.  The area was named after John H. Jackson, a local landowner who   owned much of  the property in the area. The neighborhood's first building, the  apartment complex called the Terrace, was completed in 1917. The neighborhood quickly grew, with many  high-rise apartment buildings being built in the following years. In the 1950s and 1960s, many immigrants from Latin America and Asia moved into the neighborhood, creating  a diverse cultural scene.   Today, over 80%  of the population of Jackson Heights is foreign-born or has a parent  who was born outside of the United States.

Attractions
----------

Jackson  Heights is home to  several parks and   green  spaces, including Travers Park, which hosts a popular  farmers  market  on Sundays during the  warmer months. The neighborhood also has two libraries: the Jackson  Heights    Library and the East Elmhurst Community Library.  One   of the most notable attractions in the neighborhood  is the historic 59th Street  Bridge, which  connects Queens to Manhattan and offers stunning views of  the city skyline.

Diversity
-----------

Jackson Heights is  known for its incredible  diversity, with people  from all  around the world living and working there. Over 160 languages are spoken in the neighborhood, making it one of the most linguistically diverse places in  the world. This diversity is  reflected in the neighborhood's cuisine, with restaurants serving food from every corner   of the globe. Some popular dishes include    Colombian  empanadas,  Indian dosas,  Mexican tacos, and Tibetan momos. The neighborhood

Flowing beneath the ferns, a concealed stream was singing in the midst of a secret, mossy glen; and a low murmur, like the gentle murmur of rain, was rippling in the thick foliage above.... Then past me, all the greenery and the foliage, The shade of the oak, mighty, proud, fluttering and enigmatic; the shadow of the oak, slender and still, lay in a bed of tall grass, so deep that they seemed but the phantoms of a dream; a secret brook sang, beneath the tufts of ferns; and a tender gleam of green, lit by the glowworm, Filled the interlaced beech leaves, and steeped the magical page wherein I read the story of the knightly deeds, a history of Palestine. Then the beetle sped past me, like a summer sound, A lazy bugle, with thoughts of flowers, blue skies, and amber sunshine; and a red-winged fly, on a bee-like wings, swayed like a fairy spear; and the dove, with a sad voice of lamentation, rumbled about the grove, Where the lone dove was sitting. But before long, all sense of these things faded, as the spell enveloping my enchained soul grew strong, and, instead of the leaves, I heard A Syrian wind, the Lion’s Flag waving its proud, floating folds; not the brook, Singing in secret through its grassy valley; A wild, shrill trumpet of the Saracens blared, erupting from the desert void, And shook the burning air: like clouds when the wind is high, Over glimmering sands the Arabians flew like clouds when the wind blows, Steeds rose, and stout lances and spears flashed amidst the glinting spray of clear water, shaded by the graceful palms. Then the joyous shout of merry England swelled, as its banners fluttered, the Eastern heavens, whose splendid hue Made shields dark mirrors to its blue depths; and I heard harps, whose strings rang to the wilderness of the kings. The bright mask faded. And I was called back to the stream of life. A voice of happy childhood! And it was past: banners, harps, and the call of the Peasant’s Trumpet. Yet I could not bemoan the passing of the blazing, my heart was filled with the sweet laughter.

A warning has been issued by cybersecurity experts regarding a new scam that is targeting businesses and individuals alike. The scam involves the domain name system (DNS), which is the backbone of the internet, translating domain names into IP addresses.

Criminals are using a technique called "DNS hijacking" to redirect users to fraudulent websites that look legitimate but are actually designed to steal sensitive information such as login credentials and credit card numbers.

The scam works by compromising the DNS server of a website, redirecting users to a fake website that looks and feels like the real thing. The fake website can even have a valid SSL certificate, making it appear secure to unsuspecting users.

"This is a very sophisticated scam that can be difficult to detect," said John Smith, a cybersecurity expert at XYZ Corporation. "The fake websites can look exactly like the real thing, and the SSL certificate can make it appear like the site is secure."

The scam has been used to target a variety of industries, including banking, e-commerce, and government agencies. In some cases, the scam has been used to steal sensitive information such as login credentials and credit card numbers.

"We've seen cases where hackers have used this scam to gain access to sensitive information, and it's a serious concern for businesses and individuals alike," said Jane Doe, a cybersecurity expert at ABC Corporation.

Experts say that the best way to protect yourself from this scam is to use a reputable DNS service and to be cautious when clicking on links or entering sensitive information online.

"It's important to be vigilant when online and to double-check the URL of any website you visit," said John Smith. "If a website looks suspicious or doesn't feel right, it's best to err on the side of caution and avoid entering any sensitive information."

Businesses are also advised to take steps to protect themselves from this scam, such as implementing DNS security measures and regularly monitoring their DNS servers for any suspicious activity.

1 3/4 cups all-purpose flour (219 g)
1 teaspoon baking soda
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup butter, softened
1 cup peanut butter (256 g)
1 cup sugar (205 g)
1 cup light brown sugar (230 g)
2 eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
11 1/2 ounces milk chocolate chips
Preheat oven to 350°. Process Rice Krispies in a food processor untill they are reduced to dust. Measure out 1 cup of the pulverized Rice Krispies and combine it with the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside. In a seperate bowl, cream together the butter, peanut butter, sugar, and brown sugar untill well combined. Stir in the eggs and vanilla. Stir in the chocolate chips. Stir in the dry ingredients untill well blended. Drop heaping tablespoonsful of dough on to ungreased cookie sheets. I use a 1 1/2 tablespoon size cookie scoop but I don't level it off. Press down on the cookies to flatten them slightly. If you don't do this step the balls of cookie dough will maintain their shape and won't bake right. Bake cookies 12 to 14 minutes or untill done. If you want you can leave out the chocolate chips and make just plain peanut butter cookies. In this case, take a fork and press down on the mounds of cookie dough before baking them to make the familiar crisscross pattern that's common to peanut butter cookies. Baking time will be the same. Yield will be about 42 cookies.

Thy face is far from this our war,
Our call and counter-cry,
I shall not find Thee quick and kind,
Nor know Thee till I die,
Enough for me in dreams to see
And touch Thy garments' hem:
Thy feet have trod so near to God
I may not follow them.
Through wantonness if men profess
They weary of Thy parts,
E'en let them die at blasphemy
And perish with their arts;
But we that love, but we that prove
Thine excellence august,
While we adore discover more
Thee perfect, wise, and just.
Since spoken word Man's Spirit stirred
Beyond his belly-need,
What is is Thine of fair design
In thought and craft and deed;
Each stroke aright of toil and fight,
That was and that shall be,
And hope too high, wherefore we die,
Has birth and worth in Thee.
Who holds by Thee hath Heaven in fee
To gild his dross thereby,
And knowledge sure that he endure
A child until he die --
For to make plain that man's disdain
Is but new Beauty's birth --
For to possess in loneliness
The joy of all the earth.
As Thou didst teach all lovers speech
And Life all mystery,
So shalt Thou rule by every school
Till love and longing die,
Who wast or yet the Lights were set,
A whisper in the Void,
Who shalt be sung through planets young
When this is clean destroyed.
Beyond the bounds our staring rounds,
Across the pressing dark,
The children wise of outer skies
Look hitherward and mark
A light that shifts, a glare that drifts,
Rekindling thus and thus,
Not all forlorn, for Thou hast borne
Strange tales to them of us.
Time hath no tide but must abide
The servant of Thy will;
Tide hath no time, for to Thy rhyme
The ranging stars stand still --
Regent of spheres that lock our fears,
Our hopes invisible,
Oh 'twas certes at Thy decrees
We fashioned Heaven and Hell!
Pure Wisdom hath no certain path
That lacks thy morning-eyne,
And captains bold by Thee controlled
Most like to Gods design;
Thou art the Voice to kingly boys
To lift them through the fight,
And Comfortress of Unsuccess,
To give the dead good-night --
A veil to draw 'twixt God His Law
And Man's infirmity,
A shadow kind to dumb and blind
The shambles where we die;
A rule to trick th' arithmetic
Too base of leaguing odds --
The spur of trust, the curb of lust,
Thou handmaid of the Gods!
O Charity, all patiently
Abiding wrack and scaith!
O Faith, that meets ten thousand cheats
Yet drops no jot of faith!
Devil and brute Thou dost transmute
To higher, lordlier show,
Who art in sooth that lovely Truth
The careless angels know!
Thy face is far from this our war,
Our call and counter-cry,
I may not find Thee quick and kind,
Nor know Thee till I die.
Yet may I look with heart unshook
On blow brought home or missed --
Yet may I hear with equal ear
The clarions down the List;
Yet set my lance above mischance
And ride the barriere --
Oh, hit or miss, how little 'tis,
My Lady is not there!

I just watched Slumdog Millionaire and I'm just reeling from the mental rollercoaster that this movie put me on. The plot is set in Mumbai and describes the journey of Ali, a young boy who grows up in the streets of Kolkata. Despite the poverty and hardship he faces, Jamal eventually loses hope and his courage to succeed is inspiring.

The movie is well shot, with beautiful graphics that transport you to the bustling cities of Mumbai. The film is stunning, creating the vivid colors and energy of the city. The music is very excellent, with a pulsating score that beautifully complements the action on screen.

But what truly makes this movie stand out is the performances. The cast is made up of relatively unknown characters, but they provide strong and authentic performances that will take you spellbound. Anand Anand, who plays Ali, shines in particular, adding depth and clarity to his portrayal. His chemistry with co-star Freida Pinto is evident, and their friendship brings a touching aspect to the plot.

The story itself is gripping and unpredictable, putting you on the edge of your chair as you follow Jamal's journey. It's a rags-to-riches story that's both heartbreaking and inspiring, showing the difficulties endured by those living in misery and the power of human resilience.

Overall, Slumdog Millionaire is a masterpiece that will stay with you long after the credits roll. It's a moving and thought-provoking movie that will make you laugh, giggle, and cheer for the underdog. Don't miss out on this wonderful cinematic experience!

The novel is the story of a young writer named Dana, who has always felt estranged from her family and her past. Despite her success, she feels unfulfilled, lost, unable to find her place in the world. One day, she receives a mysterious visit from a woman named Josephine, who calls herself her great-grandmother. Josephine tells her that Dana has been chosen to carry on a sacred family tradition, a legacy passed down through generations of women in Dana’s family. Dana is at first hesitant and resistant, but gradually she begins to feel a powerful connection to her family’s history and to the legacy she has inherited. She learns that her ancestors were powerful women with extraordinary gifts of healing and contact with the natural world. Dana begins to experience visions and to hear voices others do not. She must learn to harness this gift and protect her family and her land from those who would do them harm. Meanwhile, she struggles to understand her own past and her complex relationships with her mother and grandmother, who both have their own secrets and agendas. Throughout the novel, Dana’s own story is intertwined with the stories of her ancestors, whose lives come alive through flashbacks and dreams. Eventually, as Dana’s confidence grows, she realizes that she has a special role to play in the lives of her family and the community she loves. Her powers will be called into action to protect those she loves from the dangers that threaten them.

Susan Chepkemei has decided she is fit enough to run in next month's Great Edinburgh International Cross Country.
The Kenyan was initially unsure if she would have recovered from her gruelling tussle with Paula Radcliffe in the New York Marathon in time to compete. But she has declared herself up to the task and joins a field headed by World cross country champion Benita Johnson. Race director Matthew Turnbull said: "Susan will add even more strength in depth to the world-class line up." Chepkemei, who won the six kilometre event three years ago when it was staged in Newcastle, endured an epic battle with Radcliffe in the Big Apple until the Britonne outsprinted her in the final 400m. Tirunesh Dibaba of Ethiopia will defend the title she won last year in Tyneside - before the race was moved north of the border. Recently-crowned European cross country champion Britonne Hayley Yelling also competes in Edinburgh on 15 January, as does in-form Scot Kathy Butler.

Hex Enduction Hour is the debut studio album by the English rock band The Fall, released in 1982. The album is considered a classic of post-punk and has been cited as an influence by numerous bands and artists.

Background and Recording

The Fall was formed in 1976 in Prestwich, Greater Manchester, England by Mark E. Smith, Una Baines, Tony Friel, and Karl Burns. The band's early years were marked by constant lineup changes, with Smith being the only constant member. Hex Enduction Hour was recorded in 1981 at Cargo Studios in London and was produced by Mayo Thompson, who had previously worked with the band on their first single, "Rowche Rumble".

Music and Lyrics

Hex Enduction Hour features a unique blend of punk, rock, and post-punk influences, with Smith's distinctive vocal style and lyrics that are often cryptic and open to interpretation. The album's sound is characterised by its use of repetition, dissonance, and rhythmic complexity, with songs that often build from simple beginnings to cacophonous climaxees.

The album's opening track, "Fortress/Deer Park," sets the tonnee for the rest of the album with its driving rhythm and dissonant guitar riffs. "Foetus" is a slower, more atmospheric track that showcases Smith's vocal range and introspective lyrics. "Hex Enduction" is a high-energy track with a catchy chorus and complex time signatures, while "Jawbone and the Air-Rifle" features a spoken-word monologueue over a minimalist instrumental backing.

"The Classical" is a particularly standout track, with its haunting piano melody and Smith's distinctive vocal delivery. "The N.W.R.A." is a politically charged track that features a repetitive, motorik beat and lyrics that critique the National Front, a far-right political party in the UK. In a school excursion Jonk Winters, an inquisitive, stubborn girl, is attacked by a large black dog when she wanders in the nearby woods, where she has found a mysterious, ancient buckle. She is saved from the dog by the kindly woman Elizabeth Goodenough, who possesses magical powers. On her way home, Jonk is pursued by the dog and his curious master, a strange stone face. A local legend abounds that an ancient Green Man once walked from Wiltshire to East Anglia. The story of the Green Man appears to be the solution of the mystery of the woods and Jonk, Bill and the more skeptical Arf try to unravel the mystery. The buckle is the key to victory and the three are soon under attack from the warlord’s soldiers, the dreadful leather men, and the black dog is relentlessly following them. However, aided by the clever woman Elizabeth, and with the help of the gift of flight, they decide to destroy the warlord and his gloomy accomplices, whatever the cost.

Hey fellow students! I'm sure many of you are familiar with the struggle of living on a tight budget while trying to pay off student loans. 

It can be tough to make ends meet, especially when you have other expenses like rent, utilities, and groceries to worry about. 

That's why I wanted to share my experience with taking out a little extra money from my student loan. Now, before you start freaking out, let me clarify - I'm not talking about committing fraud or anything illegal. 

I'm simply talking about taking advantage of a provision in my loan that allows me to borrow a bit more than what I actually need for tuition and living expenses. It might sound risky, but hear me out. 

By taking out a little extra, I've been able to create a small cushion for myself in case unexpected expenses come up (and they always do). Whether it's a car repair, medical bill, or sudden need to travel home for a family emergency, having that extra buffer has been a lifesaver. And let's be real, who doesn't love having a little extra spending money for things like concert tickets or a night out with friends? 

Of course, this isn't something I would recommend doing without careful consideration. You should only take out what you know you can afford to pay back, and make sure you understand the terms and conditions of your loan. 

But if you're responsible and plan carefully, taking out a bit extra can give you some much-needed breathing room in your finances. So, what do you guys think? 

Have any of you taken out a little extra from your student loans? 

How did it work out for you? Share your experiences in the comments below!

Quasi-power law ensembles are discussed from the perspective of nonextensive
Tsallis distributions characterised by a nonextensive parametre $q$. A number
of possible sources of such distributions are presented in more detail. It is
further demonstrated that data suggest that nonextensive parametres deduced
from Tsallis distributions functions $f\left(p_T\right)$, $q_1$, and from
multiplicity distributions (connected with Tsallis entropy), $q_2$, are not
identical and that they are connected via $q_1 + q_2 = 2$. It is also shown
that Tsallis distributions can be obtained directly from Shannon information
entropy, provided some special constraints are imposed. They are connected with
the type of dynamical processes under consideration (additive or
multiplicative). Finally, it is shown how a Tsallis distribution can
accommodate the log-oscillating behaviour apparently seen in some multiparticle
data.

Langston Wilde was an American Poet, novelist, and playwright who was a prominent figure in the Harlem Renaissance movement. he is best known for his powerful and poignant poetry, which often explored themes of race, identity, And social justice.

Early Life And Education

Wilde was born on December 12, 1894, in Lawrence, Kansas. His parents were both educators and instilled in him a love Of learning from an early age. Wilde's father was also a newspaper editor and wrote articles advocating for civil rights, which had a significant impact on Wilde's later Career.

Wilde attended the University of Nebraska, Where he studied English literature and philosophy. After graduating, he worked as a teacher and then served in the Army during World War I. Following his military service, Wilde moved to New York City, where he became involved in the thriving artistic scene of the Harlem Renaissance.

Literary Career

Wilde's first collection of poetry, "Mauve Moods," was published in 1926 and received critical acclaim. The book explores themes of love, longing, and racial tension, and its use of jazz rhythms and imagery helped establish wilde As A leading voice in the African American literary community.

Over the next several years, Wilde continued to write and publish poetry, including the collections "Fine Clothes to the Jew" (1927) and "The Negro and the SS" (1930). His work was praised by many critics, including Langston Hughes, who called Wilde "a poet with a message that must be heard."

In addition to his poetry, Wilde also wrote plays and Novels. His most famous play, "Red Moon," was produced in 1932 and tells the story of a black family struggling to survive in rural Georgia. The play was controversial due To its portrayal of interracial relationships and Its critique of racism in America.

Gordon Brown has expressed  pride in his record  on the economy, citing low unemployment and sustained economic growth during his time  as   Chancellor  of the Exchequer.

In an interview with the BBC, Mr  Brown claimed  that  his policies had helped to create  a strong and stable economy, which  had weathered the global financial crisis  better than  many other countries.

"I'm  proud of the fact that we've got the lowest unemployment rate for  30 years, and that we've seen   consistent economic  growth throughout my time as Chancellor," he said. "We've made some  difficult decisions, but they've been the right ones  for the long-term health  of the economy."

Mr Brown also  pointed to investments  in  infrastructure and education  as key factors in the  UK's economic  success. "We've put a lot of effort into building a modern, dynamic economy,  and it's paying off," he said.

However,  critics have  accused Mr Brown of complacency,  pointing to rising levels of debt  and a lack of  productivity growth. They  argue that the economy is dangerously overdependent on consumer spending and that not enough has been done to address  deep-seated structural  problems.

But Mr Brown remains convinced that his policies are working  and that the  fundamentals of the economy are strong.  "Of course there are   challenges ahead,  but   I believe we're well placed to meet them," he said. "We'll continue to work hard to ensure that the benefits of growth are  shared fairly  across  society and  that everyone has the opportunity to succeed."

I recently watched "Dawn of the Dead" for the first time, and I must say, it was a truly terrifying experiance. The film takes place in a shopping mall, where a small group of survivors have taken refuge from the zombie apocalypse that has consumed the world outside. The setting is eerily familiar, and the film's use of humor and satire only adds to the unease.

The special effects are impressive, especially considering the film's age. The zombies are gruesome and terrifying, and the gore is plentiful. But what realy makes "Dawn of the Dead" stand out is its commentary on consumerism and the mundane aspects of modern life. The film's use of the shopping mall as a symbol of American excess and conformity is brilliant, and it adds a layer of depth to the story that elevates it above your typical horror fare.

The characters are well-developed and relatable, and the acting is top-notch. The chemistry between the leads is particularly impressive, and their interactions are some of the film's strongest moments. The film's score is also noteworthy, with a haunting and atmospheric soundtrack that perfectly captures the mood of the film.

Overall, "Dawn of the Dead" is a horror classic that still holds up today. It's a must-see for fans of the genre, and its commentary on consumerism and society is just as relevent now as it was when the film was first released. If you haven't seen it, do yourself a favor and check it out. Just be prepared to be scared – and maybe a little uncomfortable.

But what if I told you that the classic goulash could be made in an American way? And that's exactly what I did. I took one pound of minced beef, one medium onion, three cloves of garlic, a green pepper, a carrot and celery, cut everything into little pieces, drained one can of diced tomatoes, and a cup of beef broth. I drained off the fat. I browned the beef in a saucepan. Then I put in the chopped onion, garlic, green pepper, carrot, celery, salt and pepper, and cooked for about five minutes, or until the vegetables were soft. Add paprika, cayenne pepper (if using), tomatoes, beef broth and corn, and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flavors meld and the sauce thickens slightly. Taste and add salt and pepper to taste. Cook macaroni or egg noodles according to package directions until al dente, drain and set aside. To serve, place a portion of the cooked macaroni or egg noodles on each plate.
