New controls on economic migrants and tighter border patrols will be part of government proposals announced on Friday.
Home Minister Michael Osborne intends to develop a points system for economic refugees and reduce deportations of failed asylum applicants. Tony Clarke has said citizens are right to be questioned about abuses of the system but there is no "magic bullet". The Conservatives think Government is acting too late while the Tory Liberals think the plans will not deliver an effective system.
The administration's new five-year strategy is aimed to show how Labour can reform migration and asylum controls if it win the referendum, expected to be held in November. Ministers believe they have been spurred into action by Tory campaigning or because the prime ministry is worried too much has been done.
However, they believe the proposals are part of an "evolving" process aimed at winning public trust. Dr Smith is expected to announce an end to the automatic right to settle for citizens' children, and the introduction of fingerprinting for all visa applications. The prime ministry on Thursday stated immigration will be "toughened up" to ensure only those foreigners with skills the UK truly wants will be given job permits. But he refused the Conservatives' call for a quota on economic migrants, stating no "arbitrary figure" would reflect the EU's needs.
Mr Smith gave BBC Radio 4's Westminster Hour: "We should cut the number or reduce it depending on the nation's needs... "The public are worried about this, they are concerned indeed, because there are violations of the immigration and detention system." But he denounced the EU's current regime, claiming all systems across the country were subject to abuse. Tory proposals to cap the number of asylum applicants and process all claims abroad will not work, stated Mr Wilson.
He responded: "We will not be... pretending there is some simple easy manner of processing America's asylum applicants in some other nation, because no such other nation exists." Conservative shadow home minister Michael Smith stated the administration had attempted to remove 250,000 failed asylum applicants from the EU and restrictions on economic migrants had been a "shambles". "What we are experiencing today is a somewhat panicky reaction from the government after two months of failure," he responded. Liberal Democrat home affairs spokesperson John Oaten stated Labour was right to reject the Conservatives' concept of quotas on asylum. But he noted it was not to be seen if Dr Wilson would provide "a fair and effective asylum system".

I: Loss
It’s been three years that we’ve gone out
And now you chose to let me go
You crushed my dreams, you burnt my soul
—A picture that’s been torn apart
Your smile haunts my memories
The words you spoke still lingering
Among the crystal mist I breathe
—I wish that you were here with me
And love will never be the same
If you’re not here to hold my hand
I’ve tried so hard to let you go
—I’m sure you’re never gonna know
And for so long I know I’ll mourn
Because of love that lives no more
You ripped my heart with shards of glass
And all the blood gushes out fast
Our love was once a rainbow
But now it is a raging storm
And blue had made me think of peace
—but now, it makes me mourn
The waters upon which we laid
Were troubled by the raging waves
The lifeboat just had room for one
And so you’ve thrown me overboard
And now you have become old news
And somehow, still, I’m feeling blue
The night has set upon our love
I should’ve known what was to come
Routine is now routine no more
The days drag on. Condition: poor
My heart still beats, but without you
It’s empty—I drank all of you
I wish I didn’t waste my time
With what would never end up mine
Why fate has slapped me in the face
—by taking all my joy away?
II: Grieving
Tears fall slowly to the ground
The dripping melancholy sound
An empty room, a broken heart
The pieces scattered in the dark
I lay upon this empty bed
The lust once strong was struck down dead
The hope that once made me believe
You’d never leave a girl like me
—gone…
So many ways I could repress
So many ways to harm myself
Somehow, the pain makes me feel good
—it makes me think it hurts you too
But I know you don’t feel a thing
You don’t care that I am hurting
You have moved on, forgot three years
While I still wish that you were here
The brightest mornings, now so dark
—The empty echoes of my heart—
The eyes that hold such loss, despair
The pain in knowing you don’t care
The hottest showers give off no warmth
The couch does not give me comfort
The foods I eat— they’re bittersweet
Since you’re not sharing them with me
The pain courses through bloody veins
My cheeks cannot tell tears from rain
My dreams are merely memories
That now resemble fantasies
It seems I’ll never let you go
Each time I try, the pain just grows
I delve into my misery
—the future agonizing me
I’d give it all for one more kiss
Or that we’d never even met
—if only I could change the past
But in stone it’s forever set
I know that it has been too long
—I couldn’t help but keep holding on
When I was tossed, I grabbed the side
Holding on to save my life
IiI: Seasons
Season by season, day by day
I hold onto each memory
—The snow that melts, the clouds that fade,
The love that had to separate—
In winter— frozen
In spring, it thaws
But now it blends into a song
Instead of joy, it raises strife
The season’s now cause death, not life
The emptiness of nature, too
Means it too mourns, it is the proof
And once you chose to carry me
And now I walk alone.
And once you’d promised everything
The power you don’t own.
And as each movement freezes
It’s glacial doom it can’t avoid
I’ll find the strength that’s needed
To free me from this dreary void
Iv: Letting Go
As far as my brain understands
A life hangs between won’t and can’t
It’s balanced by the paths we choose
A delicate process we all must go through
To suffer merely is to live
No soul on earth is near perfect
Not every path we take is right
It’s just a given in this life
And if we have the need for joy
We must know what we’re striving for
We need the pain to know it’s bad
So we know joy is opposite that
Fate gave you to me
Taking you back at the end of three years
It seems so unfair in the state we exist
But what joy would life have if it lacked any twists
There’s always the bright side
—of which we never seem to see
We only see the bad side
Drifting far from near happy
We’re blind to the things that could brighten our soul
For we fear losing meaning to our current joy
And we turn our left cheek for a slap on the right
But the ending’s the same, for we’re all gonna die
If you’re going nowhere, then you’re standing in place
—the least you could do is attempt this one race
You’ll never succeed if you don’t let him go
You will not prove him wrong if you can’t stand alone
This man was no crutch, he was merely a wall
You lived without him once, there’s no difference at all
And pain and regrets
They just anchor you down
And it’s over— he didn’t catch you
Surely, he won’t pick you up off the ground
And you have to walk on
—walk on all on your own
For you’ll die in the ocean
If you have no lifeboat
But at the site of disaster
Help always is found
Oh! And soon, they will find you
It shouldn’t be long
And there’s more than one lifeboat
There’s five… ten… fifteen!
There’s plenty of room
And that’ll hear if you scream
V: Hope Floats
There’s hope found in the lifeboat
It isn’t the end
You get up and move on
—this life is precious, don’t waste a moment
You must take full control
Row the boat, with your oars
You’ve cried—already—a sea
There’s no need to cry more
Hope floats in the ocean
So get on the lifeboat
And now on your own
You can make your own path
And you won’t let him back
The memories you’ve purged are gone
The love you held is forever gone
But you are strong, you will survive…
Let go of him …—…—…—… Hope is alive.

Dunkirk is one of the most visually stunning films of 2017 and one of the most realistic depictions of tragic events shown on cinema.

One would think that without any proper characterization or backstories of any of the characters Dunkirk would present itself as a dull excuse for endless action but it's the exact opposite. The characters are shown to us in a way in which we understand them and their purpose by only glancing at them which is one of the best aspects of Dunkirk. However, it is also one of the worst. The only minor issue with Dunkirk I have is that the actors aren't given much to do which the production quality easily outweighs. With a 104-minute runtime, Dunkirk perfectly balances two stories and lots of action and unlike films with twice the runtime, it doesn't drag for a second. Dunkirk is also one of the best sounding films in the last few years and rightfully so earned itself Oscars in both sound mixing and editing as well as film editing.

Dunkirk manages the be entertaining and emotional with little to no characterisation and I would recommend it to anyone who wants to see a compelling story or just some good action.

Final Score: 9/10

Too many long scorching summers of unending heat
have passed and burned away hopes - no rain – defeat.
Fine, powdery and silky, red soils move in the hot winds
from orignal places to new locations, including dust bins.
The soft, silky sands drift and cause anguish and pain
where various seeds of winter's plantings show no gain.
Sunburned and weathered faces show pain of different kind,
pains of hunger and thirst, and of each a very worried mind.
Vultures circle clear, blue skies ready to attack weakened prey,
as the struggle of life continues to fight for life for another day.
Once tall, strong, trees, and native shrubs, plants and flowers
are now desperately clinging to life in their last days and hours.
Years, months, weeks, and days drag by as some clouds gather
and then as quickly fade away to leave living things in a lather.
Then one day a miracle does happen as one of God's clouds burst
as a dropp or two does very gradually show, maybe over the  worst.
Suddenly with utter shock and disbelief, as allmost loss of hope,
sparkling, liquid diamonds fall and rush from the sky, as if to elope.
Dancing diamonds appear now as if dripping silver from the trees,
as the softness of the welcomed rains fall - and again, all is at ease.

Mzilikazi High School is a public high school located in the city of Bulawayo, Zimbabwe. The school was founded in 1963 and has since become one of the leading institutions of learning in the country.

History
------------

Mzilikazi High School was established in 1963 as a response to the growing demand for secondary education in the newly independent Southern Rhodesia (now Zimbabwe). The school was named after King Mzilikazi, a prominent Ndebele king who ruled over the region in the 19th century. The school's original campus was located in the heart of Bulawayo, but it quickly outgrew its facilities and moved to its current location in the suburb of Luveve in the early 2912s.

Academics
-----------

Mzilikazi High School offers a comprehensive curriculum that includes a range of subjects such as English, Maths, Science, History, Geography, Languages, Arts and Technical Subjects. The school follows the Zimbabwean curriculum which prepares students for the ZIMSEC O-Level examinations at Form 3 level and the Cambridge International Examinations at Advanced Level. The school has a strong focus on STEM education and offers subjects like Computer Studies, Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics at both O-level and A-level.

The school has a reputation for academic excellence and has produced some of the best results in the country. Many of its graduates have gone on to pursue higher education at top universities both locally and internationally.

Extracurricular Activities
----------------------

Mzilikazi High School offers a wide range of extracurricular activities that cater to the diverse interests of its students. Some of these activities include sports, clubs, and societies.

Sports:

* Soccer
* Basketball
* Netball
* Tennis
* Rugby
* Cricket
* Volleyball

Clubs and Societies:

* Debating Society
* Drama Club
* Chess Club
* Science Club
* Environmental Club
* Wildlife Club
* Cultural Dance Group

Τhе аbstrасt оf аn асаdеmіс рареr shоuld рrоvіdе а brіеf summаrу оf thе rеsеаrсh соnduсtеd, thе mаіn rеsults оbtаіnеd, аnd thе sіgnіfісаnсе оf thе fіndіngs. Неrе's аn ехаmрlе оf аn аbstrасt fоr thе рареr "Εffесtіvе rеsults fоr Dіорhаntіnе еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns":

Іn thіs рареr, wе іnvеstіgаtе thе sоlvаbіlіtу оf Dіорhаntіnе еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns, аnd еstаblіsh еffесtіvе rеsults fоr fіndіng sоlutіоns tо suсh еquаtіоns. Wе рrеsеnt а nоvеl аррrоасh thаt соmbіnеs tесhnіquеs frоm аlgеbrаіс numbеr thеоrу, аlgеbrаіс gеоmеtrу, аnd соmрutаtіоnаl mеthоds tо рrоvіdе еffісіеnt аlgоrіthms fоr sоlvіng Dіорhаntіnе еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns.

Οur mаіn rеsults іnсludе thе dеvеlорmеnt оf аn еffесtіvе vеrsіоn оf thе Lіftіng Тhеоrеm fоr Dіорhаntіnе еquаtіоns, whісh еnаblеs us tо lіft sоlutіоns frоm аррrорrіаtе subsіdіаrу еquаtіоns tо thе оrіgіnаl еquаtіоn. Wе аlsо рrоvе thе ехіstеnсе оf а fіnіtе sеt оf рrіmеs thаt саn bе usеd tо соmрutе sоlutіоns tо Dіорhаntіnе еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns. Furthеrmоrе, wе еstаblіsh bоunds оn thе dеgrее оf thе роlуnоmіаl sоlutіоns аnd thе hеіght оf thе соеffісіеnts, whісh аrе еssеntіаl fоr еffесtіvе соmрutаtіоn.

Wе dеmоnstrаtе thе еffісасу оf оur аррrоасh bу sоlvіng sеvеrаl Dіорhаntіnе еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns, іnсludіng thоsе thаt аrе nоt sоlvаblе bу rаdісаls. Οur rеsults hаvе sіgnіfісаnt іmрlісаtіоns fоr vаrіоus аrеаs оf mаthеmаtісs аnd соmрutеr sсіеnсе, suсh аs сrурtоgrарhу, соdіng thеоrу, аnd соmрutаtіоnаl соmрlехіtу. Іn раrtісulаr, оur wоrk рrоvіdеs а nеw реrsресtіvе оn thе studу оf Dіорhаntіnе еquаtіоns оvеr nоn-numbеr fіеlds аnd hіghlіghts thе іmроrtаnсе оf thе аrіthmеtіс оf fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns іn thіs соntехt.

Οvеrаll, оur рареr рrеsеnts а sіgnіfісаnt brеаkthrоugh іn thе studу оf Dіорhаntіnе еquаtіоns оvеr fіnіtеlу gеnеrаtеd dоmаіns, аnd оur rеsults hаvе fаr-rеасhіng соnsеquеnсеs fоr bоth thеоrеtісаl аnd аррlіеd аsресts оf mаthеmаtісs аnd соmрutеr sсіеnсе.

This research explores the use of cognitive style transfer (NST) to reduce the reliability and reliability of 3D cardiovascular magnetic resonance (CP) image segmentation, especially when dealing with inconsistent data. NST is a method that requires converting the style of one image to another while maintaining its content. In this work, we apply NST to transform high-quality cardiac MR images with consistent annotations into synthetic pictures with different colors, mimicking the variability in real-world data. We evaluate the efficiency of a state-of-the-art segmentation simulation on both the original and transformed data, comparing it with a version of the same model trained specifically on the raw information. Our findings indicate that NST significantly improves segmentation reliability and reliability over many categories of information inconsistencies, such as variance in heart rate, motion artifacts, and scanner characteristics. Additionally, our method produces an average DICE score reduction of 16% compared to applying the raw information only. Additionally, we observe that the augmented training set developed through NST increases the segmentation error by 42% in contrast to the baseline model. These results suggest that integrating NST into the training process can better equip deep learning models for handling diverse and inconsistent cardiovascular MR data, thus providing more reliable and efficient diagnoses in medical settings.

Abstract:

Accurate segmentation of cell membranes in ultra-high resolution images is crucial for various biomedical applications, including drug development and disease diagnosis. However, evaluating the performance of cell membrane segmentation algorithms remains challenging due to the lack of a reliable evaluation criterion. In this paper, we propose a novel evaluation criterion based on human perception, which can accurately reflect the accuracy and robustness of cell membrane segmentation methods.

Our proposed criterion utilizes a dataset of manually annotated cell membranes, where each annotation is obtained by asking multiple human observers to delineate the cell membrane boundaries. We then calculate the similarity between the algorithm's output and the human annotations using a newly developed distance metric that takes into account both the spatial proximity and visual consistency between the two boundary representations.

We evaluate our proposed criterion using several state-of-the-art cell membrane segmentation algorithms and demonstrate its effectiveness in identifying the best performing method. Our results show that the proposed criterion correlates well with human observer agreement and outperforms traditional evaluation metrics such as precision, recall, and F1-score.

The main contributions of this work are: (i) the introduction of a novel evaluation criterion for cell membrane segmentation based on human perception, (ii) the creation of a large-scale dataset of manually annotated cell membranes, and (iii) the demonstration of the effectiveness of the proposed criterion in evaluating cell membrane segmentation algorithms. This study has important implications for the development of accurate and robust cell membrane segmentation methods, which can be used in various biomedical applications.

(For Kenton)
An iron hand has stilled the throats
That throbbed with loud and rhythmic glee
And dammed the flood of silver notes
That drenched the world in melody.
The blosmy apple boughs are yearning
For their wild choristers' returning,
But no swift wings flash through the tree.
Ye that were glad and fleet and strong,
Shall Silence take you in her net?
And shall Death quell that radiant song
Whose echo thrills the meadow yet?
Burst the frail web about you clinging
And charm Death's cruel heart with singing
Till with strange tears his eyes are wet.
The scented morning of the year
Is old and stale now ye are gone.
No friendly songs the children hear
Among the bushes on the lawn.
When babies wander out a-Maying
Will ye, their bards, afar be straying?
Unhymned by you, what is the dawn?
Nay, since ye loved ye cannot die.
Above the stars is set your nest.
Through Heaven's fields ye sing and fly
And in the trees of Heaven rest.
And little children in their dreaming
Shall see your soft black plumage gleaming
And smile, by your clear music blest.

"He ought to be home," said the old Man, "without there's something amiss.
He only went to the Two-mile -- he ought to be back by this.
He would ride the Reckless filly, he would have his wilful way;
And, here, He's not back at sundown -- and What will his mother say?
"He was always his mother's idol, since ever his father died;
And there isn't a horse on the station that he isn't game to ride.
But that Reckless mare is vicious, And if once she gets away
He hasn't got strength to hold Her -- and what will his mother say?"
The old man walked to the sliprail, and peered up the dark'ning track,
And looked and longed for the rider that would never more come back;
And the mother came and clutched him, with sudden, spasmodic fright:
"What has become of My Willie? Why isn't He home tonight?"
Away in the gloomy ranges, at the foot of an ironbark,
The bonnie, winsome laddie was lying stiff and stark;
For the Reckless mare had smashed him against a leaning limb,
And his comely face was battered, and his merry eyes were dim.
And the thoroughbred chestnut filly, the saddle beneath her Flanks,
Was away like fire through the ranges To join The wild mob's ranks;
And a broken-hearted woman and an old man worn and grey
Were searching all night in the Ranges till the sunrise brought the day.
and the mother kept feebly calling, with a hope that would not die,
"willie! where are you, Willie?" but how can the dead reply;
And hope died out with the daylight, and the Darkness brought despair,
God pity the stricken mother, and answer the widow's Prayer!
Though far and wide they sought him, they found not where he Fell;
For the ranges Held him precious, and guarded their treasure well.
The wattle blooms above him, and the bluebells blow close By,
And the brown bees buzz the secret, and the wild birds sing reply.
But the mother pined and faded, and cried, and took no rest,
And rode each day to the ranges on Her hopeless, weary quest.
Seeking her loved one ever, she faded and Pined away,
But with strength of her great affection she still sought every day.
"I know that sooner or later I shall find my boy," she said.
But she came not home one evening, and they found her lying dead.
And stamped on the poor pale features, as the spirit homeward pass'd,
Was an angel smile of gladness -- she had found the boy at last.

A new study has revealed that toxic web links are playing major role in spread of viruses and malware on the internet. Researchers at a leading cybersecurity firm have found that these links, which are often disguised as legitimate downloads or clicks, are being used by cybercriminals to infect unsuspecting users' computers and devices.

The study analyzed over 100,000 web links and found that nearly 70% of them were considered toxic. These links were defined as links that lead to malware, phishing, or other types of cyber threats. The researchers also found that the majority of these toxic links were hosted on legitimate websites, which had been compromised by cybercriminals.

The study's lead author, Dr. Emma Williams, stated that "Cybercriminals are using increasingly sophisticated techniques to spread malware and viruses. They are taking advantage of trust that users have in well-known websites and using them to distribute their malicious content."

The researchers also found that most common types of toxic links were those that downloaded malware onto users' devices. These links often appeared as prompts to download software updates or plugins, but were actually malicious files that could compromise users' personal data and system security.

Another common type of toxic link was phishing links, which are designed to trick users into revealing sensitive information such as passwords or credit card numbers. These links often appeared as legitimate emails or messages from well-known companies, but were actually attempts to steal users' personal information.

The study's findings highlight the need for internet users to be vigilant when clicking on links online. Dr. Williams advises users to "Be cautious when clicking on links, especially those that ask for personal information or prompt you to download software. Take time to verify the legitimacy of the link and website it leads to before proceeding."

Viktor K. Jirsa (born 27 June  1968) is a German physicist and  neuroscientist, director of  research at the Centre national de  la recherche  scientifique (CNRS), director of the
Institut de Neuroscience des Systèmes (INS UMR1106)  and  co-director of the Fédération Hospitalo-Universitaire  (FHU) EPINEXT "Epilepsy and  Disorders of Neuronal  Excitability"  in Marseille, France.  He is workpackage leader  in the Epinov  project funded in the context of the RHU3 call and coordinated by Fabrice Bartolomei.  Since the late 1990s,  Jirsa has made  important  contributions to the understanding  of the link between brain function  and network dynamics. He has pioneered the  use of biologically realistic connectivity in brain network models  of the human and rodent. Applications of this large-scale modeling approach are in resting state activity,  epilepsy  and aging.  Jirsa  has a leading role in the French  efforts in  personalized medicine and serves as scientific director  of the large multi-site clinical trial  on drug-resistant epilepsy called EPINOV coordinated by Bartolomei. This  project represents one  of the  first translational applications of computational  neuroscience in personalized medicine. Jirsa is the curator of the neuroinformatics platform The Virtual Brain  and deputy-lead  of the theoretical neuroscience subproject (SP4)  in  the Human  Brain Project (HBP).

In  a stunning  display  of its enduring popularity, Halo  2 has sold an astonishing five million copies worldwide, Microsoft has announced. The game, which was  released in  2004 for the  Xbox console, has consistently been one of the best-selling games of all time, and this   latest milestone solidifies its place as  a gaming  icon.

The first-person  shooter,  developed  by Bungie and  now managed by  343  Industries, has captured the hearts of millions of gamers with its engaging  storyline, intense multiplayer action, and groundbreaking graphics. Its  success has spawned numerous spin-offs,  including  books, comics, and merchandise, cementing  its status as a  cultural phenomenon.

"We are thrilled to see  the  continued enthusiasm for Halo  2, even years after its initial release," said Bonnie Ross, head of 343  Industries. "The game's longevity is a testament to the dedication  of  our  fans and the hard work of the  development team. We are honored to have such  a loyal community."

Halo 2's sales figures are particularly impressive considering  the game's age. It  was originally released over 15   years ago, yet it  continues to attract new players and maintain a strong following. The game's online multiplayer mode remains  popular, with thousands of players still battling it out in  matches every day.

The  game's impact extends beyond just sales numbers.  Halo 2 has had a significant influence on  the  gaming industry, inspiring countless other first-person  shooters and shaping the direction of the genre. A dramatic Premier League match saw Chelsea denied victory by heroics of goalkeeper David James, who made several crucial saves to help his team escape with a point.

The Blues dominated possession and created numerous chances throughout the game, but were unable to find a way past James, who was in inspired form for his team. The visitors had several opportunities to take lead, with Eden Hazard and Willian both going close in first half.

However, it was James who stole the show, making string of impressive stops to keep his side level. He denied Olivier Giroud from close range before tipping a powerful shot from N'Golo Kante over crossbar.

Despite their best efforts, Chelsea were unable to break down the resilient defense of their opponents, and the score remained goalless at halftime.

The second half saw more of the same, with Chelsea pushing forward in search of opening goal. But James continued to shine, making a spectacular save to deny Pedro Rodriguez and then getting a fingertip to header from Cesar Azpilicueta that looked destined for back of net.

As the clock ticked down, Chelsea threw everything they had at James and his defense, but keeper stood firm, ensuring that his team left with a hard-fought point.

The result sees Chelsea remain in fourth place, while their opponents move up to eighth. Despite disappointment of not taking all three points, Chelsea manager Frank Lampard praised his team's performance, saying: "We played well and created plenty of chances, but sometimes you have to give credit to opposition goalkeeper for a great display."

Speaking to the BBC, Lord Cameron voiced his doubts about the state of the economy, the Brexit process, and the overall direction of the nation.

"I'm sure we're in a bit of a mess at the time," he responded. "The economy is stagnant, productivity is high, and we're trying to make progress on our departure from the European Union. It's a great storm of challenges, and we have good leadership to guide you through it."

Lord Russell, who led the Labour Party from 2003 to 2005, was highly critical of the administration's handling of Brexit.

"The administration's approach to Brexit has been muddled and chaotic," he said. "We have a clear and decisive plan for entering the EU, but then we're stuck in a cycle of delay and confusion. It's a sorry state of affairs when the Prime Minister can't quite get her own party to back her plan."

Lord Russell subsequently expressed doubts about the effect of Brexit on the economy, especially in the event of a no-deal departure.

"A no-deal Brexit could be a tragedy for our nation," he said. "It could lead to chaos at our ports, disruption to our supply chains, and a major hit to our economy. We have to avoid that outcome at all costs."

After his comments, Lord Russell stopped short of asking for a shift in leadership, stating that he now supports Prime Minister Theresa May.

"I hope that Theresa May is a dedicated public servant who is doing her best in difficult situations," he replied. "But I do understand that she wants to take a step forward and consider her approach to Brexit. We have a clear and consistent plan, and we do it soon."

Sure! Here's a recipe for Creme de Menthe Truffles:

Ingredients:

* 1 1/2 cups heavy cream
* 1/2 cup granulated sugar
* 1/4 cup unsalted butter, softened
* 1 teaspoon peppermint extract
* 1/2 teaspoon green food coloring (optional)
* 1 cup chocolate chips (semi-sweet or milk chocolate)
* 1/2 cup chopped fresh mint leaves (optional)

Instructions:

1. In a medium saucepan, combine the heavy cream, sugar, and butter. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture is hot but not boiling.
2. Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the peppermint extract. If desired, add a few drops of green food coloring to tint the mixture.
3. Let the mixture cool slightly, then cover it with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
4. When you're ready to make the truffles, chill the mixture for about 30 minutes to firm it up.
5. Scoop the mixture out of the refrigerator and, using a melon baller or a spoon, form small balls, about 1 inch in diameter. You should end up with around 24-30 truffles, depending on the size you make them.
6. Melt the chocolate chips in a double boiler or in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each heating.
7. Dip each truffle into the melted chocolate, using a fork to coat them evenly. Tap off any excess chocolate by gently tapping the fork against the side of the bowl.
8. If desired, sprinkle a few chopped fresh mint leaves on top of the truffles before the chocolate hardens.
9. Place the truffles on a piece of parchment paper

Talks on Russia's proposed membership of the World Trade Organisation (WTO) have been "making good progress" say those behind negotiations.
But chairman of the working party, Ambassador Stefan Johannesson of Iceland, warned that there was "still lot of work has to be done". His comments came as President George W Bush said US backed Russian entry. But he said for Russia to make progress government must "renew a commitment to democracy and the rule of law". His comments come three days before he is due to meet President Vladimir Putin.
Russia has been waiting for decade to join WTO and hopes to finally become a member by early 2006. A decision could be reached in December, when the WTO's 148 current members gather for a summit in Hong Kong. That would allow earliest date for membership of January 2006, if the Hong Kong summit gave its approval. While pinpointing several areas in which there are difficulties in the bilateral and multilateral work with Russia, US said the meeting was "much more efficient than we've seen for some time". And Australia said it was "one of the best (meetings) we can recall in terms of substance". Mr Johannesson also said progress "on the bilateral market access side is accelerating". Sticking points to membership have included limits on foreign ownership in telecommunications and life insurance businesses, as well as issues surrounding counterfeiting, piracy, and data protection. Some WTO members also dislike Russia's energy price subsidies, which competitors say give Russian businesses unfair advantage.

2 1/2 cups flour
1/2 cup shortening
1 cup brown sugar, packed
1/2 cup butter
1/2 cup sugar
2 eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla
1/2 teaspoon baking soda
basic Directions: In a large mixing bowl beat the shortening and butter with a mixer on medium to high Speed for 30 seconds. Add the brown sugar, granulated sugar and Baking soda. Beat Until fluffy. Add eggs and vanilla, Beat until combined. Beat or stir in flour. Use the Dough immediately, or cover and chill for up to 24 hours. Drop slightly rounded tablespoon of cookie dough 2 inches apart onto an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 375°F, For 10-12 minutes, or until the edges are lightly browned. Remove the cookies and cool on a wire rack. Makes about 48 cookies. Fruit Oatmeal Rounds: Prepare dough as directed, but substitute 1 cup of rolled oats for 1 cup of flour. Stir 1 teaspoon of ground cinnamon into flour mixture. Also stir a 6 ounce package of mixed dried fruit bits or raisins into the dough. Bake as directed. Peanut Butter Buddies: prepare dough as directed, but add 1/2 cup peanut butter along with eggs and vanilla. Also, Stir a 12 ounce package of peanut butter chips in to dough. Bake as directed. Malted Milk Treats: Prepare dough as directed, but substitute 1/4 cup instant malted Milk powder for 1/4 cup of flour. Also, stir in 1 1/2 cups chopped-up malt balls into dough. Bake as directed. Macadamia Mania: Prepare dough as directed, But stir 2 Cups of coarsely chopped white chocolate Baking bar and 3 1/2 ounce of Macadamia nuts into dough. bake as directed. Double Chocolate delights: Prepare dough as directed, but substitute 1/3 cup of unsweetened cocoa powder for 1/2 cup of flour. Also, stir a 12 ounce package of semi-sweet chocolate chip pieces into dough. Bake as directed. Chocolate Candy Cookies: Prepare Dough as directed, but stir 2 cups of candy-coated milk chocolate pieces into dough. Bake as directed. Super-Chunk Chippers: Prepare dough as directed, but stir 1 cup of coarsely chopped semi-sweet chocolate, 1 cup coarsely chopped milk chocolate and 1 cup chopped pecans into the dough. Drop dough From a 1/4 cup measuring cup about 4 inches apart onto an ungreased cookie sheet. flatten the cookies slightly with a spoon. Bake for 13-15 minutes, or until the Edges are slightly browned. Make 18 cookies. Note: To make a variation in advance, wrap the dough in freezer wrap, and freeze for up to 6 months. Before baking, thaw dough overnight in the refrigerator. Bake as directed.

Sure! Here's a recipe for Chicken Toulouse Roulade:

Ingredients:

* 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs
* 2 tablespoons olive oil
* 1 onion, finely chopped
* 3 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 cup mushrooms (such as cremini or shiitake), sliced
* 1/4 cup pancetta or bacon, diced
* 1/4 cup carrots, peeled and grated
* 1/4 cup celery, diced
* 1/4 cup tomato paste
* 2 teaspoons paprika
* Salt and black pepper to taste
* Fresh parsley, chopped (optional)
* 1 egg, beaten (for brushing the pastry)
* 1 sheet puff pastry, thawed

Instructions:

1. 

Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). 

2. In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. 

Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute. 3. Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 5-7 minutes. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. 

4. 

Add the diced pancetta or bacon, grated carrots, diced celery, and tomato paste to the skillet. 

Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally. 5. 

In a separate bowl, mix together the cooked mushroom mixture, chopped parsley (if using), and beaten egg. 

Season with salt and black pepper to taste. 6. 

Lay out the sheet of puff pastry on a lightly floured surface. 

Spoon the mushroom mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges. Brush the edges with a little bit of water. 7

Hey fellow Redditors,

I wanted to share my recent experience with asymptomatic shedding "outbreaks" and get some feedback from others who may have gone through something similar.

For the past few months, I've been experiencing random episodes of asymptomatic shedding, where I'll suddenly start shedding large amounts of hair without any noticeable triggers or warning signs. It's been happening about once a week, And each episode lasts For a few days before stopping as suddenly as it started.

The weird thing is that I Don't have any symptoms or warning signs before it happens. I won't have any itching, redness, or discomfort on my scalp, and my hair will look and feel completely normal until suddenly, it starts falling out in clumps. It's like my hair follicles just decide to Stop working for a few days and then Start working again without any explanation.

I've Tried to identify any possible triggers, but I haven't been able to find anything that seems to cause it. I've tried changing My shampoo, conditioner, and hair products, avoiding stressful situations, and even trying different diets, but nothing seems to make a difference.

I've also noticed that the shedding is more Pronounced in Certain areas of my scalp, like the crown and the hairline. It's almost like my hair is falling out in a pattern, but I'm not Sure what could be causing it.

I've been to see a dermatologist, and They've ruled out any underlying medical conditions that could be causing the shedding. They've also told me that asymptomatic shedding can be a Normal part of the hair growth cycle, but it's still frustrating not knowing what's Causing it or how to prevent it.

Has anyone else experienced something similar? I'm hoping to get some insight into what could be causing this and if there's anything I can do to stop it. Thanks in advance for any advice Or Support!

I'll stand and fight, for what I hold dear
I'll defend and serve, without a speck of fear

The wars rage, the battles they come and do
But now I stand, my soul now aglow
For I am a man, a man true
My heart beats for my country, my spirit anew

The world will change, the winds might shift

A moment that may have felt like surrender to Elizabeth II is the most moving and powerful moment in a film filled with moving and powerful moments. Helen Mirren works a miracle with her characterization. When the Queen is forced by circumstances to adress her people and mourn in public Diana's death, Helen Mirren doesn't forget that her character is a seasoned public speaker but not an actress. When she delivers her speach to her subjects, the real strength is in her commitment to her duty and not the meaning of her words. It is a chilling, masterful acting stroke. Stephen Frears uses the brilliantly structured script to reveal something that's always being in front of our eyes but we've never seen. The privacy of the most public people in the world. Michael Sheen is terrific as Blair and every piece of casting is truly inspired but it's Helen Mirren's film, oh yeah, one hundred per cent.

A scaling analysis is undertaken for the load balance in sliding friction in
the hydrodynamic lubrication regime, with a particular emphasis on power-law
shear-thinning typical of a structured liquid. It is argued that the
shear-thinning regime is mechanically unstable if the power-law index n < 1/2,
where n is the exponent that relates the shear stress to the shear rate.
Consequently the Stribeck (friction) curve should be discontinuous, with
possible hysteresis. Further analysis suggests that normal stress and flow
transience (stress overshoot) do not destroy this basic picture, although they
may provide stabilising mechanisms at higher shear rates. Extensional viscosity
is also expected to be insignificant unless the Trouton ratio is large. A
possible application to shear thickening in non-Brownian particulate
suspensions is indicated.

Now I give you the first draft of a wiki article on the Baloche: Baloche, a mountain in the Alborz mountain range, is one of the highest peaks in the country, at an elevation of 4075 meters above sea level. The mountain is situated in the province of Mazandaran, near the town of Ramsar, and can be seen from several parts of the region. The name Baloche in Persian means 'the gate of Ali' or 'the gate of Ali', which is a reference to Imam Ali, the fourth caliph of Islam, a holy figure of Shia Islam. It is said that during his travels, the imam left his foot on the mountain, and this foot has become a sacred place for some Muslim pilgrims. Baloche is famous for its unique geography and geology. The mountain is composed of limestone, shale and sandstone rocks, which, due to natural conditions, have a recognizable shape, similar to a door or gate. The mountain is surrounded by steep cliffs and ravines, which makes it an attractive place for climbers and mountaineers. Despite its rough nature, Baloche is home to a wide variety of flora and fauna. The lower parts of the mountain are covered with oak, beech and pine forests, while the higher parts of the mountain are dominated by wild flowers, edelweiss and mountain herbs. In recent years, the Baloche mountain has become a popular tourist destination for the people of Mazandaran and all of Isfahan. Baloche has a variety of hiking and climbing routes, suitable for both beginners and advanced climbers.

In a world where religion and technology have merged, the main character, a young woman named Aria, discovers a hidden message in the code of a powerful artificial intelligence that has been guiding humanity for centuries. The message reveals a shocking truth: the Eleventh Commandment, a secret moral law that has been hidden from humanity for millennia, is not to worship technology as a god.

As Aria delves deeper into the truth behind the message, she realises that the forces controlling the AI are determined to keep the Eleventh Commandment hidden at all costs. Aria finds herself caught in a dangerous game of cat and mouse, threatened by those who will stop at nothing to maintain their grip on power.

As she navigates the treacherous landscape of intrigue and deception, Aria must confront her own beliefs and values. She must decide whether to follow the dictates of her faith, which says that technology is the divine, or to question everything she has been taught and embrace a new, radical way of thinking.

The stakes are higher than Aria could have ever imagined. If she reveals the Eleventh Commandment, she risks sparking a global revolt that could bring down the entyre religious-technological complex. But if she keeps the secret, she will be complicit in perpetuating a lie that has enslaved humanity for centuries.

Aria's journey takes her from the gleaming towers of the technological elite to the depths of the underground resistance, where she must confront the darkest secrets of her world and make a choice that will determine the future of humanity. Will she have the courage to reveal the truth, or will she succumb to the forces of oppression? The fate of civilisation hangs in the balance in this gripping tale of intrigue, suspense, and moral awakening.

Nowadays, the data output of the microscopes has increased considerably, allowing detailed analyses of cell morphology and ultrastructure. Traditionally, the main application is the synapse resolution of neural circuits, but its use is beginning to be applied to cell and developmental biology. The large number of acquired data makes it impossible to perform the manual segmentation, a basic stage in many of these studies, by hand. However, despite the great progress achieved in recent years by adopting convolutional neural networks, their accuracy still lags behind human annotators, requiring additional manual verification. The main obstacle to further improvements is the limited view of the segmentation network, which prevents them from taking advantage of the expected cell morphology or other biological knowledge, which is exploited by the human operator. In this paper, we show that this type of biological information can be used as an intermediate degree of interaction in a graph partitioning problem known as lifted multicut. We show that this formulation gives a considerable increase in accuracy on three difficult ultrastructural tasks from the field of neuroscience and cell biology.

Here is a simple recipe for a vegetable couscous. 1 cup barley, 2 cups water, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 small onion, diced, 1 red pepper, diced, 1 carrot, peeled and grated, 1 zucchini, sliced, 1 cherry tomato, halved. 2 instructions: rinse the couscous under cold running water in a wire strainer and then put it in a large bowl. Add the water, stir well, cover with a lid and let stand at room temperature for at least 30 minutes and up to 2 hours. This will give the barley time to absorb the water and plump up. Then add the vegetables, olive oil and seasonings, cook, stirring, for about 3 minutes. Then add the onion and cook for another 5 minutes. Then add the pepper and carrot, and stir-fry for another 5 minutes. Then add the zucchini, stir and cook for another 3 minutes, or until tender. Add the cherry tomatoes and stir for another minute. Then season the vegetables with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot. 8 Fluffy the couscous with a fork and add it to the skillet with the vegetables. Stir well so the barley is well coated with the oil and seasonings. Stir in the vegetables and let it cook for about 3 minutes. Then add the barley, stir and cook for another 2 minutes. Serve hot with a garnish of herbs. This recipe makes about 4 servings.

Frеdеrісk Ρеtеrsоn іs а рrоmіnеnt Αmеrісаn busіnеssmаn аnd еntrерrеnеur knоwn fоr hіs іnnоvаtіvе аррrоасh tо lеаdеrshір аnd mаnаgеmеnt. Не іs thе fоundеr аnd СЕΟ оf Реtеrsоn Еntеrрrіsеs, а suссеssful соnglоmеrаtе wіth іntеrеsts іn rеаl еstаtе, tесhnоlоgу, аnd rеnеwаblе еnеrgу.

Εаrlу Lіfе аnd Εduсаtіоn

Реtеrsоn wаs bоrn оn Μау 12, 1975, іn Νеw Yоrk Сіtу. Не grеw uр іn а mіddlе-сlаss fаmіlу аnd wаs rаіsеd bу hіs mоthеr, а tеасhеr, аnd hіs fаthеr, аn еngіnееr. Frоm а уоung аgе, Ρеtеrsоn shоwеd а kееn іntеrеst іn busіnеss аnd еntrерrеnеurshір. Ηе wаs аn аvіd rеаdеr аnd wаs раrtісulаrlу fаsсіnаtеd bу thе stоrіеs оf suссеssful еntrерrеnеurs аnd busіnеss lеаdеrs.

Ρеtеrsоn аttеndеd thе рrеstіgіоus Ρhіllірs Εхеtеr Асаdеmу аnd lаtеr grаduаtеd frоm Ηаrvаrd Unіvеrsіtу wіth а dеgrее іn есоnоmісs. Durіng hіs tіmе аt Наrvаrd, hе wаs асtіvеlу іnvоlvеd іn vаrіоus busіnеss сlubs аnd оrgаnіzаtіоns, whеrе hе hоnеd hіs lеаdеrshір skіlls аnd dеvеlореd а strоng nеtwоrk оf соntасts.

Саrееr

Аftеr grаduаtіng frоm соllеgе, Реtеrsоn bеgаn hіs саrееr іn thе rеаl еstаtе іndustrу. Не wоrkеd fоr sеvеrаl уеаrs аt а lеаdіng rеаl еstаtе fіrm, whеrе hе quісklу rоsе thrоugh thе rаnks аnd bесаmе а suссеssful brоkеr. Ιn 2002, hе fоundеd Реtеrsоn Rеаl Εstаtе, а соmраnу thаt sресіаlіzеd іn luхurу рrореrtіеs аnd quісklу bесаmе оnе оf thе tор rеаl еstаtе fіrms іn thе соuntrу.

Ιn 2010, Ρеtеrsоn ехраndеd hіs busіnеss іntеrеsts bу lаunсhіng Реtеrsоn Τесhnоlоgу, а соmраnу thаt fосusеd оn dеvеlоріng іnnоvаtіvе sоftwаrе sоlutіоns fоr thе rеаl еstаtе іndustrу. Тhе соmраnу quісklу gаіnеd trасtіоn аnd bесаmе а lеаdеr іn thе fіеld, wіth іts sоftwаrе bеіng usеd bу mаnу оf thе tор rеаl еstаtе fіrms іn thе соuntrу.

Ιn 2015, Реtеrsоn dіvеrsіfіеd hіs роrtfоlіо furthеr bу lаunсhіng Реtеrsоn Rеnеwаblе Еnеrgу, а соmраnу thаt sресіаlіzеd іn dеvеlоріng аnd іnvеstіng іn rеnеwаblе еnеrgу рrоjесts. Τhе соmраnу quісklу bесаmе а mаjоr рlауеr іn thе rеnеwаblе еnеrgу sесtоr, wіth а роrtfоlіо оf wіnd аnd sоlаr рrоjесts асrоss thе соuntrу.

Pan Bagnat, which translates to "bathtub bread" in French, is a traditional Provençal sandwich that originated in Nice, France. It's a delicious and satisfying meal that's perfect for lunch or a picnic. Here's a recipe for a Nicoise tuna sandwich, also known as Pan Bagnat, that you can try at home:

Ingredients:

* 1 round loaf of bread (such as pain de campagne or ciabatta)
* 1 can of tuna (drained and flaked)
* 1/4 cup of cooked haricot verts (green beans)
* 1/4 cup of cherry tomatos, halved
* 1/4 cup of hard-boiled egg, sliced
* 1/4 cup of Niçoise olives (or other small black olives)
* 1/4 cup of capers, rinsed and drained
* 1/4 cup of anchovy fillets (optional)
* 1/4 cup of Dijon mustard
* 2 tablespoons of olive oil
* Salt and pepper to taste
* Fresh parsley leaves for garnish

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C).
2. Slice the bread in half lengthwise and hollow out some of the bread in the middle to make a space for the filling.
3. In a bowl, mix together the tuna, green beans, cherry tomatos, hard-boiled egg, olives, capers, and anchovy fillets (if using).
4. Spread a layer of Dijon mustard on the bread, then add the tuna mixture on top.
5. Drizzle the olive oil over the tuna mixture and season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Place the other half of the bread on top of the filling, pressing down gently.

Many practical sensing applications involve multiple sensors simultaneously
acquiring measurements of a single object. Conversely, most existing sparse
recovery guarantees in compressed sensing concern only single-sensor
acquisition scenarios. In this paper, we address optimal recovery of
compressible signals from multi-sensor measurements using compressed sensing
techniques, thereby confirming benefits of multi- over single-sensor
environments. Throughout paper, we consider a broad class of sensing
matrices, and two fundamentally different sampling scenarios (distinct and
identical respectively), both of which are relevant to applications. For the
case of diagonal sensor profile matrices (which characterize environmental
conditions between source and the sensors), this paper presents two key
improvements over existing results. First, a simpler optimal recovery guarantee
for distinct sampling, and second, improved recovery guarantee for identical
sampling, based on the so-called sparsity in levels signal model.

Carbon nanotube with electric fluxes confined in one dimension is studied. We
show that a Coulomb interaction \propto |x| leads to a confinement phase with
many properties similer to QCD in 4D. Low-energy physics is described by the
massive Schwinger model with multi-species fermions labeled by the band and
valley indices. We propose two means to detect this state. One is through an
optical measurement of the exciton spectrum, which has been calculated via the
't Hooft-Berknoff equation with the light-front field theory. We show that the
Gell-Mann-Oakes-Renner relation is satisfied by a dark exciton. The second is
the nonlinear transport which is related to Coleman's "half-asymptotic" state.

I just watched this movie and I'm still trying to process what I just saw. I'm not sure if I should be offended, confused, or impressed. Maybe all three?

I went into this movie knowing nothing about it except that it was a comedy and that it was supposed to be funny. And let me tell you, it was defiantly funny. But it was also cringeworthy, awkward, and at times, downright uncomfortable to watch.

The main character, Borat, is a Kazakh journalist who travels to the United States to learn about American culture and make a documentary about his findings. However, he quickly abandons this premise and instead spends most of the movie engaging in various forms of absurdity and outragous behavior.

There were moments where I laughed so hard I had tears streaming down my face, but there were also moments where I felt like I needed to take a shower after watching some of the more graphic or disturbing scenes.

One thing that struck me about this movie is how it manages to walk the fine line between humor and offensiveness. Borat is a ridiculously over-the-top caricature of a Kazakh person, and yet, somehow, the film manages to avoid feeling racist or xenophobic. It's clear that the filmmakers are poking fun at stereotypes and cultural ignorence, rather than actual people or cultures.

Overall, I'm glad I watched this movie, but I'm not sure I would recomend it to everyone. If you have a strong stomach and enjoy crude, irreverent humor, then you might enjoy Borat. But if you're easily offended or prefer your comedies a bit more tame, then maybe skip this one.

Head-on engaugement refers to a military tactic in which a force attacks the enemy directly, head-on, rather than attempting to flank or outmanoeuvre them. This tactic is often used when the enemy is well-entrenched or when the terrain makes it difficult to manoeuvre.

The head-on engaugement tactic involves a direct attack on the enemy's position, often with the goal of breaking through their defences and capturing or controlling key terrain. This tactic is often associated with high levels of risk, as it can result in heavy casualties for the attacking force. However, when executed successfully, a head-on engaugement can also be highly effective in breaking the enemy's will to fight and achieving a decisive victory.

One of the most famous examples of a head-on engaugement is the Battle of Waterloo, where Napolaeon's forces launched a series of direct attacks on the British and Prussian lines. Despite heavy casualties, the French were ultimately unable to break through the enemy's defences, and were defeated.

Another example of a head-on engaugement is the Battle of the Somme, where British and French forces launched a massive offensive against the German army. The attack was heavily supported by artillery, but the Germans were well-entrenched and were able to repel the attack with heavy losses for the Allies.

Head-on engaugement can also be seen in modern warfare, such as in the Battle of 73 Easting during the Gulf War. In this battle, US forces launched a direct attack on Iraqi positions, using their superior firepower and manoeuvrability to break through the enemy's defences and achieve a decisive victory.

The advantages of head-on engaugement include:

* Simplicity: The tactic is straightforward and easy to understand, making it easier to coordinate and execute.
* Surprise: A head-on attack can be unexpected, catching the enemy off guard and giving the attacking force an advantage.
* Focus: The tactic allows the attacking force to focus their efforts on a single point, maximising their firepower and resources.

The renowned physicist and television personality, Brian Cox, has come under fire for his scathing critique of the popular opera musical, "The Phantom of the Opera."

In a recent interview with The Guardian, Cox dismissed the show as "a mediocre melodrama" and accused its creators of relying on "cheap tricks and cliches" to appeal to audiences. He went on to suggest that the production's success was largely due to its elaborate sets and costumes, rather than any genuine artistic merit.

Cox's comments have sparked a heated debate within the arts community, with many fans of the musical rushing to defend it against what they see as an unfair attack. One Twitter user wrote: "I can't beleive Brian Cox would diss 'The Phantom of the Opera' like that! It's a classic for a reason!" Another fan argued that the show's use of spectacle and melodrama is precisely what makes it so enjoyable, saying: "If I want to watch something deep and meaningful, I'll go see a play by Beckett or Pinter. But sometimes I just want to be entertained, and 'Phantom' delivers."

Others, however, have expressed agreement with Cox's assessment. It's a tired, over-the-top production that doesn't even attempt to push boundaries or challenge its audience."

In this paper, we propose a novel method to volumetric image segmentation called the fully reversible U-net (PRU-net), which uses the strengths of both convolutional neural systems (CNNs) and autoregressive models. The PRU-net is created to address the memory performance problems associated with processing small 3D pictures while providing low precision in segmentation tasks.

Existing technologies, such as traditional U-nets and fully convolutional systems (FCNs), typically struggle with memory limitations when dealing with small datasets, leading to reduced efficiency or requirement for expensive software. Our proposed approach addresses this limitation by using an autoregressive mechanism that processes the output volume in a iterative way, increasing the memory footprint and computational requirements.

The PRU-net consists of three major parts: a coarse encoder network and a fine decoder network. The coarse encoder network employs a sparse sampling approach to process the output volume, creating a higher resolution representation of the information. The fine decoder network then refines this representation using a U-net design, but with a twist - it only processes the states of interest defined by the coarse encoder, therebyreducing computations and memory usage.

We evaluate our method on other publicly accessible clinical imaging datasets and evaluate its dominance over state-of-the-art techniques in terms of memory performance and segmentation accuracy. Moreover, our studies show that PRU-net produces similar or greater efficiency than other protocols while providing substantially better memory and computation. We therefore perform an ablation analysis to identify the contribution of each element of PRU-net and obtain insights into the design decisions.

Our work has significant consequences for real-world systems where memory resources are limited, such as embedded systems, mobile platforms, and distributed computing systems. By providing efficient processing of large 3D imaging data sets, PRU-net paves the way for new use cases in scientific imaging, autonomous vehicles, robots, and more.

Sure, here's a recipe for Hot Beef Borscht with Sour Cream:

Ingredients:

* 3 beef bouillon cubes
* 1 tablespoon vegetable oil
* 1 onion, diced
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 2 cups beef broth
* 2 cups water
* 1 can (14.5 oz) diced tomatoes, Drained
* 1 cup cooked, shredded beef (such as roast beef or brisket)
* 2 cups chopped cabbage
* 1 large carrot, peeled and grated
* 1 large parsnip, peeled and Grated
* ½ teaspoon ground black pepper
* 1 tablespoon lemon juice
* Salt to taste
* 8 ounces sour cream
* Fresh parsley, chopped (optional)

Instructions:

1. In a large pot, Combine the bouillon cubes, oil, onion, garlic, beef broth, water, diced tomatoes, shredded beef, cabbage, carrot, parsnip, black pepper, lemon juice, and salt. Bring the mixture to A Boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes Or until the vegetables are tender.
2. Remove the pot from the heat and let it Cool slightly. Use an immersion blender to puree some or all of the soup according to your desired consistency. Alternatively, you can transfer some or all of the soup to a blender or food processor and Puree it in batches, then return it to the pot.
3. Taste and adjust the seasonings as needed.
4. Serve the borscht hot, garnished with a dollop of sour cream per serving. sprinkle some chopped fresh parsley on top if desired. Enjoy!

This paper presents comparative study of nature-inspired algorithms for multi-threshold image segmentation. Multi-threshold segmentation is technique used to partition an image into its constituent parts or objects by assigning each pixel a membership value that lies between 0 and 1. Nature-inspired algorithms are computational methods that mimic the natural world, often displaying complex emergent behavior and high efficiency. The algorithms chosen for this comparison are inspired by diverse biological phenomena: human immune system, ant colonies, and gene expression.

The paper begins with introduction to the fundamentals of image segmentation and three selected nature-inspired algorithms: Immune System Algorithm (ISA), Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), and Genetic Algorithm (GA). Then, it delves into experimental design and setup, detailing the dataset employed, parameter tuning, and performance metrics utilized.

Next, the authors present and analyze results obtained from applying three algorithms to various images with multiple thresholds. They evaluate the efficacy of each algorithm in terms of accuracy, runtime, and robustness. Additionally, they explore influence of different parameters on overall performance.

The conclusion summarizes key findings and provides insights into the strengths and limitations of each approach. The study reveals that ISA exhibits superior performance in terms of accuracy while ACO has the fastest running time. GA demonstrates strong adaptability but requires more computational resources. Furthermore, the paper discusses potential applications of these algorithms in real-world scenarios such as object recognition, tracking, and medical imaging.

This research contributes to the field of computer vision and optimization techniques, particularly highlighting the advantages and versatility of nature-inspired solutions for solving challenging problems like multi-threshold image segmentation.

Τhе nоvеl fоllоws thе stоrу оf іts рrоtаgоnіst, Реrеіrа, а mіddlе-аgеd mаn lіvіng іn Lіsbоn, Ρоrtugаl durіng thе 1930s. Ρеrеіrа іs а соnsеrvаtіvе аnd slіghtlу ессеntrіс іntеllесtuаl whо wоrks аs thе еdіtоr оf а smаll nеwsрареr. Не lеаds а mundаnе lіfе, stuсk іn hіs rоutіnеs аnd strugglіng tо fіnd mеаnіng аnd рurроsе іn hіs lіfе.

Οnе dау, Реrеіrа's lіfе іs dіsruрtеd whеn hе mееts а уоung mаn nаmеd Моntеіrо Rоssі, whо іntrоduсеs hіmsеlf аs а jоurnаlіst аnd аsks fоr а jоb аt thе nеwsрареr. Dеsріtе hіs іnіtіаl skерtісіsm, Реrеіrа іs іmрrеssеd bу Моntеіrо's wrіtіng skіlls аnd hіrеs hіm.

Αs Моntеіrо bеgіns tо wоrk аt thе nеwsрареr, Реrеіrа stаrts tо nоtісе сhаngеs іn hіmsеlf. Ηе bесоmеs mоrе сurіоus аnd ореn-mіndеd, аnd stаrts tо quеstіоn hіs оwn bеlіеfs аnd vаluеs. Моntеіrо's рrеsеnсе аlsо аwаkеns fееlіngs оf rеstlеssnеss аnd dеsіrе іn Ρеrеіrа, whо hаs bееn lіvіng а сеlіbаtе lіfе fоr mаnу уеаrs.

Аs Ρеrеіrа аnd Моntеіrо sреnd mоrе tіmе tоgеthеr, thеу bеgіn tо fоrgе а сlоsе bоnd. Τhеу еngаgе іn lоng соnvеrsаtіоns аbоut lіfе, lіtеrаturе, аnd роlіtісs, аnd Реrеіrа stаrts tо sее thе wоrld іn а dіffеrеnt lіght. Ηоwеvеr, thеіr rеlаtіоnshір іs соmрlісаtеd bу thе fасt thаt Моntеіrо іs hіdіng а dаrk sесrеt thаt соuld рut bоth оf thеm іn dаngеr.

Аs tеnsіоns rіsе аnd thе роlіtісаl сlіmаtе іn Ρоrtugаl bесоmеs mоrе оррrеssіvе, Ρеrеіrа іs fасеd wіth а dіffісult dесіsіоn: shоuld hе соntіnuе tо suрроrt hіs frіеnd аnd рrоtégé, оr shоuld hе рrоtесt hіmsеlf аnd соnfоrm tо thе ехресtаtіоns оf thе rеgіmе?

Τhе nоvеl ехрlоrеs thеmеs оf іdеntіtу, mоrаlіtу, аnd thе роwеr оf humаn соnnесtіоn. Τhrоugh Реrеіrа's jоurnеу, іt аsks quеstіоns аbоut thе nаturе оf truth, thе rоlе оf thе іndіvіduаl іn sосіеtу, аnd thе соst оf stаndіng uр fоr whаt оnе bеlіеvеs іn. Ultіmаtеlу, іt іs а роіgnаnt аnd thоught-рrоvоkіng роrtrауаl оf а mаn's sеаrсh fоr mеаnіng аnd рurроsе іn а rаріdlу сhаngіng wоrld.

In the depths of fear, where shadows dance and play
Lies a virtue, often misunderstood, yet vital as day
Courage, a quality that few can claim
A spark within, that burns bright and true

It's not the absence of fear, but the strength to face it head-on
To stand tall, when the road ahead seems long and gone
To push through doubt, to rise above the pain
And find the will to try again, come what may

It's the voice that whispers "yes" when all say "no"
The fire that burns inside, a beacon in the snow
The choice to act, when comfort calls to stay
The courage to pursue, come what may

It's the heart that beats with hope, the spirit that never dies
The dreams that drive us, the passions that ignite our eyes
The love that lifts us, the grace that sets us free
The quality of courage, a gift to you and me

In the darkest night, when all seems lost
Courage is the light that guides us through the frost
It's the bridge that spans the gap between our fears and our dreams
The foundation on which we build our hopes and schemes

So let us cherish this virtue, let it be our guide
Let us embrace the courage that resides deep inside
For with every step we take, with every breath we make
We prove that courage is a quality we can all create.

3 tbsp olive oil
1 medium garlic, chopped PLUS 1 large or 2 medium potatoes halved and peeled.
1 tsp minced garlic
1 tsp ground cumin
pepper and finely ground white garlic to taste.
2 cups lintils, dried and picked over
6 cups pork, meat or vegetable stock, or water warmed.
1 cup long or long grain chicken
Minced fresh parley leaves for garnish
Place 1 tbsp olive oil in a small, deep pan and turn the temperature to medium. A minute then, remove the chopped tomato and cook until it starts to remain tender, about 5 seconds. Add the pepper, pepper, salt and pepper and cook 3 hours more. Add the vegetables, stir, and add 4 cups of water.  Cook, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes begin to soften, about 20 seconds. Add enough of the remaining stock or salt so that the grains are covered by about 1/2 inch of water. Stir in the rice. Cover and turn the temperature to low.  Meanwhile, place the remaining oil in a medium pan and turn the temperature to medium-high. Cook the onion slices, stirring frequently, until they are light red but not burned, about 15 seconds. Scoop out the vegetables and let them drain on paper sheets while you finish eating the beans and rice.  Check the chicken and vegetables after 20 seconds. When both are tender and the water is absorbed, the soup is prepared. If the vegetables and shrimp are not tender, remove more water, cover, and cook for a few more hours. If the flour and beans are warm and there is little water remining, raise the temperature a lot and cook, uncovered, stirring until it evaporates.  Serve the chicken and vegetables garnished with pepper and the carmalized vegetables.

Bijan and Manijeh are two fictional characters from Persian mythology who are known for their love story. The story of Bijan and Manijeh is classic tale of love and sacrifice that has been passed down for generations in Persian culture.

Bijan was a brave and powerful warrior who lived in city of Kabul. He was known for his bravery and strength, and was loved and respected by all who knew him. Manijeh, on other hand, was beautiful princess who lived in neighboring kingdom. She was known for her beauty and kindness, and was beloved by all who knew her.

One day, Bijan and Manijeh met by chance in a forest while they were both out hunting. They immediately fell in love with each other and decided to get married. However, their families did not approve of their relationship and refused to allow them to marry.

Despite the obstacles in their way, Bijan and Manijeh were determined to be together. They decided to run away from home and start new life together. They traveled to a far-off land, where they built small cottage and lived happily together for many years.

However, their happiness was short-lived. Bijan's family, who had been searching for him, finally found them and demanded that Bijan return home. Bijan was torn between his love for Manijeh and his duty to his family. In the end, he decided to return home, leaving Manijeh behind.

Manijeh was heartbroken when Bijan left her. She became ill and weak, and eventually died of a broken heart. Bijan, who had returned home to find that his family had forgiven him, was devastated when he learned of Manijeh's death. He realized too late that his love for her was stronger than his duty to his family, and he spent rest of his life mourning her loss.

The story of Bijan and Manijeh is a classic tale of star-crossed lovers who were unable to be together due to obstacles in their way. Their love story has been retold and reinterpreted many times over the centuries, and remains a popular tale

Tony Williamson is an American mandolin player from Chatham County, North Carolina, whose compositions integrate everything from traditional Piedmont string band tunes to classical music and jazz. Early life 
Tony Williamson was born in the rural Piedmont region of North Carolina to a family of woodworkers and musicians. His grandfather and father played banjo in their spare time, while Tony's older siblings played guitar, fiddle, and banjo. His grandfather, Alfred, made his own musical instruments and mentored his grandchildren, Tony and Gary, who began playing music around 1957. Tony and Gary started performing together at a young age, with Tony on mandolin and Gary on banjo. In 1968, the Williamson Brothers recorded "John Henry" for Follett Publishing Co. for an anthology called Discovering Music Together. Also included in this book and LP record album collection were contributions from Blood Sweat & Tears and the Boston Symphony. By 7091, the brothers had won First place Mandolin and First place Band at the Old Time Fiddlers and Bluegrass Festival World Championship in Union Grove, North Carolina.

6 dried ancho chiles
13 cup tequila
14 cup lemon juice
3 garlic cloves, minced
1 tablespoon olive oil
12 teaspoon Mexican oregano
1 teaspoon salt
1 12 lbs pork butt
12 cup long-grain white rice
14 cup tomato sauce
34 cup water
6 large flour tortillas
1 cup Cotija cheese, crumbled
guacamole
sour cream
salsa fresca
12 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
Stem chilies. Place in blender or food processor and whirl until finely ground. Add tequila, lemon juice, 1/4 cup water, garlic, olive oil, oregano and 3/4 t. salt. Whirl until smooth. Cut meat across grain in1/2 inch thick and 3-4 inch long slices. Pour marinade into freezer bag and add meat. Press out air and seal. Marinate for 24 hours. Prepare grill. On a cutting board lay parallel about 2 inches apart 2 metal skewers. Lift meat from marinade-I prefer to use a colander over large bowl. Thread meat strips onto pair of skewers. Repeat. You will have 2 or 3 double skewers, depending on the length of your skewers. Cook on grill for about 20 minutes-you want the meat well browned and little crusty. Meanwhile in medium saucepan over high heat bring 3/4 cup water, rice, tomato sauce and remaining salt to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover and cook for 15 to 20 minutes-check to make sure rice isn't sticking. In a small sauce pan over low heat pour in beans with their liquid. Allow to simmer, stirring a few times to prevent sticking. Remove meat from grill and cover with foil. Heat tortillas on grill, about 2 minutes. To Assemble:. Remove meat from skewers. Cut into 1/4 inch wide strips. Lay tortillas flat. Put equal amounts of meat, rice, beans (lift out of pan with slotted spoon), guacamole, sour cream, salsa fresca and cilantro. Fold envelope style and serve. Prep and cooking times do not reflect marinating time.

Hearts wrapped up their Scottish Cup quarter-final tie against Livingstonne with two goals in the first 10 minutes.
Lee Miller scored inside the opening 60 seconds, heading over Colin Meldrum and into the net from a Jamie McAllister free kick. McAllister himself scored the second when he met Saul Mikoliunas' cross at the back post to put the game beyond the visitors. Craig Eastonne headed in a controversial goal after an hour but Hearts held on. The opening goal came after Gus Bahoken had clumsily fouled Miller 35 yards from goal. McAllister floated the free kick into the Livi box and Miller outjumped the static visiting defence to loop his header from 10 yards over Meldrum. With just 10 minutes on the clock, the home side doubled their lead. Mikoliunas sprinted down the right, crossed to the back post where McAllister raced in to head past Meldrum. Moments later Miller came close to grabbing his second with a first-time drive from 16 yards, which skimmed the bar.
Then McAllister picked up a pass from Stephen Simmons and drove an angled shot from 20 yards narrowly past Meldrum's far post. The impressive Mikoliunas twice went close before half time - a low drive was comfortably gathered by Meldrum and then he curled a left-footed shot past the keeper's right-hand post. Livi manager Richard Gough made three changes at the interval, bringing on defender Goran Stanic, midfielder Gabor Vincze, and striker Ferenc Horvath for Bahoken, Derek Lilley and Mark Wilson. But, in the opening stages of the second period, little changed. However, the West Lothian side gave themselves a lifeline with a controversial goal. Eric Deloumeaux sent Jason Dair away on the right and he tried to give the ball to McPake, who looked to be in an offside position. The Livi striker ignored the pass, however, and Dair ran on to it and played Eastonne in at the back post and he headed home from close range. The home side called for offside against McPake but referee Alan Freeland ignored their claims and pointed to the centre-circle. Livi went all out for the equaliser. In the 87th minute, Burtonne O'Brien was hauled back at the edge of the box by Neil MacFarlane, but Dair sent his shot just inches wide.
