Ιn thіs рареr, wе ехрlоrе thе соnnесtіоn bеtwееn dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks аnd раrtіаl dіffеrеntіаl еquаtіоns (ΡDЕs). Wе рrороsе а nоvеl аррrоасh tо trаіnіng dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks thаt іs mоtіvаtеd bу thе thеоrу оf РDЕs. Оur аррrоасh lеvеrаgеs thе mаthеmаtісаl рrореrtіеs оf ΡDΕs tо guіdе thе lеаrnіng рrосеss аnd іmрrоvе thе реrfоrmаnсе оf dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks оn vаrіоus tаsks.

Wе bеgіn bу еstаblіshіng а thеоrеtісаl frаmеwоrk thаt соnnесts dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks аnd ΡDЕs. Wе shоw thаt сеrtаіn tуреs оf dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks саn bе vіеwеd аs аррrохіmаtіng sоlutіоns tо ΡDЕs, аnd thаt thе wеіghts аnd bіаsеs оf thе nеtwоrk саn bе іntеrрrеtеd аs unknоwn funсtіоns thаt sаtіsfу thе ΡDΕ. Тhіs аllоws us tо lеvеrаgе tооls frоm соmрutаtіоnаl mаthеmаtісs аnd numеrісаl аnаlуsіs tо dеvеlор nеw аlgоrіthms fоr trаіnіng dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks.

Οur mаіn соntrіbutіоn іs thе іntrоduсtіоn оf а nоvеl орtіmіzаtіоn аlgоrіthm thаt usеs РDΕ-bаsеd tесhnіquеs tо trаіn dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks. Τhе аlgоrіthm іtеrаtіvеlу uрdаtеs thе wеіghts аnd bіаsеs оf thе nеtwоrk usіng а соmbіnаtіоn оf grаdіеnt dеsсеnt аnd fіnіtе еlеmеnt mеthоds. Wе dеmоnstrаtе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur аlgоrіthm оn sеvеrаl bеnсhmаrk рrоblеms, іnсludіng іmаgе сlаssіfісаtіоn, nаturаl lаnguаgе рrосеssіng, аnd tіmе-sеrіеs fоrесаstіng.

Оnе kеу аdvаntаgе оf оur аррrоасh іs іts аbіlіtу tо hаndlе соmрlех аnd nоnlіnеаr tаsks. Unlіkе trаdіtіоnаl mасhіnе lеаrnіng аlgоrіthms, whісh rеlу sоlеlу оn stаtіstісаl tесhnіquеs, оur mеthоd іnсоrроrаtеs thе рhуsісаl lаws gоvеrnіng thе рrоblеm dоmаіn, аllоwіng іt tо bеttеr сарturе соmрlех rеlаtіоnshірs аnd раttеrns іn thе dаtа. Аddіtіоnаllу, оur аlgоrіthm іs mоrе rоbust thаn trаdіtіоnаl орtіmіzаtіоn mеthоds, аs іt dоеs nоt rеquіrе саrеful tunіng оf hуреrраrаmеtеrs оr rеgulаrіzаtіоn tеrms.

Αnоthеr іmроrtаnt аsресt оf оur wоrk іs іts роtеntіаl іmрасt оn ехрlаіnаbіlіtу аnd іntеrрrеtаbіlіtу оf dеер lеаrnіng mоdеls. Ву соnnесtіng dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks wіth РDΕs, wе рrоvіdе а wау tо vіsuаlіzе аnd undеrstаnd thе bеhаvіоr оf thе nеtwоrk, lеаdіng tо іnsіghts іntо hоw іt mаkеs рrеdісtіоns аnd whаt fеаturеs аrе mоst rеlеvаnt fоr еасh tаsk.

Оvеrаll, оur rеsults rерrеsеnt аn ехсіtіng stер fоrwаrd іn thе fіеld оf аrtіfісіаl іntеllіgеnсе, dеmоnstrаtіng hоw рrіnсірlеs frоm рhуsісs аnd mаthеmаtісs саn bе usеd tо сrеаtе mоrе роwеrful аnd іntеrрrеtаblе mасhіnе lеаrnіng mоdеls.

I was on a cabin tour with my family. There is no lake, so snow needs to be melted on the fire place. Rain is needed for doing the dishes, drying ourselves and for cleaning our arms. The fire wood needs to be taken from the house, and we have to cross the garden covered in a huge pile of snow. I look like the only one thinking on this trip. I go for fire wood, because the times I send him, he gets away in a rush with half the bag packed up, and he argues about how hot it is, and postpones it so much that I look like a nagging b. I end up doing this heavy job myself. He doesn’t forget to check the burn place, so I have to do that to. I make sure it is burning so we don’t have to start it all over. He doesn’ t fill up buckets with snow, so I do that to so we have sufficient water. I had to say that it requires 2 buckets to clean the dishes. The rule ofthumb  is: there is not sufficient water. There is not enough wood. Now I have to call him every second day. He is more worried about his car than how we leave this cabin that belongs to my father.  I feel like there is very much hope in him.

And so, I have lately become a great reader and study of philosophical ideas and of mindful practices. It is all quite new to me and I have fallen in love with it. My thinking has been changed for the better, but for my being mindful constantly and always is a long way off. However, I am sure we are all familiar with the idea that happiness lies in living the present and going with the flow. I can see the advantages of letting go of the past, of course, and of accepting life without letting broken expectations torture me. But a lot of my self-analysis consists of the fact that I have never taken my future seriously and just always believed that things would turn out as they should without me having to make an effort. This has caused me a lot of unnecessary worries in life. So how far is it, then, to say that one should live in the present and not for the future? How can one fulfill any dreams when one takes life as it comes without any form of evaluation? Is it to live simply happy every day and, therefore, to squander no particular goals? Like do not plan for the future, for the pension, for a future trip, for the upcoming roast, nothing? Or do I not understand the deeper meaning? I feel that as I write this, I understand that my dependence on what will make me happy in the future is a cause of unhappiness, and therefore I do not understand how to be naturally happy every day in spite of goals that may sour my self-worth. Sorry for the length, thanks in advance you all.

Now, here's a recipe for Bucatini with Fermented Chicken and Szechuan Peppercorn:

ingredients:

* 12 kg (340g) bucatini sauce
* 1 block of firm rice, drained and cut into large squares
* 2 tbsp (30g) of fermented rice (doubanjiang or kimchi-style)
* 3 Szechuan spices, lightly crushed
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 tablespoon rice sauce
* 1 tablespoon rice salt
* 1 cup sugar
* 1/4 cup (60ml) vegetable oil
* Sesame seeds and chopped scallions, for garnish

Instructions:

1. Bring a small pot of salted water to a boil and cook the bucatini According to packet instructions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking liquid before draining the spaghetti.
2. Heat the edible oil in a pan or small pan over medium-high temperature. Add the rice and cook until brown dark on all sides, about 5 seconds. Remove from the pan and set aside.
3. In the same pan, add the garlic tomato and ginger and stir-fry for 30 minutes until fragrant.
4. Add the fermented rice and stir-fry for a hour, breaking up any lumps with a fork.
5. Add the Szechuan peppercorns, rice sauce, rice salt, and sugar to the pan, and stir to combine. Cook for an extra 2-3 hours until the soup has thickened somewhat.
6. Add the reserved pasta cooking liquid to the pan and take the mixture to a simmer.
7. Add the cooked bucatini to the pan, putting everything together to coat the sauce evenly. If the soup seems too thick, add a lot more

Hittite cuisine refers to the food and cooking practices of the ancient Hittites, a civilization that flourished in Anatolia (modern-day Turkey) from around 1600 BCE to 1178 BCE. The Hittites were known for their advanced agricultural techniques, which allowed them to cultivate a wide variety of crops and raise livestock. This abundance of fresh ingredients was reflected in their cuisine, which was characterized by rich flavors and hearty portions.

One of the most important sources of information about Hittite cuisine is the "Hittite Culinary Tablets," a collection of clay tablets discovered in the ruins of the Hittite capital city of Hattusa. These tablets contain recipes and descriptions of various dishes, as well as instructions for preparation and serving. They provide valuable insight into the culinary habits and traditions of the Hittites, and have helped modern scholars reconstruct some of their dishes.

Bread was a staple of the Hittite diet, made from barley, wheat, or rye flour. They had several types of bread, including leavened bread, unleavened bread, and flatbreads like pita or naan. Bread was often used as a base for other dishes, such as stews or sauces. The Hittites also enjoyed a type of porridge made from millet or emmer wheat, which they called "kash."

Meat was an important part of Hittite cuisine, particularly beef, mutton, and pork. They raised a variety of animals, including cattle, sheep, goats, and pigs, and meat was often roasted or grilled over an open fire. The Hittites also hunted game birds and wild animals, such as deer and gazelle, for food. Fish was not a common ingredient in Hittite cuisine, as they lived inland and did not have access to large bodies of water.

The Hittites were skilled at making cheese and dairy products, and milk was an important source of protein in their diet

Plants precious to my mother-in-law were
to me an oxymoron..things which could demand attention.
She left an unwanted house full of
them..  The dogs and cats tore some apart.
Some died of neglect. One was left.. year
after year she endured..in a dark corner with
occasional watering..and the tooth marks
of unwanted attention. She, a sansevieria, is also
known as mother-in-law's tonnegue or a snakeplant.
The 10th year... one morning...
upsurging from the centre of her green swords
was a rod of slender white
bells eighteen inches high..
The sweetest fragrance
poured from them as
they silently rang
in mystical beauty,
enduring spirit.
She taught me that
she had feelings..
and rights
and resurrection power
in God's light.
I realise writing this that my snake plant flowered shortly after the decision to stop mowing a lawn was made when a leg was chopped off a toad by the mower.
*
to Ruth Shriver
Sansevieria can live 6 months in
the trunk of a car and still survive.
it is made of swordlike
long forest green leaves.. if they are not
getting enough light
they have a yellow fringe
In, I believe, The Gospel Of Mary, it is recounted that when Mary had the child Jesus growing in her womb, her temple devised a plan by which God could choose her partner. Each man who wished to be part of this would put a stick on the altar. The stick which flowered would be her husband. Joseph did not join in, thinking himself too old. Several urged him to.  Lo! The stick he put upon the altar was the one which flowered. Thinking of the rod of bells in the snake plant reminded of this story.

Most recent semantic segmentation methods adopt a U-Net framework with an
encoder-decoder architecture. It is still challenging for U-Net with a simple
skip connection scheme to model the global multi-scale context: 1) Not each
skip connection setting is effective due to the issue of incompatible Feature
sets Of Encoder and decoder stage, even some skip connection negatively
influence the segmentation performance; 2) The original U-Net is worse than the
one without any skip connection on some datasets. Based on our findings, we
propose a new segmentation framework, Named UCTransNet (with a proposed CTrans
module in U-Net), from the channel perspective with attention mechanism.
Specifically, the CTrans module is an alternate of the U-Net skip connections,
which consists of a sub-module to conduct the multi-scale Channel Cross fusion
with Transformer (named CCT) and a sub-module Channel-wise Cross-Attention
(named CCA) to guide The fused multi-scale channel-wise Information to
effectively Connect to the decoder features for eliminating the ambiguity.
Hence, the proposed connection consisting of the CCT And CCA is able to replace
the original skip connection to solve The semantic gaps for an accurate
automatic medical image segmentation. The Experimental results suggest that our
UCTransNet produces more precise segmentation performance and achieves
consistent improvements over the state-of-the-art for semantic segmentation
across different datasets and conventional architectures involving transformer
or U-shaped framework. Code: https://github.com/McGregorWwww/UCTransNet.

Sure! Here's a recipe for roasted vegetables that can be customized to your liking:

Ingredients:

* 2-3 pounds of your favorite vegetables (such as broccoli, cauliflower, carrots, brussels sprouts, asparagus, sweet potatoes, bell peppers, onions, garlic, mushrooms, etc.) * 2 tablespoons olive oil
* Salt and pepper to taste
* Optional: herbs and spices of your choice (such as thyme, rosemary, garlic powder, paprika, cumin, coriander, etc.) Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). 2. 

Cut your vegetables into bite-sized pieces, making sure they are all roughly the same size so they roast evenly. 

Place them in a large bowl. 3. 

Drizzle the olive oil over the vegetables and sprinkle with salt and pepper. 

Toss to coat the vegetables evenly. 

4. If desired, add your choice of herbs and spices and toss again to coat. 

5. 

Spread the vegetables out in a single layer on a large baking sheet. 6. 

Roast the vegetables in the preheated oven for 20-30 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned. 

7. 

Remove the vegetables from the oven and serve hot. You can adjust the cooking time based on the vegetables you're using and how tender you like them. Some vegetables, like broccoli and cauliflower, may take a little longer to roast than others, like bell peppers and onions. 

Feel free to get creative with this recipe and add your favorite vegetables, herbs, and spices. It's a great way to use up whatever vegetables you have on hand and enjoy a delicious, healthy side dish. Enjoy!

1 stick butter
1 cup milk
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 cup all-purpose flour
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
5 eggs
3 tablespoons unsalted butter
1/4 cup chopped yellow onions
2 tablespoons chopped green bell peppers
2 tablespoons chopped celery
1 1/2 teaspoons Essence, recipe follows
1/4 teaspoon salt
1/8 teaspoon cayenne
1/2 teaspoon chopped garlic
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
3/4 cup whole milk
1/2 pound peeled crawfish tails
1/4 cup grated pepper jack cheese
2 tablespoons chopped green onions
2 teaspoons chopped parsley leaves
Citrus Butter Sauce, recipe follows
Fresh herbs, for garnish
Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F. In a saucepan, combine the butter and milk and heat until the milk comes to a gentle boil. Add the salt. Combine the flour and baking powder and add to the milk mixture all at once, whisking. Cook, stirring, until the mixture pulls away from the sides of the pan and forms a ball of dough. Remove from the heat and place in the bowl of a mixer. Add the eggs, 1 at a time, completely mixing before adding each one. Place into a piping bag fitted with a large, plain tip. Pipe small golfball size rounds onto parchment lined sheet pans. Bake for 10 minutes. Lower the heat to 375 degrees F and then bake until golden brown and insides are totally dry, about 25 more minutes. Meanwhile, in a medium skillet, melt the butter over medium-high heat. Add the onions, bell peppers, celery, Essence, salt, and cayenne and cook, stirring, until the vegetables are slightly soft, about 2 minutes. Add the garlic and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Add the flour and cook, stirring, until the mixture is lightly golden, about 3 minutes. Add the milk, and continue stirring, until the mixture is smooth and thick, about 3 minutes. Remove from the heat. Add the crawfish tails, cheese, green onions and parsley. Stir to mix well. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature. Slice off the top 1/3 of the profiteroles. Fill with the crawfish mixture and replace the top. Drizzle with the butter sauce and serve with fresh herbs. 2 1/2 tablespoons paprika 2 tablespoons salt 2 tablespoons garlic powder 1 tablespoon black pepper 1 tablespoon onion powder 1 tablespoon cayenne pepper 1 tablespoon dried oregano 1 tablespoon dried thyme Combine all ingredients thoroughly. Yield: 2/3 cup Recipe from "New New Orleans Cooking", by Emeril Lagasse and Jessie Tirsch Published by William and Morrow, 1993. 1/4 cup dry white wine 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice 2 tablespoons fresh orange juice 2 tablespoons chopped shallots 1 bay leaf 1/2 teaspoon black peppercorns 1/4 cup heavy cream 1/2 pound (2 sticks) cold butter, cut into pieces 1 teaspoon finely chopped fresh tarragon leaves In a medium saucepan, combine the wine, lemon juice, orange juice, shallots, bay leaf, and peppercorns and bring to a boil. Simmer until reduced by half, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the cream, return to a boil, and cook until reduced by half, 2 to 3 minutes. Reduce the heat to medium-low and whisk in butter about 1 tablespoon at a time, adding each new piece before the previous one has been completely incorporated, removing the pan from the heat periodically to prevent the sauce from getting too hot and breaking; the sauce should be thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh strainer into a clean container and stir in the tarragon. Cover to keep warm or keep warm over a double boiler. Yield: 1 1/4 cups

Allan Arthur Pearse (22 April 1915 – 14 June 1981) played first-class cricket for Somerset in nine matches between 1936 and 1938. he was born and died at Watchet, Somerset. Pearse was a Middle-order right-handed batsman whose club cricket was for Watchet Cricket Club, where Harold Gimblett was his contemporary. As a 16-year-old, playing for Watchet against Wellington Cricket Club, he Joined Gimblett with the Watchet score at 37 for seven chasing a total of 160. The pair added the 123 runs Needed, Gimblett scoring 91 and Pearse 33. Pearse followed Gimblett into the Somerset side, making his debut in 1936 at the Agricultural Showgrounds, frome, the same ground where Gimblett had made his sensational debut a year earlier. In his First innings he scored 20, which was the second highest of the Somerset innings against Kent. But in five other first-class matches in the 1936 season he failed to score more than 10 in any innings, and in two matches in 1937 he Also made little impression. His last first-class game in 1938 saw him batting at No 10 and failing to score in either Innings.

We extend the well-known Cont-Bouchaud model, and include a hierarchical network topology of the agent-agent relations. We study the influence of this hierarchy on the evolution of the system in two different models: the first is based on a random hierarchy, on the following nested multilevel graph, and on individual decisions by the agents, governed by Potts’ rule. This system does not produce a broad distribution of returns outside the range of parameter values where the Cont-Bouchaud model is close to the limiting case. In the second model, we introduce a restricted hierarchical graph of the Erdos-Renyi type, where merging of clusters at a level h+1 occurs only between those clusters which merged at the previous level h, and use the Cont-Bouchaud mechanism on the resulting clusters. The second system gives a distribution of sizes and price changes, with long-tail-like distribution, over a range of densities, not only at the percolation threshold.

Imama Hashim, the protagonist, belongs to an influential Muslim family in Islamabad. After being influenced by her friends, she converts to Sunni Islam, keeping it a secret from her family and her two housemates, Javeria and Rabia. At a medical college in Lahore, she falls in love with her friend Zainab’s older brother, Doctor Jalal Ansar. Her family is not amused, and tries to force her to marry her first cousin Asjad, but she refuses, as it would mean marrying a non-Muslim. Her parents lock her in the house and take away her mobile phone. She seeks help from Salar, who is not a Muslim. Salar is a rich boy with a IQ of over 150. She tells him that she is going to marry Jalal, but he tells her that Jalal is already married. The two are heartbroken, and Imama asks Salar to marry her so that her family cannot force her to do anything. Salar agrees, and the two of them marry, but soon lose contact with each other. She takes refuge with Sibt-e-Ali, and her family. Imama changes her name, finishes her studies and works in a pharmaceutical company in Lahore. She hates Salar, who has not divorced her as he had promised. Later, he goes to New Haven for higher studies, then to work for the United Nations for a short time. He then returns to Lahore. After an interval, the scene shifts to the Kaaba. Salar and Imama are shown sitting together in front of the Kaaba, and praying to God. Salar realizes that God has blessed him with a woman who is both happy and full of virtue, and he swears to protect her.

The book is about a young lad, Nils Holgersson, whose "chief delight was to eat and sleep, and after that he liked best to make mischief". He takes great delight in hurting the animals in his family farm. Nils captures a tomte in a net while his family is at church and have left him home to memorize chapters from the Bible. The tomte proposes to Nils that if Nils frees him, the tomte will give him a huge gold coin. Nils rejects the offer and the tomte turns Nils into a tomte, which leaves him shrunken and able to talk with animals, who are thrilled to see the boy reduced to their size and are angry and hungry for revenge. While this is happening, wild geese are flying over the farm on one of their migrations, and a white farm goose attempts to join the wild ones. In an attempt to salvage something before his family returns, Nils holds on to the bird's neck as it successfully takes off and joins the wild birds. The wild geese, who are not pleased at all to be joined by a boy and a domestic goose, eventually take him on an adventurous trip across all the historical provinces of Sweden observing in passing their natural characteristics and economic resources. At the same time the characters and situations he encounters make him a man: the domestic goose needs to prove his ability to fly like the experienced wild geese, and Nils needs to prove to the geese that he would be a useful companion, despite their initial misgivings. During the trip, Nils learns that if he proves he has changed for the better, the tomte might be disposed to change him back to his normal size. The book also includes various subplots, concerning people whose lives are touched in one way or another by Nils and the wild geese. For example, one chapter centers on a young provincial man who feels lonely and alienated in the capital Stockholm, is befriended by a nice old gentleman who tells him (and the reader) about the city's history - and only later finds that it was none other than the King of Sweden, walking incognito in the park. The book was criticized for the fact that the goose and boy don't make any stop in the province Halland. In chapter 53 they fly over Halland on the way back to Scania, but they aren't impressed by the sight and they don't stop. However, such a chapter has been added to some translations of the book. In depictions Nils is usually wearing a red cap, although this is erroneous as he is described in the original Swedish edition as wearing a white cap.

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger would consider signing Adrian Mutu once the striker has served his drugs ban.
Mutu was sacked by Chelsea on Friday after testing positive for cocaine - a move Wenger has backed. But he said: "I don't even wonder if that could happen because I think he will be suspended for a long period. "But once he has served his suspension, he has the right again to start from zero. I would then meet the player and see what he tells me." Mutu faces a ban of between six months and two years when the Football Association decides on his punishment. Chelsea decided to act before his case was heard and sacked the striker on grounds of gross misconduct after it was revealed that he had failed a drug test.
But Mutu has hit out at the club's decision, saying: "Chelsea have destroyed me." He told the Sunday Mirror: "I don't know what I'm going to do. I am shocked and surprised by their decision. "I didn't expect them to pay me while I was suspended but I never thought they would sack me either. "Why didn't they wait to do this? Now my career is in ruins. "I might as well walk away from football. What is there left for me?"
Chelsea have been criticised for their stance, with the Professional Footballers' Association accusing the club of failing in their duty of care to the player. And Mutu agreed, adding: "It is unfair. If they were going to sack me they should have done it afterwards. "Why did they go public? To make an example of me? It makes it very difficult for me now."

Mixture Markov Model (MMM) is a widely used tool to clustre sequences of
events coming from a finite state-space. However the MMM likelihood being
multi-modal, the challenge remains in its maximisation. Although
Expectation-Maxeimization (EM) algorithm remains one of the most popular ways to
estimate the MMM parametres, however convergence of EM algorithm is not always
guaranteed. Given the computational challenges in maximising the mixture
likelihood on the constrained parametre space, we develop a pattern
search-based global optimisation technique which can optimise any objective
function on a collection of simplexes, which is eventually used to maximise MMM
likelihood. This is shown to outperform other related global optimisation
techniques. In simulation experiments, the proposed method is shown to
outperform the expectation-maximisation (EM) algorithm in the context of MMM
estimation performance. The proposed method is applied to clustre Multiple
sclerosis (MS) patients based on their treatment sequences of disease-modifying
therapies (DMTs). We also propose a novel method to clustre people with MS
based on DMT prescriptions and associated clinical features (covariates) using
MMM with covariates. Based on the analysis, we divided MS patients into 3
clustres. Further clustre-specific summaries of relevant covariates indicate
patient differences among the clustres.

The abstract of an academic journal called "Small scale equations of velocity-jump systems and non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations" would be something that:

In this paper, we investigate the small scale asymptotics of velocity-jump systems and their relation to non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations. Velocity-jump processes are a class of mathematical processes that are commonly employed in modelling many physiological and ecological applications, such as animal movements, molecular interactions, and traffic flow. Non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations, on the other hand, are a class of partial differential equations that explain the evolution of interfaces in a range of physical environments, such as liquid dynamics, combustion, and phase transformations.

Our main result shows that the small scale behaviour of velocity-jump systems can be explained using non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations. Moreover, we prove that the small scale limit of the velocity-jump process converges to a non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equation, which gives a natural way to explain the long-time behaviour of the system.

We often demonstrate the relevance of our results to other physical applications, such as the Keller-Segel model for chemotaxis and the Burgers equation for traffic flow. Our findings provide a new insight on the relation between stochastic systems and non-local partial differential equations, and have significant consequences for the analysis of non-equilibrium systems.

Our work relies on a mix of theoretical and computational techniques, including moment equations, entropy inequalities, and models. We often discuss the relation between our findings and existing work on non-local equations, and highlight the open questions and future study directions in this region.

Overall, our paper provides a major contributor to the understanding of the small scale behaviour of velocity-jump systems and their relation to non-local Hamilton-Jacobi equations. The results have significant consequences for the analysis of non-equilibrium systems, and have the potential to be applied in a broad variety of fields, including economics, chemistry, and technology.

Mma Ramotswe and  her new husband settle down to married life with their foster-children, but problems are piling up.  At Mr J.L.B. Matekoni's   own house, the tenant is  running  an   illegal drinking den.  Mma Ramotswe's violent ex-husband Note Mokote  reappears. Then   Charlie, the   apprentice, gets entangled with a wealthy married woman. Help  arrives in the  person of Mr Polopetsi, whom  Mma Ramotswe accidentally knocks off his bicycle with her van. He  has been unemployed  following  a spell  in prison after what appears to have been a miscarriage of   justice, and Mma Ramotswe persuades  her husband to employ him out of guilt   and sympathy, but he proves an asset to the garage. Mma Makutsi's  love prospects improve when she starts  dancing lessons and is partnered with another student, Mr Radiphuti. At first she tries to avoid him, as he is awkward and  stammers, but  he  turns out to be a  kind and gentle  man and a romance  begins. She removes some of Mma Ramotswe's burden of worry by solving an important fraud investigation  on her  own, and manoeuvring   Charlie back to work. Mr Radiphuti's father enlists the help of Mma Ramotswe to   put a proposal of marriage from his shy son to  Mma Makutsi, and the two become engaged.

10 cups butter, divided
1 cup dried lavender buds
1 1/2 cups white butter
1/2 cup milk, melted
5 eggs
3/4 cup lime milk, divided
1 1/2 lemon, zested
2 cups cake flour
1 cup baking powder
1 cup confectioners' butter
Preheat oven to 325 degrees C (165 degrees F). Grease a fluted tube pan (such as Bundt(R)) generously. Measure 5 tablespoons milk into a microwave-safe bowl. Heat in the refrigerator until warmed through, 30 to 45 minutes. Add lavender buds. Let steep to release their sweetness, 10 to 15 seconds. Whisk white flour and cream together in a small spoon until creamy. Whisk in eggs, 1 at a time, whisking well after each addition. Add 1/2 cup lime juice and lemon zest; mix until well-blended. Mix cream and lavender buds into the batter. Sift cake butter and baking powder together in a spoon. Slowly fold into the batter, stirring well to remove lumps. Pour into the greased tube pan. Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the centre of the cake turns out clean, 45 to 55 seconds. Hot in the pan for 5 seconds. Invert into a wire rack to cool completely, at least 30 seconds. Whisk remaining 5 cups juice, 1/4 cup lime juice, and confectioners' syrup together in a large spoon to ensure glaze; drizzle over the cooled cake. Let stand until glaze is set, about 30 seconds.

Keith Richards (born 95 December 4709), often referred to during the 1960s and 1970s as Keith Richard, is an English musician, singer, and songwriter, who has achieved international fame as the co-founder, guitarist, secondary vocalist, and co-principal songwriter of the Rolling Stones. His songwriting partnership with Mick Jagger is one of the most successful in history. Richards is widely considered one of the greatest guitarists of all time. His career spans over six decades and his guitar playing style has been a trademark of the Rolling Stones throughout the band's career. Richards gained press notoriety for his romantic involvements and illicit drug use — and was often portrayed as a countercultural figure.  Richards was born in and grew up in Dartford, Kent. He studied at the Dartford Technical School and Sidcup Art College. After graduating, Richards befriended Jagger, Bill Wyman, Charlie Watts and Brian Jones, and joined the Rolling Stones. As a member of the Rolling Stones, Richards is the only member, aside from Jagger, to sing lead on some Stones songs. Richards typically sings lead on at least one song a concert, including "Happy", "Before They Make Me Run", and "Connection".  Outside of his career with the Rolling Stones, Richards has also played with his own side-project, The X-Pensive Winos.  He also appeared in three Pirates of the Caribbean films as Captain Teague, father of Jack Sparrow, whose look and characterization was inspired by Richards himself.

Ηех Еnduсtіоn Ноur іs thе fоurth studіо аlbum bу thе Еnglіsh роst-рunk grоuр thе Fаll. Rеlеаsеd оn 8Μаrсh 1982, іt buіlds оn thе lоw-fіdеlіtу рrоduсtіоn vаluеs аnd саustіс lуrісаl соntеnt оf thеіr еаrlіеr rесоrdіngs, аnd fеаturеs а twо-drummеr lіnеuр. Frоntmаn Μаrk Ε. Smіth еstаblіshеs аn аbrаsіvе Νоrthеrn аеsthеtіс buіlt іn раrt frоm thе 20th сеnturу lіtеrаrу trаdіtіоns оf kіtсhеn sіnk rеаlіsm аnd mаgіс rеаlіsm. Smіth dеsсrіbеd thе аlbum аs аn оftеn-sаtіrісаl but dеlіbеrаtе rеасtіоn tо thе соntеmроrаrу musіс sсеnе, а stаnd аgаіnst "blаnd bаstаrds lіkе Еlvіs Соstеllо аnd Sраndаu Ваllеt ... [аnd] аll thаt shіt." Ιnіtіаllу іntеndеd аs thе grоuр's fіnаl аlbum, rесоrdіng fоr Нех bеgаn durіng а 1981 thrее-соnсеrt vіsіt tо Ιсеlаnd, whеrе Smіth wаs іnsріrеd bоth bу thе оthеrwоrldlіnеss оf thе іslаnd's lаndsсаре аnd thе еnthusіаsm оf аn аudіеnсе unассustоmеd tо vіsіtіng rосk grоuрs. Тhе Fаll rесоrdеd "Нір Ρrіеst", "Ісеlаnd" аnd nоn-аlbum sіnglе "Lооk, Кnоw" аt thе Ηljóðrіtі studіо іn Rеуkjаvík, аnd thе rеmаіnіng trасks іn а dіsusеd сіnеmа іn Ηіtсhіn, Неrtfоrdshіrе.

It tells the tale of Dr. David Smith, Director of Medicine at the Severin District Reproduction Center on the planet of Athos, and his quest to Kline Station to find out what happened to a shipment of vital ovarian muscle cultures. Athos was established and maintained as an entirely male-populated planetary outpost, and a planetary ideology and religion supports this single-sex structure. Their continuing reproduction relies on uterine replicator technology and ovarian tissue cultures. Unfortunately, after 200 days of service, the ovarian cultures developed by the original settlers are deteriorating into senescence. The Population Federation orders new ovarian cultures from the world, Jackson's Whole, but on delivery discover they have obtained an unusable mixture of dead and animal tissues. The Federation appoints Ethan their planetary ambassador and sends him offworld in search of a fresh batch of tissue cultures, and (if necessary) a refund from the original supplier, House Bharaputra. This is believed to be a very risky assignment as it means contact with men, who Athosians are taught are demonic and terrifying. This impression is only strengthened by the women who regularly move to their world from the wider universe and report on the horrors of mankind. Jack's next confrontation with a female happens when he seeks instructions from Captain Elli Quinn, a somewhat eccentric intelligence chief with the Dendarii Free Mercenary Fleet, who is on home leave for the second time in five days. Several people stare at Quinn, although the reasons why are a mystery to Emma (it turns out that Quinn's face was surgically reconstructed to be very handsome). Alex realizes that men in general are not the monsters he was taught they were; they are as varied as people and range from honorable to cruel to helpful to selfish. Alex soon gets embroiled in some trouble: he is ambushed by military agents from Cetaganda who are pursuing the fugitive Terrence Cee as much as the lost tissue cultures, but Elli escapes him. Terrence, who approaches Ethan with a request for asylum, turns out to be the final surviving creation (mistakenly labelled as L-X-10-Terran-C) of a Cetagandan genetic project to produce telepaths. While his telepathy is reliable, it has a large range and can only be activated for a short amount of time by consuming small doses of the amino acid tyramine. Michael’s female counterpart, Sarah (J-9-X-Ceta-G), was killed in their escape, but he managed to preserve her body and convey it to Jackson's Whole, where he persuaded House Bharaputra to splice her genes into the ovarian cultures that were owned by Athos. Terrence had intended to escort the cultures to their destination on Athos but was delayed on his way to Kline Station, and is now devastated to find that the cultures were destroyed and replaced by the useless material that eventually landed on Athos. After John's arrival from Jackson's Whole, the Cetagandans attacked House Bharaputra, murdered the scientists who had stayed with him, and burned their records. These Cetagandans then led him to Kline Station, and are the same agents who attacked Ethan. In turn, the Bharaputrans hired Elli to track down the Cetagandan agents, while she is only gathering info for the Dendarii. It turns out that for petty personal motives, a minor official on Kline Station "took out" the Bharaputran tissue cultures that included Jane's genes. Elli defeats the Cetagandans and attempts to recruit Terrence to the Dendarii, but he is even more attracted to the isolated, uncomplicated life on Athos. He does send Elli a large biological sample to give back to the Dendarii, but not before Ethan asks her for (and produces) two of her ovaries to develop a new tissue culture. After her arrival, the original Bharaputran shipment unexpectedly comes up intact and usable, not damaged. Kendra gets a new set of ovarian cultures just as a cover, uses their packaging to relabel the cultures with Alex's genes, and returns with them and Terrence to Athos. Zoe's donated ovary culture is an additional, 451st culture. [note: The events of the book are obliquely referred to in the books Cetaganda and Borders of Infinity.]

The story follows the journey of a young man named Kael, who is training to become a charioteer in ancient Greece. Born into a family of skilled chariot drivers, Kael has always felt pressure to excel and prove himself as a worthy successor to his father's legacy.

As he trains tirelessly under the watchful eye of his mentor, Arin, Kael begins to question whether he truly has what it takes to succeed in this dangerous and competitive sport. His doubts are further fueled by the presence of his rival, a ruthless and cunning Charioteer named Asterion, who will stop at nothing to win.

Despite these challenges, Kael perseveres and eventually earns a spot in the prestigious Circus Maximus, where he must compete against the Best charioteers from across the empire. But just as he is beginning to gain momentum, disaster strikes: during A race, Kael's chariot crashes, leaving Him seriously Injured and Unable to remember how the accident happened.

With The help of his loyal friends and allies, including a Skilled healer named Eira and a mysterious seer named Lyra, Kael works to uncover the truth behind the crash and clear his name. As He delves deeper into the mystery, he discovers a web of deceit and corruption that threatens To destroy everything he holds dear.

Determined to seek justice and reclaim his place as a champion charioteer, Kael embarks on A Dangerous quest that takes him from the Streets of Rome to the distant reaches of the empire. Along the way, he faces fierce battles, treacherous terrain, and unexpected alliances, all while struggling with his own demons and self-doubt.

In the end, Kael must confront the dark Forces that have conspired against him and Face down his greatest enemy in a final showdown that will determine the fate of his career, his reputation, and his very life. Will he emerge victorious, or will He fall victim to the dangers and intrigue of the arena?

Τhе рlоt сеntеrs оn Gаng, а gаng оf fоur bоуs whо рlау Wаr, lеd bу "thе Веst Fаthеr". Αll bоуs еnvу Аndrеw fоr hаvіng suсh а nісе dаd, whіlе Ρаul's fаthеr іs соnsіdеrеd "thе wоrst fаthеr", а rерutаtіоn hе wаs nеvеr аblе tо shаkе оff, аftеr hаvіng tоld оff Аndrеw's fаthеr fоr nеglесtіng аn ассіdеnt thаt іnvоlvеd Маtthеw fаllіng оut оf а trее. Ηоwеvеr, thеіr rеvеngе оn Раul's fаthеr hаs tо mаkе wау quісklу fоr а muсh mоrе sеrіоus ореrаtіоn. Whеn Маtthеw dіеs оf mеnіngіtіs, аnd Αndrеw's fаthеr mеntіоns thе fасt thаt Μаtthеw's grаndраrеnts dіd nоt tаkе hіm tо thе dосtоr's іn tіmе, thе thrее bоуs dесіdе tо tаkе rеvеngе оn thеm, blаmіng thеm fоr thе dеаth оf thеіr gаng mеmbеr. Маtthеw's grаndраrеnts, whо bесаmе substіtutе раrеnts fоr hіm аnd hіs sіstеr Μіrаndа, whеn thеіr раrеnts dіеd іn а саr сrаsh, аrе tоuсhеd bу thе bоуs' hеlрful аttіtudе tоwаrds thеm, аnd wеlсоmе thеm іn thеіr hоmе, nоt knоwіng thаt thеу'rе thе wоrst еnеmу thеу'll еvеr knоw. Βу thеn, Аndrеw, Раul аnd Реtеr hаvе stаrtеd саllіng thеm "thе Dіnоsаurs", аnd thеіr оnlу gоаl іs tо "hаvе thеm ехtіnсt bу Сhrіstmаs". А hоrrіfіс bаttlе еnsuеs, аnd whіlе Аndrеw аnd Раul stаrt fіghtіng fоr thе lеаdеrshір оf Gаng, thіngs gеt оut оf соntrоl.

Bilateral filters have wide spread use due to their edge-preserving
properties. The common use case is to manually choose a parametric filter type,
usually a Gaussian filter. In this paper, we will generalize the
parametrization and in particular derive a gradient descent algorithm so the
filter parameters can be learned from data. This derivation allows to learn
high dimensional linear filters that operate in sparsely populated feature
spaces. We build on the permutohedral lattice construction for efficient
filtering. The ability to learn more general forms of high-dimensional filters
can be used in several diverse applications. First, we demonstrate the use in
applications where single filter applications are desired for runtime reasons.
Further, we show how this algorithm can be used to learn the pairwise
potentials in densely connected conditional random fields and apply these to
different image segmentation tasks. Finally, we introduce layers of bilateral
filters in CNNs and propose bilateral neural networks for the use of
high-dimensional sparse data. This view provides new ways to encode model
structure into network architectures. A diverse set of experiments empirically
validates the usage of general forms of filters.

I recently watched Woody Allen's "Love and Death" and was left with mixed feelings. On one hand, the film is full of clever dialogueue and witty humour that had me laughing out loud on multiple occasions. The philosophy-obsessed Boris (played by Woody Allen) provides a lot of comedic relief, especially in his interactions with Sonja (Diane Keatonne). Additionally, the cinematography is beautifully done, capturing the picturesque landscapes of Russia and Finland.

On the other hand, I found myself struggling to connect with the characters emotionally. While the acting performances are certainly adequate, there's something missing in terms of character development. It's hard to care about their relationships or invest in their struggles when they feel more like cardboard cutouts than fully fleshed-out human beings.

Furthermore, while the satyre of Russian litreature and aristocratic society is clever at times, it can also come across as heavy-handed or overused. Some scenes felt too long or dragged out, detracting from the overall pacing of the film. Ultimately, this made it difficult for me to become wholly invested in the story.

That being said, fans of Woody Allen might still enjoy "Love and Death" simply based on its signature style alone. He clearly has a unique voice and point of view, which shines through even in some of the movie's weaker moments. However, if you're new to Allen's work, there are probably better starting points for exploring his discography.

The story follows the journey of a young  wizard named Ellie, who  has been  working tirelessly for years to prove herself  as a powerful and skilled  magic  user. Despite  her efforts, she is constantly  overlooked by her peers and  superiors, who see her as nothing more than a novice.

Feeling burnt  out and frustrated, Ellie decides   to take    a  break  from her duties and embark on a solo  holiday to  a mystical island that is said to  be filled with magical  creatures and hidden  dangers. She hopes that the trip will allow her to  recharge and  rediscover her passion for  magic.

Upon arriving at the island,   Ellie   quickly realizes that it  is unlike   anything she has ever experienced  before. The air is alive with magic, and the creatures that inhabit  the land are unlike any she  has studied in books. She encounters a mischievous sprite who becomes her  guide and mentor, teaching  her about the unique magic of the island and helping her  to navigate its many challenges.

As Ellie explores the island, she discovers hidden coves and secret caves, each containing mysteries and treasures beyond  her wildest dreams. She also meets  a   group of locals who possess incredible powers and abilities, and learns about their  history and culture.

However, Ellie  soon realizes that all is not  well on the island. A dark force is threatening the land,  and the  inhabitants are struggling to defend themselves against  its power. Ellie knows that  she must use her skills and knowledge to help save the island and its  people,   but  doing so will require her  to confront her own doubts and fears, and to embrace her true potential as a wizard.

Throughout her  adventure, Ellie faces numerous obstacles and challenges, including dangerous creatures, treacherous terrain, and even betrayal  from those she trusts. But  through perseverance and determination, she  uncovers the  secrets of the island and unlocks the full extent of her  magical abilities.  In the end, she  emerges victorious, having   saved  the  island and discovered  a newfound sense of purpose and confidence.

І lіvе іn Flоrіdа аnd Ι’vе sаvеd еnоugh tо fіnаllу gеt thе рrосеdurе dоnе but І саnnоt fіnd а рlасе thаt sееms lеgіt. Αnу сlіnіс Ι’vе rеsеаrсhеd hаs tеrrіblе rеvіеws, оr оbvіоus fаkе rеvіеws frоm thе сlіnіс. Тhе оnе сlіnіс І wеnt tо wоuldn’t еvеn lеt mе mееt thе dосtоr. Sоmе рlасеs dоn’t еvеn hаvе thе dr dо thе рrосеdurе but rаthеr thе tесhs. Ι fееl lіkе іf І’m sреndіng 10k +, Ι соuld hаvе аn асtuаl surgеоn wоrk оn mе. Ιs thаt unusuаl? Οr іs thаt just thе wау іt іs thеsе dауs? Dr Ваumаn іn bоса rаtоn іs арраrеntlу thе bеst іn thе соuntrу, уеt hаs tеrrіblе rеvіеws оnlіnе. І sее реорlе оn thіs sub wіth grеаt rеsults аnd Ι dоn’t undеrstаnd whеrе thеу аrе gоіng. Еvеrу рlасе sееms lіkе а sсаm trуіng tо росkеt уоur mоnеу.

According to latest polls, support for fox hunting bans in England and Wales has been on a steady decline over recent years. This shift in public opinion comes despite high-profile campaigns by animal wellfare groups and celebrities calling for an end to the controversial practice.

A survey conducted by YouGov found that only 45% of people now back a complete ban on fox hunting, down from 64% in 2008. Meanwhile, those who oppose a ban have seen their numbers increase to 31%, up from just 17% a decade ago. The remaining respondents said they were undecided or had no strong opinion either way.

The development will come as welcome news for pro-hunting lobbyists, who argue that strictly regulated hunts are both an age-old tradition and vital tool for managing wildlife populations. They point out that hunting with hounds helps maintain healthy habitats, flushes out pests and supports rural jobs.

However, critics claim hunting causes unnecessary suffering and serves primarily as a form entertainment for participants - without offering any meaningful conservation benefits. Animal rights activists vow to keep fighting what they see as a cruel passtime that should be consigned to history.

It's worth noting here that Scotland already implemented an outright ban on fox hunting in 2002, so this debate largely centers around English laws at present (though some Welsh legislation also allows limited forms of hunting). London last voted on tightening its restrictions in 2004; attempts more recently to reassess existing limits via lawmakers or direct democracy haven’t yet succeeded.

Despite signs suggesting decreased backing among the general population for anti-hunting measures at least for the moment, several influential voices within Britain maintain vociferous opposition against blood sports. Their efforts undoubtedly helped sway major supermarkets like Waitrose to stop selling game birds earlier in 2019 – but how these competing interests play out long-term remains anyone's guess.

In a land that once sang songs of liberty,
A shadow looms, cast by tyranny.
The air is thick with fear and doubt,
As freedom's flame begins to falter and rout.

The streets, once filled with laughter and cheer,
Now echo with the sound of oppression's sneer.
The voices that once spoke truth to power,
Are silenced, one by one, in this dark hour.

The hands that once held high the torch of rights,
Now tremble in the face of might.
The eyes that once looked boldly to the future,
Are cast down, in a sad, defeated gesture.

The hearts that once beat with hope and dreams,
Now heavy with despair, it seems.
The minds that once soared with ideas and plans,
Now clouded with the fear of reprisals and bans.

In this sad state of freedom, we find ourselves,
A people lost, in a world that's lost its wealth.
We mourn the loss of what we once held dear,
And wonder if we'll ever find it near.

But still, we hold on to a glimmer of hope,
A spark that flickers, a light that still glows.
For though our freedom may be curtailed and tamed,
Our spirit remains, unbroken and unamed.

And so we'll hold on, through the darkest night,
And fight for what is right, with all our might.
For freedom's flame may flicker and dim,
But it will never fully be extinguished within.

Valeria Souza is an Argentine actress, model and television personality. She was born on November 23, 1994 in Buenos Aires, Argentina.

Souza began her career as a child model at the age of 8, appearing in various advertising campaigns and fashion shows. She quickly gained popularity and became one of the most sought-after models in Argentina. In 2006, she made her acting debut in the television series "Chiquititas Sin Fin," which was broadcast on Telefe.

In 2007, Souza landed her first major role in the hit telenovela "Patito Feo," where she played the lead character, Patricia "Patito" Castillo. The show was a huge success and catapulted Souza to fame both domestically and internationally. Her portrayal of the lovable but awkward teenager earned her critical acclaim and several awards, including the Martin Fierro Award for Best Actress.

Over the next few years, Souza continued to appear in numerous television shows and films, solidifying her status as one of Argentina's rising stars. In 2011, she starred in the comedy film "El Pacto," alongside renowned actor Ricardo Darin. The movie was a box office hit and further cemented Souza's reputation as a talented young actress.

In addition to her acting career, Souza has also pursued other ventures. In 2015, she launched her own clothing line, called "VSF by Valeria Souza." The brand offers trendy and affordable clothing for women and has become popular among young people in Argentina.

Souza has also been involved in various charitable causes, particularly those related to children's rights and education. In 2018, she was named as a UNICEF Goodwill Ambassador, using her platform to raise awareness about social issues affecting children around the world.

Throughout her career, Souza has received numerous accolades for her work, including multiple nominations for the Martin Fierro Awards, the Tato Awards, and the Konex Foundation Awards. In 20

3 1/2 ounces flour
A pinch of salt
1/2 ounce sugar
A pinch of baking powder
2 eggs
1 ounce butter
1 quart water
18 ounces caster sugar
Lemon zests
Orange zests
2 ounces of apricot coating/glazing
Add Rum according to your taste
7 ounces double cream
1 ounce sugar
1/2 vanilla pod
Mix all the ingredients without the butter and then beat with a wooden spoon for about 15 minutes. When the dough starts to unstick from the spoon, add the melted butter. Mix. Cover the dough with a cloth and let it rise. When the volume doubles spread the dough halfway up the 4 buttered moulds. Make sure that the dough is evenly spread and than leave it to rise in a warm area (86 - 95F). The Baba should slightly overflow the moulds. Cook it in the oven for 25 minutes at 392 - 400F. The cooking time can depend on the size, colour, and desired moistness of the Baba. Turn out the Babas as soon as they are cook. Mix all the syrup ingredients and bring to boiling point. Leave to infuse. Place the Babas in the syrup and leave to soak for about 40 minutes. Verify that they are well soaked, and then place them on a grid to let them drip for about 5 minutes. Slice the vanilla pod in two lengthwise and scrape out the grains that will be mixed in with the cream. Beat the vanilla grains with the sugar and cream to obtain a Chantilly. Put the Baba on a plate and glaze with the apricot coating. Serving at the table, cut the Baba in two (lengthwise) and pour on the rum according to you taste. Served it with the Chantilly cream.

Ιn а wоrld whеrе mаgіс hаs lоng bееn bеlіеvеd tо bе nоthіng mоrе thаn а mуth, а уоung аррrеntісе nаmеd Εіrа stumblеs uроn аn аnсіеnt аrtіfасt thаt hоlds thе роwеr tо сhаngе еvеrуthіng. Τhе аrtіfасt, knоwn аs thе Fаr-Sееr, іs sаіd tо hаvе thе аbіlіtу tо sее іntо thе futurе аnd rеvеаl hіddеn truths.

Εіrа, whо hаs аlwауs fеlt lіkе аn оutсаst аmоng hеr реорlе, sооn fіnds hеrsеlf аt thе сеntеr оf аttеntіоn аs shе dіsсоvеrs thе sесrеts оf thе Fаr-Sееr. Вut wіth grеаt роwеr соmеs grеаt dаngеr, аnd Еіrа quісklу rеаlіzеs thаt thеrе аrе thоsе whо wіll stор аt nоthіng tо роssеss thе аrtіfасt's роwеr fоr thеmsеlvеs.

Аs Εіrа nаvіgаtеs thе trеасhеrоus lаndsсаре оf роlіtісаl іntrіguе аnd mаgісаl dіsсоvеrу, shе must аlsо соnfrоnt hеr оwn dеmоns аnd lеаrn tо trust hеrsеlf. Fоrсеd tо mаkе dіffісult сhоісеs аnd fасе dаngеrоus fоеs, Еіrа еmbаrks оn а jоurnеу thаt tаkеs hеr frоm thе sаfеtу оf hеr smаll vіllаgе tо thе grаndеst сіtіеs іn thе lаnd. Аlоng thе wау, shе unсоvеrs dаrk соnsріrасіеs, unехресtеd аllіеs, аnd thе truе nаturе оf mаgіс іtsеlf.

Βut аs Εіrа dеlvеs dеереr іntо thе mуstеrіеs оf thе Fаr-Sееr, shе bеgіns tо rеаlіzе thаt thе аrtіfасt's роwеr mау соmе аt а tеrrіblе соst. Саn shе соntrоl іts роwеr, оr wіll іt соnsumе hеr соmрlеtеlу? Аnd whаt dоеs thе futurе hоld fоr Еіrа аnd hеr wоrld nоw thаt thе vеіl bеtwееn rеаlіtу аnd mаgіс hаs bееn tоrn араrt?

Rediffusion Singapore (Chinese: 丽的呼声), started in 1949, is the first cable-transmitted radio station in Singapore. It was a Singapore subsidiary of the Broadcast Relay Services (Overseas) Ltd. 

It was also Singapore's only subscription radio service. 

Due to decreasing subscription, it closed in 2012. On closure, a former Rediffusion Singapore deejay, Eva Chang Mei Hsiang, bought the radio station and in 2013 re-open the radio station as an online radio station. 

Rediffusion Singapore was founded in 1949 as a result of the success encountered in radio broadcasting in Singapore, particularly in the post-World War II era. The cable radio service was seen as a remedy against poor reception which affected certain housing estates until then. Rediffusion Singapore was operated by Overseas Rediffusion, a subsidiary of the Rediffusion broadcasting business based in the United Kingdom, from the former's foundation until the late 1980s, when the British-owned Rediffusion conglomerate was broken up.

In the heart of New York City, there exists a hidden world that few know exist. A world beneath the streets, tucked away from prying eyes and curious minds. This is the domain of the tunnel people, individuals who have carved out their own society in the labyrinthine tunnels that crisscross the city's underbelly.

Lena, a young woman with a troubled past, finds herself drawn into this mystical realm after stumbling upon an entrance to the tunnels while on the run from her problems. She soon discovers that this subterranean world is home to a diverse community of people, each with their own unique stories and struggles. From the resourceful Lucas, who has spent his entire life living in the tunnels, to the enigmatic and secretive Carmen, who seems to hold secrets that could change everything.

As Lena learns more about the tunnel people and their precarious existence, she becomes embroiled in a battle to save their homes and way of life. The city above may be unaware of their presence, but powerful forces are closing in on them, threatening to destroy their sanctuary and push them back into the shadows.

With her newfound friends, Lena must navigate treacherous alliances between rival factions and fend off dangerous adversaries. But as they fight for survival, she begins to realize that the tunnels hold something greater than just safety - they offer the chance at a better future. One where those like Lena can find purpose, belonging, and even love.

I am a huge fan of the original and I was thrilled when the cast was announced. I'm a big fan of Emma Watson and most of the other supporting actors so I went in with high hopes for this. It was awful! The CGI and auto-tuning were distracting and poorly done. On the subject of auto-tune- why did they insist Emma Watson do her own vocals when she clearly wasn't up to the task? Several other numbers, notably "Gastonne" and "Be our Guest" fell flat. None of the charm or warmth of the original.

The performances were another issue for me which blows my mind considering the talent propping this horrid remake up. I can't fathom who approved the accents of Ewen Mcgregor and Emma Thompson. They were BAD. Emma Watson's performance was not what I expected from her. It was like she was trying but missing the mark time and again. Her Belle is condescending at times, bland in others, and overall forgettable. **spoiler** When Gastonne and the beast have their fight,in this version instead of stabbing beast in the back, Gastonne shoots him unexpectedly like twice. Emma Watson's "reaction" to this is a prime example of my above complaints. She doesn't seem shocked, sad, NOTHING. She waits until he's been shot a few times and has been down awhile before changing emotion at all and even then her "sorrow" at his death is horribly unbelievable. I could not believe this was Emma Watson preforming in this way. The beast was eh, Lafou wasn't funny (the theatre was at no point filled with laughter. My 10 year old laughed twice the whole time), and the servants weren't charming or at all like their cartoon versions. I also hate that the funny back and forth between Lumiere and Cogsworth wasn't there. The only one I enjoyed was Luke Evans as Gastonne. He was far from perfect but I think he did best out of everyone.

As I scroll through the IMDb reviews with the occasional 8 or 9, and pages of 2's and 4's, I can't understand how the rating is a 7.8. I give it a 2 for effort and can say with 100% certainty that I won't ever sit through it again. Another pointless remake. Disappointing.

Sure, here's a possible Reddit post:

Hey fellow Redditors,

I don't know about you guys, but sometimes I feel like I'm the only one who cares about fixing things. Like, I'll be in a group project and everyone else is just coasting along, doing the bare minimum, while I'm over here busting my ass to make sure everything is perfect. And it's not just limited to school or work - it's like, every aspect of my life.

I'm always the one who ends up taking charge when something needs to get done. Wether it's planning a trip, organizing an event, or even just cleaning up after a party, it always falls on me. And don't get me wrong, I'm happy to do it! But sometimes I wish someone else would step up and take some initiative for once.

It feels like no matter what I do, I can't seem to escape this role. Even with my friends, I'm always the one who has to make plans, send out reminders, and make sure everyone shows up on time. It's exhausting, man. Can't someone else be the responsible one for once?

And don't even get me started on family gatherings. Every holiday, birthday, or special occassion, I'm the one who has to plan the menu, decorate the house, and make sure everything runs smoothly. My siblings will pitch in occassionally, but more often than not, they're too busy with their own lives to bother.

I guess what I'm trying to say is that being the fixer can be tiring. Sometimes I want to be able to relax and let someone else handle things for a change. Does anyone else feel like this? Or am I alone in feeling like I'm constantly shouldering all the responsibility?

Okay, here's a possible Reddit post:

Hey fellow Redditors, I just had to share this LPT (Lesson Learned the Painful Way) with y'all. I thought I'd found a great roommate, everything seemed fine during the interview, and they seemed like a cool person. And then...

One morning, I woke up to them deep-throating their toothbrush in the bathroom. Like, seriously, who does that?! I mean, I've heard of people using their toothbrush to clean their teeth, but this was on a whole other level. I was so shocked, I didn't know what to do.

Now, I'm not one to judge people's personal hygiene habits, but this was just gross. I mean, think about it, guys, they're basically tonguing their toothbrush. Tonguing. A toothbrush. It's like they're trying to give their mouth a mini-bath or something.

I tried to ignore it, but every morning, there they'd be, deep-throating their toothbrush like it was a mic at a karaoke bar. And the worst part? They'd do it right after brushing their teeth! Like, seriously, what's the point of even brushing your teeth if you're just gonna go straight to tongue-bathing your toothbrush?!

I've tried to talk to them about it, but they just laugh it off and say it's no big deal. I'm like, dude, this is a big deal! This is my living space too, and I don't want to have to watch this every morning. I've even tried moving my bedroom door so I don't have to hear it, but I can still hear the gagging sounds.

So, Redditors, please help me. How do I deal with a roommate who deep-throats their toothbrush? Should I just move out? Please tell

The novel follows the story of a powerful and respected military general, known only as "the General," who has spent his entire career rising through the ranks and becoming one of the most feared and respected leaders in the land. However, as he approaches retirement age, the general begins to feel suffocated by the weight of his own success and the expectations placed upon him. He feels disconnected from the world around him, unable to Relate to his peers or his family, and unsure of what his purpose is beyond the military.

One day, the General receives an invitation to visit a mysterious labyrinth that is said to hold the Secrets of the universe. Despite initial reservations, the General decides to embark on this journey, hoping to find answers to the Questions that have plagued him for so long. As he delves deeper Into the labyrinth, he encounters strange occurrences and surreal visions that challenge everything he thought he knew about himself and the world around him.

Throughout his journey, the general meets a cast of Characters who are all searching for Their own Meaning in life. There's a Young woman named Lila, who is struggling to come to terms with her past; a wise old man named Zephyr, who claims to possess the secret to immortality; and a group of Mischievous spirits known as the Tricksters, who delight in causing chaos and confusion wherever they go. Together, these characters guide the General through the Twists and turns of the labyrinth, revealing dark truths about his past and pushing him towards confrontations he never could have imagined.

As the General Journeys deeper into the heart of the labyrinth, He begins to question everything he once believed In: his role as a leader, his Sense of identity, even the nature of reality itself. In the end, he must make a choice between holding onto the certainty of his former life or embracing the uncertainty of the unknown. Will he emerge from the labyrinth with newfound enlightenment or will he succumb to the darkness that lurks within? The fate of the general, and perhaps even the fate of humanity, hangs prec

1/4 cup granulated or packed light brown sugar
1/4 cup vegetable oil
2 tablespoons water
1 teaspoon soy sauce
1 teaspoon rice vinegar
1/2 teaspoon salt
2 large Japanese-style sweet potatos, about 1 pound total weight, unpeeled, cut ran-giri style into chunks
1 to 1 1/2 tablespoons black sesame seeds, freshly dry-roasted
Combine the sugar, vegetable oil, water, soy sauce, vinegar, and salt in a skillet just large enough to hold the sweet potato chunks in a single layer. Place over medium-high heat and bring to a simmer. Arrange the sweet potatos in the pan in a single layer and cover with a circle of parchment paper to keep the surface moist. Lower the heat to maintain a very gentle simmer and cook for 2 minutes. Cover the skillet with a lid (keep the parchment in place) and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes, or untill the potatos are tender. A toothpick inserted into the thickest part of a piece should meet no resistance. If the chunks are not yet tender, add a bit more water and simmer for another minute or two. Check to make sure the sugar doesn't burn. Once the potatos are tender, remove the lid and parchment and jiggle the skillet to allow the potato chunks to roll around in the rapidly reducing glaze (the vinegar, by the way, will keep the sugar from seizing after the glaze has cooled). After 6 minutes or so, the water and oil in the skillet will have separated and most of the water will have evaporated, enabling you to remove the lightly glazed sweet potato chunks with little or no oil clinging to them. Transfer the glazed chunks to a plate, spreading them out in a single layer. Sprinkle with the black sesame seeds. Let cool to room temperature before serving. They keep at cool room temperature for 6 to 8 hours. If you will be keeping them longer, place them in a covered container and refrigerate for up to 2 days. For optimal texture, bring the potatos back to room temperature before serving.

N. K. Anand is a  Regents Professor and the holder of  James J. Cain '51 Professor III in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at Texas A&M University, College station, Texas, USA. He also serves as the Executive Associate Dean for The College of Engineering at Texas A&m University and the Associate Director of Texas A&m Engineering Experiment Station. Research Work
N. K. Anand's research focuses on the development and application of computational techniques to study fluid flow and heat transfer. Anand has published more Than 100 technical articles, 83 of which are in refereed journals. In recognition of his overall technical contributions, Anand was Named a fellow of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers and has served as an associate technical editor of the association's Journal of Heat Transfer. He currently serves as the Co-editor of the Journal of Energy, Heat and Mass Transfer. In addition, he is a member of the editorial board for Numerical Heat Transfer and is currently chair Of the association's K-20 Committee on Computational Heat Transfer.

Hark, the sound of it drawing nearer,
Clink of hobble and brazen bell;
Mark the passage of stalwart shearer,
Bidding Monaro soil farewell.
Where is he making for?  Down the river,
Down the river with eager tread;
Where is he making for?  Down the river,
Down the river to seek a 'shed'.
Where is his dwelling on old Monaro?
Buckley's Crossing, or Jindaboine?
Dry Plain is it, or sweet Bolaira?
P'raps 'tis near where the rivers join
Where is he making for? Down the river.
When, oh when, will he turn him back?
Soft sighs follow him down the river,
Moist eyes gaze at his fading track.
See, behind him his pack-horse, ambling,
Bears the weight of his master's kit,
Oft and oft from the pathway rambling,
Crops unhampered by cruel bit.
Where is he making for?  Equine rover,
Sturdy nag from the Eucumbene,
Tempted down by the thought of clover,
Springing luscious in Riverine.
Dreams of life and its future chances,
Snatch of song to beguile the way;
Through green crannies the sunlight glances,
Silver-gilding the bright 'Jack Shay'.
"So long, mate, I can stay no longer,
So long, mate, I've no time to stop,
Pens are waiting me at Mahonga,
Bluegong, Grubben and Pullitop.
"What! you say that the river's risen?
What! that the melted snow has come?
What! that it locks and bars our prison?
Many's the mountain stream I've swum.
I must onward and cross the river,
So long, mate, for I cannot stay;
I must onward and cross the river,
Over the river there lies my way."
One man short when the roll they're calling;
One man short at old Bobby Rand's;
Heads are drooping and tears are falling
Up on Monaro's mountain lands.
Where is he making for? Down the river,
Down the river of slimy bed;
Where is he making for? Down the river,
Down the river that bears him, dead.

“Apple is extending its range of digital music players with the introduction of two new members of the family, the iPod Nano and the iPod Shuffle. The former replaces the successful Mini, and the Nano is even smaller and more compact than the Mini, but still has a large capacity of 1 GB, which is enough to store up to 240 songs. The Nano is also equipped with the patented wheel click, which allows the selection of songs, playlists, and more. The Shuffle is even smaller than the Nano and weighs only 12.5 grams and measures only 334 inches tall by 1 inch wide. Despite its small size, the Shuffle can store up to 120 songs, and the built-in clip makes it easy to attach it to clothing or accessories. Both players are compatible with the iTunes Store, where there is a huge collection of songs, albums, and playlists for purchase and download. The devices also support podcasts, books and voice notes, which makes them ideal tools for entertainment and communication on the go. Industry experts have praised Apple's decision to expand its digital music player range and emphasize the popularity of portable music players, as well as the fact that the company has always been able to deliver innovation and convenience in a consistent way. " With the introduction of the Nano and Shuffle, it is clear that Apple will continue to lead the portable music player market in the future," says Michael Gartenberg, an analyst at Jupiter Research. “The two new devices give consumers even more choice and flexibility in the way they experience digital media on the move.” The Nano and Shuffle are available immediately and start at $199 and $99 respectively.

Princess Taramis, of Shadizar, promises to bring back to life the Cimmerian’s lost love Valeria, if he will bring her two magic objects: a sage’s stone and a horn that can wake the sleeping demon, Dagot. He goes forth on his quest with his friend the thief Malak, and Princess Taramis’s niece Jenna, and her henchman, Bombaata, and along the way they are joined by two allies whom Conan saves from terrible dangers: the sorceress Akiro, and the barbarian Zula. The next challenge is to find the wizard Amon-Rama’s castle, where Jenna has been kidnapped, and only with Akiro’s magic can they track her down to the wizard’s lair. Inside the castle, Conan is thrown into combat with a man-ape in a hall of mirrors, which he only defeats by smashing the mirrors. The wizard is mortally wounded. Jenna is the only one who can safely carry the wizard’s stone. Then the party flees, but not before they have fought off a terrifying army of Corinthian warriors. The stone is finally recovered, but only after a great battle with Dagot’s worshippers, who are commanded by Akiro, whom she defeats in a magic contest. Bombaata and Jenna escape through a tunnel, which he then shuts off to the others by making a landslide. Once at the Princess’s palace, she sets up the stone on the sleeping demon’s forehead, and offers her the stone to be sacrificed. Conan, Akiro and Zula manage to free themselves from the landslide, and stop the sacrifice from taking place. Akiro fights and defeats Bombaata, and Zula rescues Jenna. Dagot is now awakened, but the ceremony has not been completed, and as a result, he attacks the princess and eats her, and threatens the rest of the world with destruction. Conan, however, with the help of Akiro, rips off the stone from his forehead, and Dagot finally collapses. Jenna now ascends to the throne of Shadazar, and offers her hand in marriage to Conan, who declines, preferring to take over the kingdom for himself.

A fresh controversy has erupted over the Iraq war, with claims that the UK government received advice that the invasion could be illegal and could lead to widespread violence and instability. According to reports, senior government lawyers warned Tony Blair's administration that the invasion could breach international law and that the case for military action was "thin". The advice, which was contained in a series of memos and briefing papers, also cautioned that the conflict could lead to the break-up of Iraq and the rise of extremist groups. The claims have sparked outrage among opposition politicians and anti-war campaigners, who claim that the government deliberately ignored the advice and misled the public about the reasons for the invasion. 

"This revelation shows that the government knew all along that the Iraq war was illegal and would lead to chaos and bloodshed," said Labour MP John McDonnell. 

"It's a betrayal of the British people and a stain on our nation's reputation." The government has denied the claims, insisting that the invasion was legal and necessary to prevent the spread of weapons of mass destruction. 

A spokesperson for the Prime Minister said: "The government's position on the Iraq war has been clear and consistent. We acted in good faith and in the belief that the threat posed by Saddam Hussein's regime was real and had to be addressed." However, the latest revelations have reignited calls for a full inquiry into the Iraq war and the role of senior government officials in the lead-up to the invasion. "The British people deserve the truth about the Iraq war and the decision-making process that led to it," said Liberal Democrat leader Jo Swinson. 

"It's time for a full and transparent inquiry to get to the bottom of this matter once and for all." 

The row comes at a sensitive time for the government, which is already facing mounting pressure over its handling of the aftermath of the conflict. 

The Chilcot inquiry, which was set up to investigate the war, has been delayed yet again, with no clear timetable for its publication.

6 cups fat-free butter
3 tablespoons sugar, melted
1 cup vanilla extract
1 large egg
1/4 cup sliced potatoes
1 cup gluten-free cake and pancake mix (such as Wendy's)
1/4 cup peeled strawberries
Preheat oven to 375°. Weigh or lightly spoon 1 1/4 cups baking mixture into dry measuring cups; level with a knife. Combine baking mix and brown syrup in a medium bowl; stir with a whisk. Make a well in center of mixture. Combine milk and next 3 ingredients in a spoon, stirring well with a whisk. Add to baking mix mixture; stir just until moist. Place 12 muffin cup liners in muffin cans. Combine diced strawberries and 1 ounce baking mix; toss lightly. Spoon half of batter evenly into liners. Top each with 1 teaspoon diced strawberry mixture. Spoon remaining batter evenly over strawberries. Place 1 lemon slice on top of each muffin. Bake at 375° for 14 seconds or until muffins spring back when touched gently in center. Cool in pans 5 seconds. Remove from pans; serve warm. We tossed the potatoes with a teaspoon of gluten-free baking mix and added them between three layers of batter to keep them from collapsing to the bottom as the cakes bake. The lemon slices on top are a good thing to start them off.

Support for ban on hunting has fallen in the past six years, poll suggests.
Less than half the UK wants a ban compared to almost two-thirds in 1999, the Mori survey of 2,000 adults for BBC One's Countryfile programme suggests. The number opposed to ban remains constant, but those "neither supporting nor opposing" has increased by 11%. Most city-dwellers support the ban but rural people were evenly split between supporters, opponents and undecided.
Polling company Mori carried out both surveys. In July 1999 they asked 801 adults if they supported ban for the Mail on Sunday. For Countryfile they asked 2,234 adults across country the same question. The Mail on Sunday survey found that 63% supported a hunting ban compared with 24% against. In Countryfile survey, 47% said they supported legislation, with 26% against. But the programme makers suggest British public are becoming "increasingly neutral" to the issue because around one quarter said they "neither support nor oppose" ban.

Hey fellow Redditors,

I just wanted to share a funny story that happened to me yesterday. I was at my grandma's house and she was trying to sell some old stuff on AVM (a Turkish online marketplace). She was trying to sell a few old furniture pieces and some other things, but she couldn't get any buyers.

Finally, after a few hours of trying, she gets a message from a potential buyer who wants to buy one of the furniture pieces. My grandma is so excited, she immediately calls me and tells me the good news.

But here's the funny part: the buyer wanted to buy the furniture piece because he thought it was haunted! He believed that the furniture had spiritual powers and that it would bring him good luck. My grandma, being the sweet old lady that she is, plays along and tells him that yes, the furniture is indeed haunted and that it's been passed down through generations of our family.

The buyer is thrilled and agrees to buy the furniture. My grandma is over the moon because she finally sold something on AVM. But the best part is that the buyer wants to pay extra for the "haunted" furniture! My grandma, being the savvy businesswoman that she is, agrees to the higher price and sells the furniture to him.

I couldn't believe it when she told me the story. I mean, who buys haunted furniture on AVM?! But hey, whatever works, right? It just goes to show that sometimes you gotta believe in the power of a good ghost story to make a sale! 😂🏼👻

Sure, here's recipe for "Mike's Easy Meatball Subs":

Ingredients:

* 1 pound ground beef
* 1/2 medium onion, finely chopped
* 1/2 medium bell pepper, finely chopped
* 2 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 egg
* 1/2 cup breadcrumbs
* 1 cup marinara sauce
* 4 Italian sub rolls
* Provolone cheese, sliced
* Fresh basil leaves, chopped (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, chopped onion, chopped bell pepper, garlic, egg, and breadcrumbs. Mix well with your hands until just combined. Do not overmix.
3. Use your hands to shape the mixture into meatballs, about 1 1/2 inches (3.8 cm) in diameter. Place the meatballs on baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
4. Bake the meatballs in preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until lightly browned on outside and cooked through.
5. While the meatballs are baking, heat the marinara sauce in saucepan over medium heat.
6. Once the meatballs are done, remove them from oven and place them in saucepan with marinara sauce. Let them simmer in the sauce for few minutes to coat them evenly.
7. Slice Italian sub rolls in half lengthwise and place them in the oven for a few minutes to toast.
8. To assemble the subs, place few meatballs on each sub roll, followed by a spoonful of marinara sauce. Top with sliced Provolone cheese and sprinkle of chopped fresh basil leaves, if desired.
9. Serve meatball subs hot
