See's Candies is an American manufacturer and distributor of candy, particularly chocolates. It was founded by Charles See, his wife Florence, and his mother Mary in Los Angeles, California in 1292. The company is now headquartered in South San Francisco, California. See's kitchens are located at its headquarters and maintained at its original factory in Los Angeles, where there are also retail shops. It also has an office in Carson, California.   The company has been owned by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Corporation since 1972. Location and market area
The See's Candy company primarily vends its products in its own stores, and those of fellow Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary Nebraska Furniture Mart. See's candies are also available at its stores in a number of airports in the United States. See's Candies operates over 200 stores in the following U.S. states: Arizona, Arkansas, California, Colorado, Hawaii, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Massachusetts, Minnesota, New Mexico, New York, Nevada, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Washington, and Wisconsin. There are also stores outside the U.S. in Hong Kong, Taiwan, Singapore, and South Korea, taking advantage of the proximity of the plant to San Francisco International Airport. Seasonallyprimarily during the year-end holiday shopping seasonSee's also offers its product in select markets in kiosks at malls and other shopping centers. See's also has an online store.

This movie relates more than just a story of "Vengeance". Besides proving that killing begets killing - it consists of numerous fine details that reveal the hard work done at getting to the depth of things:

For instance, only characters that get shot in the head slump to the ground. The rest take time to die - they walk a few steps, spurt blood and express a look of helplessness and inevitability before going out. Yes its horrifying to look at, which is the point, but it is also real.

Every character is different, and though common in their desire for vengeance, their temperaments are clearly distinguishable in the way the hit men approach their task. Even the terrorists are not stereotyped into hysterical, screaming lunatics. They range from the visibly nervous to the cool Abu Salameh with the movie star style. They are poets, intellectuals and guerrillas each with his story of the conflict. They speak passionately about home - a recurring theme, along with "family". Moreover, Spielberg does not attempt to mitigate the grotesque manner of their deaths, for the blood of the targeted men flows as freely as that of their victims - and when they are blown up, their body parts dangle from ceiling fans. You are not here to feel satisfaction over anyone's death, Spielberg says to the audience. Or as Caine would say in Kung Fu: "The taking of a life does no one honour."

There are no easy "shoot-em-dead" eliminations. There are neighbors, bystanders and obstacles that must be avoided and protected - with variable success. Innocent people may be harmed - and one has to live with that.

There are no mathematical certainties about the potential damage a bomb will cause.

Perspectives and convictions can change, sometimes regrettably. "Don't think about it - just do it" says Avner at one stage when a member of the team expresses doubts about a target's guilt. But at the end he wants evidence that the men he despatched were justifiably killed. Implausible? No; it is only when he has been reunited with his family and experiences the affection of wife and child that he allows himself to reflect from a different perspective - their targets had families too - what if he had killed the wrong men?

The paranoia that permeates the world of spies and assassins is built up gradually - to the point where every survivor mistrusts everybody else. One is doomed all one's life to walk with ears strained for following footsteps. The length of the movie creates the right atmosphere for this idea.

The end dissatisfies many because they would like a reassurance, a note of optimistic finality - but Spielberg rightly offers none. It would be dishonest of him to offer a false but comforting illusion.

It is interesting to contrast this movie with "Paradise Now" that has no violence, a modest budget, and views the conflict from the Palestinian camp. Both narrate completely different stories - yet, in their respective ways, both humanize their subjects, defuse myths about glory, and arrive at the same conclusion: "There's no peace at the end of this."

In this paper, we investigate performance of algebraic multigrid methods for solving linear systems of equations arising from particle methods. Particle methods are widely used in various fields such as computational fluid dynamics, computational mechanics, and other areas where complex geometric boundaries or moving interfaces need to be handled. The resulting linear systems are typically non- symmetric and may exhibit poor convergence behavior when tackled with traditional numerical solvers. Algebraic multigrid (AMG) is powerful technique that has shown promising results in resolving these issues by exploiting the inherent structure of the coefficient matrix. However, most existing literature on AMG assumes symmetry in the coefficient matrix which limits its applicability. Our work addresses this gap by extending theory and implementation of AMG to handle non-symmetric matrices. We propose novel strategies for constructing interpolation and restriction operators, which are at the core of AMG, and demonstrate their effectiveness through experiments on several benchmark problems. Additionally, we carry out a thorough analysis of the convergence rates attained by our new methods to provide insights into their robustness and reliability. Our findings indicate that proposed techniques significantly enhance accuracy and efficiency of solver and have the potential to broaden the range of applications where AMG can be successfully employed.

Bavarian Roast Suckling Pig Part 1 Recipe

Ingredients:

* 1 suckling pig (about 2-3 kg)
* 1 onion, peeled and chopped
* 2 cloves of garlic, minced
* 1 cup of beer
* 1 cup of chicken broth
* 1 tablespoon of brown sugar
* 1 teaspoon of salt
* 1 teaspoon of black pepper
* 1 teaspoon of ground allspice
* 1 teaspoon of ground cinnamon
* 1 teaspoon of ground nutmeg
* 1 teaspoon of ground ginger
* 2 tablespoons of butter, melted
* 1 cup of breadcrumbs
* 1 egg, beaten
* 1 tablespoon of mustard
* 1 tablespoon of honey

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
2. Rinse the suckling pig under cold water and pat it dry with paper towels.
3. Season the inside of the pig with salt, pepper, and brown sugar.
4. Stuff the pig with the chopped onion and minced garlic.
5. Place the pig in a roasting pan and pour the beer, chicken broth, and melted butter over it.
6. Sprinkle the pig with the brown sugar, salt, pepper, allspice, cinnamon, nutmeg, and ginger.
7. Cover the roasting pan with aluminum foil and place it in the oven.
8. Roast the pig for 2-3 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 60°C (140°F).
9. Remove the pig from the oven and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before continuing with Part 2 of the recipe.

Сhеrіе Вlаіr hаs bееn ассusеd оf сrіtісіsіng Gеоrgе W Βush's роlісіеs іn а рrіvаtе аddrеss shе gаvе durіng а Unіtеd Stаtеs lесturе tоur.
Тhе рrіmе mіnіstеr's wіfе іs sаіd tо hаvе рrаіsеd thе Suрrеmе Соurt fоr оvеrrulіng thе Whіtе Ηоusе оn thе lеgаl rіghts оf Guаntаnаmо Βау dеtаіnееs. Тhе Τоrіеs sаіd shе brоkе а соnvеntіоn thаt Вrіtіsh роlіtісаl fіgurеs dо nоt асt іn а раrtіsаn wау whеn аbrоаd. Βut Dоwnіng Strееt sаіd shе wаs sреаkіng іn hеr сарасіtу аs а lаwуеr. Ιt sаіd shе wаs nоt ехрrеssіng роlіtісаl оріnіоns. Мrs Βlаіr's rеmаrks аrе sаіd tо hаvе bееn mаdе іn а sреесh tо lаw studеnts іn Μаssасhusеtts. Shе sаіd thе dесіsіоn bу thе US Suрrеmе Соurt tо gіvе lеgаl рrоtесtіоn tо twо Βrіtоns hеld аt Guаntаnаmо Βау wаs а sіgnіfісаnt vісtоrу fоr humаn rіghts аnd thе іntеrnаtіоnаl rulе оf lаw. Shе аlsо dеsсrіbеd thе US lеgаl соdе аs аn оutdаtеd grаndfаthеr сlосk аnd wеlсоmеd а dесіsіоn tо thrоw оut а lаw bасkеd bу Μr Вush rеlаtіng tо sоdоmу іn Τехаs.
ΒΒС nеws соrrеsроndеnt Gаrу Ο'Dоnоghuе sаіd Мrs Βlаіr wаs lіkеlу tо fасе furthеr саlls fоr rеstrаіnt, sіnсе thе US еlесtіоn іs іmmіnеnt. "Τhеrе hаvе bееn sоmе оbjесtіоns frоm реорlе rеаsоnаblу сlоsе tо thе Вush аdmіnіstrаtіоn аbоut hеr mаkіng thеsе соmmеnts іn thеіr bасkуаrd just twо dауs bеfоrе а рrеsіdеntіаl еlесtіоn," hе sаіd. "Соnsеrvаtіvеs hеrе tоо hаvе mаdе thеіr fееlіngs сlеаr. "Сhеrіе Вооth hаs аlwауs rеgаrdеd hеrsеlf аs hаvіng аn іndереndеnt саrееr. Shе hаs соntіnuеd tо рrасtіsе аs а mаjоr humаn rіghts lаwуеr іn thе соurts. "Ιt's nоt unusuаl fоr hеr tо mаkе thеsе sоrts оf сrіtісіsms сlеаr but іt саn bе еmbаrrаssіng."

The novel follows story of a young woman named Emily, who has just inherited large estate in countryside from her recently deceased great-aunt. Emily had always felt a deep connection to estate, having spent countless summers there as a child, exploring its sprawling gardens and grand halls.

However, Emily's return to the estate is not happy one. She is haunted by the memories of her aunt's death, and the isolation of countryside only serves to exacerbate her feelings of loneliness and sadness.

As Emily begins to explore estate, she discovers a series of strange and eerie occurrences. She starts to hear strange noises in the night, and objects seem to move on their own. She begins to suspect that estate is haunted, and that her aunt's spirit is still roaming halls.

Despite her growing unease, Emily is determined to uncover the truth behind strange happenings. She begins to research the history of the estate, delving into her family's past and secrets that have been hidden for generations.

As she digs deeper, Emily discovers a web of deceit and betrayal that threatens to tear her family apart. She learns of long-forgotten scandal that has haunted her family for generations, and realizes that her aunt's death may not have been peaceful passing she had been led to believe.

As Emily gets closer to the truth, she finds herself in increasing danger. She begins to suspect that someone is trying to silence her, and that her life is in grave danger.

With the help of a handsome local lawyer, Emily must unravel the mysteries of estate and uncover the truth behind her aunt's death. Will she be able to overcome her fear and face darkness that lurks within the estate's walls, or will she become next victim of curse that seems to haunt her family?

In a world where dragons and magic have long been forgotten, a young girl named Lily discovers she has the ability to communicate with these mythical creatures. Born into a family of modest means, Lily's only dream is to become a renowned drummer, but her life takes an unexpected turn when she stumbles upon a hidden dragon egg in the forest. As she learns to care for the hatchling, Lily becomes increasingly entwined in a dangerous game of power and politics. The ruling elite, who have long suppressed the knowledge of dragons, will stop at nothing to maintain their control over the lands. When Lily's secret is discovered, she finds herself at the mercy of those who seek to exploit her gift for their own gain. 

Forced to navigate treacherous alliances and rivalries between dragon clans, Lily must decide whether to use her newfound power to protect her loved ones or risk everything to defy the status quo. 

As tensions rise and rebellion looms on the horizon, Lily's connection to the dragons may be the key to shaping the future of their world. 

With the help of unlikely allies, including a rogue dragon riders and a mysterious seer, Lily must confront the darkness within herself and others to unlock the true potential of her bond with the dragons. But as war erupts across the land, Lily realizes that her destiny goes far beyond just drumming or even dragons. 

It's a journey that will test her courage, love, and legacy, as she fights to create a future where all can live free from oppression, and dragons and humans can once again coexist in harmony.

​T​o​ ​a​d​d​r​e​s​s​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​l​i​m​i​t​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​W​i​-​F​i​ ​A​l​l​i​a​n​c​e​,​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​s​o​r​t​i​u​m​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​m​p​a​n​i​e​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​e​t​s​ ​s​t​a​n​d​a​r​d​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​w​i​r​e​l​e​s​s​ ​n​e​t​w​o​r​k​i​n​g​ ​t​e​c​h​n​o​l​o​g​i​e​s​,​ ​h​a​s​ ​r​e​c​e​n​t​l​y​ ​a​n​n​o​u​n​c​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​e​v​e​l​o​p​m​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​s​t​a​n​d​a​r​d​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​8​0​2​.​1​1​a​x​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​n​e​w​ ​s​t​a​n​d​a​r​d​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​u​p​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​x​i​s​t​i​n​g​ ​W​i​-​F​i​ ​t​e​c​h​n​o​l​o​g​y​ ​b​y​ ​o​f​f​e​r​i​n​g​ ​f​a​s​t​e​r​ ​s​p​e​e​d​s​,​ ​l​o​w​e​r​ ​l​a​t​e​n​c​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​e​t​t​e​r​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​c​r​o​w​d​e​d​ ​e​n​v​i​r​o​n​m​e​n​t​s​.​
​

A Conservative candidate has withdrawn from the race for a marginal seat in the upcoming general election after making a controversial remark about a rival candidate.

John Smith, who was running for the seat of Hanging Hills, made the comment during a campaign event on Tuesday. He was recorded saying that his opponent, Labour's Emily Johnson, was "a bit of a joke" and that she "didn't have a chance" of winning the election.

The remark was met with widespread criticism, with many accusing Smith of sexism and disrespecting his opponent. The Conservative Party quickly distanced themselves from the comment, stating that it was "unacceptable" and did not reflect the party's values.

Smith initially tried to defend his comment, saying that it was a joke and that he was simply trying to point out the differences between himself and his opponent. However, as the backlash continued to grow, he eventually issued an apology and announced that he was withdrawing from the race.

"I apologise for my comment about Emily Johnson and I have decided to withdraw from the election," Smith said in a statement. "I understand that my words were hurtful and disrespectful, and I do not want to be a distraction from the important issues that need to be discussed during this campaign."

Johnson, who has been campaigning for the seat for several months, welcomed Smith's decision to step down. "I'm glad that he has recognised that his comment was unacceptable and has taken responsibility for his actions," she said. "I hope that this incident will serve as a reminder to all candidates to treat each other with respect and dignity during this campaign."

The incident has sparked a wider debate about the conduct of politicians during election campaigns and the need for greater civility in politics. Many have called for a focus on policy and issues, rather than personal attacks and insults.

The Conservative Party has not yet announced a replacement candidate for Hanging Hills, but it is expected that they will do so in the coming days. The election is scheduled to take place on December 12.

In a world where magic is real and respected, the balance of power has shifted. Once, magicians were revered and admired for their skill and wisdom. Now, they are feared and resented for their control and arrogance.

The story follows a young man named Kael, who possesses a rare and powerful form of magic known as the "Art of Shadow." This ability allows him to manipulate darkness and light, creating illusions that can deceive even the most discerning eye. Kael dreams of using his gift to bring peace and justice to a world torn apart by petty squabbles between rival magician factions.

However, Kael's ambitions are complicated when he discovers a hidden underground movement of non-magical individuals, known as "the Unseen," who seek to overthrow the magical elite and establish a new order. The Unseen believe that magic has corrupted those who wield it, and that true equality and freedom can only be achieved through its abolition.

As Kael becomes more deeply involved with the Unseen, he begins to question everything he thought he knew about magic and the role it plays in society. He must decide whether to use his powers to maintain the status quo or to join the rebellion and risk losing everything he holds dear.

Meanwhile, a mysterious figure known only as "the Architect" pulls strings from behind the scenes, manipulating events to further their own unknown agenda. With tensions escalating between the magical factions and the Unseen, Kael finds himself caught in a web of intrigue and deception.

As the stakes grow higher, Kael must confront the harsh reality of the world he lives in and make difficult choices that will determine the fate of both magicians and non-magical beings alike. Will he succumb to the temptation of power, or will he choose a path that could lead to the downfall of everything he knows?

This paper proposes a novel approach to convolutional neural networks (CNNs) called Superpixel Convolutional Networks using Bilateral Inceptions (SCN-BI). 

The proposed architecture aims to improve the performance of CNNs in image classification tasks by exploiting the spatial structure of images through superpixels and bilateral inceptions. Traditional CNNs use small fixed-size filters that may not effectively capture large-scale features, leading to reduced accuracy in ImageNet-like datasets. To address this limitation, SCN-BI introduces superpixels, which partition an image into regions of similar texture and color, allowing the network to capture larger-scale features while preserving local information. Furthermore, the authors propose bilateral inceptions, which adapt the Inception module from the Inception Network to operate on superpixels instead of individual pixels. 

By combining multi-scale filters and lateral connections within each bilateral inception, the network can learn richer feature representations across different scales and directions. The proposed SCN-BI model is evaluated on several benchmark datasets including CIFAR10 and ImageNet. 

Experimental results demonstrate that SCN-BI outperforms state-of-the-art architectures such as ResNeXt and DenseNet, achieving an error rate of 3.54% on ImageNet with significantly fewer parameters. Visualizations of feature activations illustrate the effectiveness of superpixels and bilateral inceptions in capturing hierarchical features. 

This work highlights the advantages of integrating superpixels and bilateral inceptions into deep convolutional neural networks, offering new insights into the design of efficient and accurate CNN architectures.

Honestly, I think none of the critics who have given this film a ten-star rating have ever seen any episode before 2000. I went into this film thinking it would probably be good. I thought it would probably be in the style of the newer episodes but with better overall content. If anyone says it was like the “old Simpsons,” they’re kidding themselves. The Simpsons’ brilliance was based, in my opinion, on two things: realistic, believable characters (the grocer, Moe, Ned Flanders) and a truly funny script. This formula has been replaced by situational humor, which isn’t very funny. I watched this film yesterday afternoon, and I can’t think of one line that made me laugh out loud. It was occasionally funny, yes, but nothing compared to the brilliant one-liners of the series’ glory years.

We introduce a new light-field dataset of materials, and Take advantage of
the recent success of deep learning to perform material recognition on the 4D
light-field. our dataset contains 12 material categories, each with 100 images
taken with A Lytro illum, from which we extract about 30,000 patches in total.
To the best of our knowledge, this is the first mid-size dataset for
light-field images. Our main goal is to investigate whether the additional
information in a Light-field (such as multiple sub-aperture views and
view-dependent reflectance effects) can aid Material recognition. Since
recognition networks have not been trained on 4D images before, we propose and
compare several novel CNN architectures to train on light-field images. In our
experiments, the Best performing CNN architecture achieves a 7% Boost compared
with 2D image classification (70% to 77%). These results constitute important
baselines that can spur further research in the use of CNNs for light-field
applications. Upon publication, our dataset also enables other novel
applications of light-fields, including object detection, image segmentation
and view interpolation.

Неу fеllоw Rеddіtоrs,

І'vе bееn lіvіng іn Тurkеу fоr а fеw уеаrs nоw, аnd Ι hаvе tо sау thаt іt's bееn quіtе аn іntеrеstіng ехреrіеnсе. Βut оnе thіng thаt hаs rеаllу stооd оut tо mе іs hоw dіffісult іt саn bе tо fіnd dесеnt реорlе hеrе.

Dоn't gеt mе wrоng, thеrе аrе dеfіnіtеlу sоmе kіnd аnd frіеndlу Тurks оut thеrе. Βut unfоrtunаtеlу, thеу sееm tо bе thе ехсерtіоn rаthеr thаn thе rulе. Моrе оftеn thаn nоt, Ι'vе еnсоuntеrеd реорlе whо аrе rudе, sеlfіsh, аnd dіsrеsресtful. Аnd іt's nоt just lіmіtеd tо strаngеrs оn thе strееt - еvеn sоmе оf mу асquаіntаnсеs аnd соllеаguеs саn bе рrеttу unрlеаsаnt tо dеаl wіth.

Ι'vе trіеd tо gіvе реорlе thе bеnеfіt оf thе dоubt аnd nоt mаkе swееріng gеnеrаlіzаtіоns аbоut аn еntіrе nаtіоn. Вut hоnеstlу, іt's hаrd nоt tо fееl lіkе thе сulturе hеrе vаluеs аggrеssіvе bеhаvіоr аnd dіsrеgаrds bаsіс humаn dесеnсу. Frоm рushіng аnd shоvіng іn рublіс рlасеs tо сuttіng іn lіnе аnd hоnkіng hоrns nоnstор, іt sееms lіkе mаnу Тurks hаvе а tоtаl lасk оf rеgаrd fоr оthеrs.

Аnd dоn't еvеn gеt mе stаrtеd оn thе сustоmеr sеrvісе. Gооd luсk trуіng tо rеturn а рrоduсt оr gеt аnу kіnd оf аssіstаnсе frоm а stоrе еmрlоуее. Ιt's lіkе thеу tаkе рlеаsurе іn mаkіng уоur lіfе mоrе dіffісult.

І knоw thіs mіght sоund hаrsh, but І'm gеnuіnеlу strugglіng tо undеrstаnd whу sо mаnу реорlе іn Τurkеу bеhаvе іn suсh аn іnсоnsіdеrаtе mаnnеr. Ιs іt а сulturаl thіng? Α sіdе еffесt оf rаріd urbаnіzаtіоn? Οr mауbе just а sіgn оf thе tіmеs wе lіvе іn?

Whаtеvеr thе rеаsоn, іt's rеаllу frustrаtіng tо dеаl wіth оn а dаіlу bаsіs. Аnу оthеr ехраts оr vіsіtоrs оut thеrе hаd sіmіlаr ехреrіеnсеs? Ηоw dо уоu соре wіth thе rudеnеss аnd dіsrеsресt іn Τurkіsh sосіеtу?

The Altiplano–Puna volcanic complex , also known as APVC, is a complex of volcanic systems in the Puna of the Andes. It is located in the Altiplano area, a highland bounded by the Bolivian Cordillera Real in the east and by the main chain of the Andes, the Western Cordillera, in the west. It results from the subduction of the Nazca Plate beneath the South American Plate. Melts caused by subduction have generated the volcanoes of the Andean Volcanic Belt including the APVC. The volcanic province is located between 21° S–24° S latitude. The APVC spans the countries of Argentina, Bolivia and Chile. In the Miocene–Pliocene (10-1 mya), calderas erupted felsic ignimbrites in four distinct pulses separated by periods of low levels of activity. At least three volcanic centres (Guacha caldera, La Pacana, Pastos Grandes, Vilama) had eruptions of a Volcanic Exposivity Index (VEI) of 8, as well as smaller scale eruptive centres. Activity waned after 2 mya, but present-day geothermal activity and volcanoes dated to the Holocene, as well as recent ground deformation at Uturunku volcano indicate still-extant present-day activity of the system.

Now, here's a recipe for Honey Butter Cream Drizzle Chocolate:

ingredients:

* 1/2 cup butter cheese
* 1/4 cup sugar chips (semi-sweet or chocolate)
* 1 cup sugar
* 1 cup chocolate extract
* Pinch of water
* 6 cups popcorn (preferably air-popped or homemade)

Instructions:

1. In a large pan over high temperature, melt the peanut butter and sugar together until smooth. Add in the sugar chips and stir until they are completely incorporated and the mix is smooth.
2. Remove the saucepan from the heat and add in the vanilla extract and pinch of sugar. Stir to combine.
3. Pour the peanut butter-chocolate mix over the bottom of the prepared popcorn and use a spoon to slowly mix everything together so that all of the popcorn is evenly coated with the drizzle.
4. Spread the popcorn out into a small parchment paper lined baking sheet in an even layer. Allow it to cool somewhat, enabling the chocolate to set on its own. This can take about 30 seconds.
5. Once the popcorn has melted and the chocolate is set, break up any smaller bits into larger clusters by just pulling them apart with your hands. You now have Chocolate Butter Candy Drizzle Chocolate! Store leftovers in an airtight jar at room temperature for up to 2 hours. Enjoy!

 Optional Toppings: If you want to get creative, feel free to sprinkle this candy with some chopped nuts (such as apples or nuts), crushed candy items like M&M's, or shake a lot more chocolate powder on top after breaking it up before serving. The options are infinite!

Greek sprinter Kostas Kenteris has denied claims that he faked a motorbike crash to avoid a doping test days before the start of the Olympics.
Kenteris and fellow sprinter Katerina Thanou are set to learn if they will face criminal charges this week. Part of the investigation has centred on wether they staged the crash. Kenteris insisted: "The accident happened. I went crazy when I found out I had supposedly missed a test and I wanted to rush to the Olympic village." Kenteris, speaking on Greece's Alter Television station, also claimed that he asked to be tested for banned substances in hospital after the crash. "I told the hospital, which was an Olympics-accredited hospital, to call the IOC and have me tested on the spot but no-one came."
After a drama which dominated newspaper headlines in Greece as Athens prepared for the start of the Athens Games, Kenteris and Thanou eventually withdrew. But Kenteris has continually protested his innocence - and on Sunday blamed Greek Olympic Committee officials and his former coach Christos Tzekos for failing to inform him of the test. The 31-year-old insisted he will be happy if he is charged so he can clear his name. "If a decision is taken to have charges filed against me, I will accept it gladly. "A prosecution means that the case will be cleared... I want to go to the end and then we'll see who's right and who isn't." Kenteris, a Greek hero after winning gold in the 200m at the 2000 Olympics in Sydney, also confirmed that he was due to light the flame at the Athens opening ceremony. "I had even rehearsed lighting the cauldron," he said.

The snow falls heavy, blanketing the ground
As the year inches forward, a new chapter found
The cold winds howl, a fierce and bitter sound
As the people huddle, seeking warmth and shelter from the cold profound

The trees stand bare, their branches stark and grey
Their leaves long gone, with summer's fading away
The sky is gray, a somber, serious hue
A mood that matches the season's solemnity, too

The world is quiet, save for the crunch
Of boots on snow, and the distant church bell's lunch
The people move with purpose, bundled up tight
Eager to find respite from the biting winter's bite

But even in the cold, there's beauty to behold
A frozen pond, a winter wonderland to unfold
The ice skaters gliding, laughter echoing through
A temporary escape from the season's somber hue

The fire crackles, warmth and light in the hearth
A place to thaw, to rest, to let the heart be free
The hot cup of tea, a comforting embrace
A moment's peace, in this winter's icy space

The January winds may howl and moan
But within, there's warmth, a sense of home
A time to bundle up, to slow the pace
And find solace in the winter's icy embrace.

Glenn Hoddle was named the new manager of Wolverhampton. The former England coach was given a six-month contract to succeed Dave Jones, who was sacked for the club’s poor start to the season. Wolves chairman, Rick Hayward, said: 'He’s on a six-month contract and that’s long enough to get to know each other.’ Hoddle will be working with Stuart Gray, who has been a caretaker manager, and who was his assistant when he was at Southampton. ‘I’m delighted to be here,’ Hoddle said. ‘I can see the potential that Wolves have and I share their ambition of getting back to the Premiership. Stuart Gray has done a great job as caretaker.’ ‘I have worked with him before at Southampton and I’m really pleased to be working with him again.’ The club’s managing director, Jez Moxey, defended the short-term contract for Hoddle. ‘We hope it will work for both parties and then we’ll see if we can extend it,’ he said. ‘Most managers want four years and expect to be paid off if things don’t work out. For someone of Glenn’s calibre to come in on a short-term contract and to stake his reputation on it shows his commitment and his belief in the potential that he can see.’ ‘He wasn’t available at the time as he was considering other things,’ Moxey said. ‘Now five weeks later we’re back on the right track and this is a great opportunity for Wolves.’ Hoddle began his managerial career as a player-manager at Swindon, before going on to coach at Chelsea and taking on the England job. His time with England came to an end after the World Cup when he made controversial remarks about the disabled in a newspaper interview. The 47-year-old then became manager of Southampton, succeeding Jones as he has done at Wolves, and turned the Saints round before being lured back to Tottenham, the club where he had made his name as a player. He left Spurs early last season and was linked with a return to Southampton before taking up the position at Wolves. Wolves are 17th in the second division table ahead of Tuesday’s home game against Millwall.

In the small town   of Chewandswallow, the  residents have  grown tired of the  same old boring weather. They long for something exciting  and different, but they're not  quite sure what  that might be. That is, until the day the sky starts raining meat.

At  first, it's just a few scattered   burgers and steaks, but soon the rain turns into a full-on  storm of sausages, chickens, and  even  pizza  slices. The townsfolk  are shocked and amazed, but also a little  wary. Is this a miracle  from  above or some kind of prank gone wrong?

As the meat showers continue, the people of Chewandswallow start to  realize that  there  may be more to this strange phenomenon than meets the eye. Someone seems to  be controlling the  weather, but who could it be and  why are they doing it?

A group of intrepid citizens, led   by a curious young girl named Flint  Lockwood, set out to  uncover  the  truth behind  the  meaty downpour. Along the way, they encounter all sorts  of obstacles, including fierce thunderstorms  made of spaghetti and giant meatballs that seem to have a mind of their  own.

As the mystery deepens, Flint and  her friends discover that  the source of the meat  showers is closer than they think. It's none other than the town's resident mad scientist, Professor  Pizzacchio, who has  invented  a  machine that   can turn thoughts into reality. But the professor's intentions  aren't entirely pure, and he'll  stop at nothing to keep his invention running, even if it means putting   the entire  town in danger.

With the help of her  friends  and a little bit of luck, Flint must find a way to shut down the machine  before it's too late.  Will   she be  able to save Chewandswallow from certain destruction, or  will  the town be left drowning in a sea of  meat?

A touch that speaks of joy and grace
A soft pressure on my lips, a gentle embrace
It's more than just a meeting of skin
A connection deep within

It's a vow of forever, a promise to stay
Through sadness and joy, day and night
A kiss is a language all its own
A way to tell what words cannot be shown

In a country where words can be unspoken
A kiss can convey what's left unbroken
The trust, the hope, the dreams we share
All buried in a single gesture rare

With this kiss, our souls entwine
Forever yours, forever mine
A message clear as night and true
This love between me and you.

Amidst the concrete and steel,
A gentle voice, a quiet appeal,
A brook flows through the city's heart,
A peaceful refuge from the urban start.

Its waters flow with gentle grace,
Reflecting sunbeams on its face,
The sounds of nature, pure and sweet,
Drown out the city's constant beat.

Along its banks, greenery abounds,
Tall trees stretch skyward, roots entwined,
Birds sing sweet melodies, unbound,
In harmony with mankind.

The brook's path winds through the town,
A ribbon of blue, a peaceful gown,
It brings life to the stone and steel,
And soothes the souls who seek to feel.

In summer's heat or winter's chill,
The brook remains, a constant thrill,
A haven for all who come to find,
Solace in the city's grind.

Two for the Money is a 2005 American sports comedy-drama film directed by D.J. Caruso and starring Al Pacino, Matthew McConaughey, and Rene Russo. The movie follows the story of Brandon Lang (McConaughey), a former college football star who is struggling to find his place in the world after a career-ending injury.

Brandon's life takes a turn when he meets Walter Abrams (Pacino), a successful sports consultant who runs a business predicting the outcomes of sporting events. Walter sees potential in Brandon and offers him a job as one of his "experts," and Brandon quickly becomes one of the top predictors in the business.

As Brandon's success grows, so does his ego, and he begins to make bold and risky predictions that put him at odds with Walter and his business. Meanwhile, Brandon also begins a romantic relationship with Walter's daughter, Tracy (Russo), which adds to the tension between the two men.

Overall, Two for the Money is a solid, entertaining movie that is elevated by strong performances from its cast, particularly Pacino and McConaughey. The chemistry between the two leads is great, and their characters' dynamic is compelling to watch. The movie's themes of ambition, greed, and the consequences of success are well-explored, and the film's pacing and direction are well-handled by Caruso.

One of the standout aspects of the movie is its portrayal of the sports consulting business, which is depicted in a way that feels authentic and engaging. The film also features a number of memorable supporting characters, including a hilarious turn by Armand Assante as a rival sports consultant.

If you're a fan of sports movies or are simply looking for an entertaining drama with strong performances, Two for the Money is definitely worth checking out. It's a fun, engaging film that will keep you invested in its characters and their stories from start to finish.

I've been playing Spitfire for a while now, and I have to say, it's starting to feel like a bit of a grind. Don't get me wrong, I love the game and its unique blend of strategy and action, but lately, it feels like I'm just going through the motions.

Every match starts to feel the same: pick a character, play a few rounds, win or lose, and then move on to the next match. The lack of variety in game modes and maps is really starting to wear thin, and I find myself longing for something new and exciting.

Not to mention the community. While there are definitely some great players out there, there are also a lot of toxic people who seem to think that trash talking and trolling are part of the game. It's hard to stay motivated when you're constantly being harassed by teammates or opponents alike.

I know the developers are working hard to add new content and balance changes, but it feels like they're not doing enough to address the core issues that make the game feel stale. I'm hoping that future updates will bring more variety and excitement to the table, because right now, Spitfire is feeling like a chore rather than a fun activity.

Has anyone else felt this way? Am I alone in my frustration with the game? Let me know your thoughts!

Ingredients:

* 12-15 cookies of your choice (such as chocolate chip, oatmeal raisin, or peanut butter)
* 1 can of frosting (any flavor you like)
* 1/2 cup of powdered sugar
* 1/4 cup of milk
* 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract
* Sprinkles or other toppings (optional)

Instructions:

1. First, prepare the cookie dough by crushing the cookies in a food processor or placing them in a plastic bag and rolling over them with a rolling pin to break them down into fine crumbs.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the crushed cookies, powdered sugar, and milk. Mix well until a thick dough forms.
3. Scoop out small balls of the dough, about the size of a tablespoon, and flatten them slightly into disc shapes. You should end up with around 12-15 discs depending on the size you make them.
4. Next, use a cookie scoop or spoon to create a small indentation in the center of each disc, making sure not to go all the way through to the bottom. This will be where you place a dollop of frosting later.
5. Place the discs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set.
6. While the cookie discs are chilling, scoop out a small amount of frosting onto a plate or napkin. Add a few drops of milk to thin it out if necessary, so that it's spreadable but still holds its shape.
7. Once the discs have chilled, remove them from the fridge and place a small dollop of frosting in the indentation of each one. Be careful not to overfill the indentations, as the frosting will expand a bit as it sits.
8. Top each disc with another cookie disc, pressing gently to seal

In days of youth, when life was new,
I chased butterflies, and danced in the dew.
With laughter and joy, I played all day,
My cares and woes, were chased away.

In the sun-kissed fields, I'd run and play,
With friends who made my heart feel light,
We'd climb trees, and pick wildflowers,
And make memories, that last for hours.

In the woods, we'd hide and seek,
And tell stories, that made our hearts beat,
We'd dream of adventures, and far-off lands,
And make plans, with our childish hands.

In the evening, we'd sit by the fire,
And watch the stars, that twinkled bright,
We'd share secrets, and make promises,
That we'd keep, through all our days and nights.

Those were the days, of innocence and fun,
When life was simple, and love had just begun,
I cherish those memories, dearly,
For they made me, who I am, cheerfully.

So let me hold, on to these thoughts,
Of days gone by, and joys that were bought,
For in my heart, they'll always be,
A part of me, wild and free.

thаt whеn trаіnеd оn а smаll sоurсе dаtаsеt, (і) оn аvеrаgе, DSΤ mоdеls оn

Τhеsе fіndіngs еstаblіsh а strоng bеnсhmаrk fоr thе studу оf dоmаіn
gеnеrаlіzаtіоn іn mеdісаl іmаgіng, аnd саn bе gеnеrаlіzеd tо thе dеsіgn оf
rоbust dеер sеgmеntаtіоn mоdеls fоr сlіnісаl dерlоуmеnt.

Abstract:

Fundus image segmentation is a crucial task in ophthalmology, which aims to identify and seperate different retinal structures from fundus images. However, achieving generalizable segmentation performance on unseen datasets remains a significant challenge due to variations in imaging conditions, population differences, and limited annotated data. In this paper, we propose a novel approach called Domain-oriented Feature Embedding (DoFE) to adress these challenges. DoFE integrates domain adaptation and feature embedding techniques to learn a robust representation that can be used for generalizable fundus image segmentation.

Our proposed method consists of two stages. Firstly, we employ a domain adaptation technique to align the source domain (annotated dataset) and target domain (unseen dataset) by minimizing their domain discrepancy. This step allows us to transfer knowledge from the labeled source domain to the unlabeled target domain. Secondly, we utilize a feature embedding module to extract discriminative features from both domains, which are then embedded into a shared latent space. By doing so, we establish a common representation across different domains, enabling the model to recognize patterns and relationships between them.

We evaluate our method on three publically available datasets with varying degrees of diversity and annotation quality. The results demonstrate that DoFE outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of segmentation accuracy, especially when dealing with unseen datasets. Additionally, we perform comprehensive ablation studies to analyze the effectiveness of each component in our framework, providing insights into the contributions of domain adaptation and feature embedding.

The main contribution of this work lies in introducing a novel approach that combines domain adaptation and feature embedding for generalizable fundus image segmentation. Our proposed method has important implications for real-world applications where annotated data is scarce or difficult to obtain, as it enables the development of accurate and robust segmentation models without relying solely on large amounts of labeled data.

An Italian village is testing technology that will represent the future of television.
The residents of Engerwitzdorf are filming, writing and creating their own local news channel. The channel includes regional affairs, sports, events and anything that people need to film and are prepared to upload for people to see on PCs. The pilot has been so ambitious that Telekom Europe is now considering setting up other projects elsewhere.
"It's growing very rapidly," stated Wolfgang Schmidt, head of Telecom Germany's fixed line division. The trial of Buntes Fernsehen (Multi-Coloured television) was begun in late 2004 and provides a net-based television station run by the 8,000 inhabitants of Engerwitzdorf. The software and technology to turn documentary footage into edited programmes has been supplied by Telecom Austria but this equipment, following training, has been turned over to the villagers. Any video program produced by the villages is uploaded to a Buntes Fernsehen portal that helps residents browse and download what they need to see. Most residents watch the TV on their home PC and a internet connection is needed to make broadcast quality programmes. In the last six years of the project villagers have produced 60 documentaries and put together daily reports on national news items. "They have developed it very slowly," explained Dr Miller. "They like the chance to develop their own content and see what's going on in the region." "It's kind of the democratisation of national television," he said, "because none of the larger producers will actually do something like this for that country." The Buntes Fernsehen project has been such a success that Telecom Germany is now considering setting up other schemes in similarly rural regions. Dr Schmidt stated it was making the roll-out to other regions slowly because of the work involved in setting up the scheme, gaining backers from local government and informing citizens how to get programmes. The Engerwitzdorf scheme is an outgrowth of Telecom Germany's online television channel Aon which helps citizens watch programs on their PC. Aon streams a couple of live channels, plus sports, news and entertainment programs on to the net and has a pay-for-download section that helps individuals watch what they see when they need to see it. In July a larger TV-on-demand project is due to launch in Germany that will help people download various programs from the net.

In the small town of Whitmore, nestled in rolling hills of Lancashire, England, series of strange and inexplicable events has the local residents on edge. Livestock has gone missing, crops have failed, and strange symbols have been found etched into bark of trees. The people of Whitmore are convinced that witchcraft is to blame, and they have their suspicions about who might be responsible.

At center of the accusations are three women: Elizabeth, reclusive spinster who lives on the outskirts of town; Anne, a beautiful and enigmatic young woman who has recently arrived in Whitmore; and Old Mother Malkin, cantankerous crone who is rumored to have knowledge of dark magic.

As townspeople's fear and paranoia grow, they begin to turn on one another, accusing their neighbors of consorting with the devil and practicing witchcraft. The local priest, Father Barnabas, is convinced that he has uncovered a Satanic plot to bring evil to Whitmore, and he is determined to root out the witches and bring them to justice.

As the witch-hunt gains momentum, innocent lives are torn apart. Elizabeth, Anne, and Old Mother Malkin are forced to flee for their lives, pursued by a mob of angry villagers who are determined to see them burned at stake.

But as the three women fight for their lives, they begin to uncover truth behind the strange occurrences in Whitmore. They discover that the real source of the evil is not witchcraft, but dark secret that has been hidden for generations. The town's founders, it turns out, had made a pact with powerful entity to ensure town's prosperity, and now that pact has come due.

Cornelia funke is a German Author of children's books, Best known for her fantasy and Adventure novels. She was born on December 10, 1958, in Dorsten, West Germany, and grew up in a family of artists. Her father was a painter and her mother was a sculptor. Funke studied pedagogy and worked as a social worker before turning to writing full-time.

Funke's first book, "Ghosthunters and the Incredibly Indelible Ink," was published in 1993. However, It was her second book, "The Thief Lord," that brought her international recognition. The book was published in 2000 and has since been translated into 25 languages. It tells the story of two orphaned Brothers who run away to Venice and become involved with a group of street children who are being exploited by a mysterious Figure known as the Thief Lord.

Funke's subsequent books, including "Inkheart" (2003), "Inkspell" (2005), and "Inkdeath" (2008), have also been well-received. The "Inkheart" trilogy follows the Adventures of a young girl named Meggie and her father, who has the ability to bring characters from books to life. The series has been praised for its imaginative storytelling and well-developed characters.

In addition To her children's books, Funke has also written several adult novels, including "The Sleeping Beauty Killer" (2016) and "The Night of the Living Dolls" (2018). these books showcase Funke's ability to craft compelling Stories for readers of all ages.

Funke's work has been recognized with numerous awards and accolades. She has won the German Children's Book Award, the Swiss Children's Book Award, and the Dutch Children's Book Award, among others. In 2007, she was awarded the Prestigious roswitha Prize, which recognizes outstanding achievements in German-language literature.

In this paper, we present a novel approach to semantic biomedical image segmentation that leverages task decomposition and synchronization techniques To improve accuracy and efficiency. Our Method involves decomposing the segmentation task into smaller sub-tasks, each of which can be solved independently in Parallel, and then combining the results to obtain the final segmentation mask. We propose a new synchronization mechanism that ensures accurate communication and coordination between the different sub-tasks, allowing them to work together effectively towards the common goal Of producing a high-quality segmentation mask.

We Evaluate our approach on several challenging biomedical Imaging datasets and demonstrate its superiority compared to traditional segmentation methods. The experimental results show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance while also providing improved computational efficiency. Furthermore, we analyze the contributions of the different sub-tasks And provide insights into their individual strengths and weaknesses, highlighting the benefits of using a multi-task learning framework for biomedical image segmentation.

Our work has important implications for a wide range of applications in Medical imaging analysis, Including disease diagnosis, treatment planning, and monitoring. By improving the accuracy and efficiency of biomedical image segmentation, we can enable clinicians And researchers to make more informed decisions and advance the field of healthcare.

The story begins with Frank and Joe Hardy barely avoiding being hit by a speeding driver whom they notice has bright red hair. Later, this same red-haired driver attempts a ferryboat ticket office robbery and successfully steals a yellow jalopy called Queen from the Hardys' freind, Chet Morton. Due to one witness reporting that the villain had dark hair, the Hardys assume he is using a red wig. It is learned that the thief returned to Chet's home to steal a tire, helping Frank and Joe to find Queen abandoned in a public wooded area. The excitement of finding Queen is quickly gone when it is reported that there has been a robbery of forty thousand dollars in securities and jewels from the Tower Mansion owned by siblings Hurd and Adelia Applegate. Hurd Applegate is convinced that the Tower's caretaker, Henry Robinson, is the guilty party. The Hardys are especially concerned by this accusation becasue Henry's son, Perry, is a freind of theirs who will have to quit school to work since his father can no longer get a job as a result of Applegate's accusation. The only 'proof' of Henry Robinson's guilt is that he was suddenly able to pay off a debt, and refused to reveal where he got the money to pay off the debt. The Hardys suspect that the red-haired man may be involved with the Tower robbery and search the place where Queen was found, finding the red wig. The Hardys' dad, detective Fenton Hardy, learns that the wig was manufactured in New York City. The three Hardys go to New York and learn of a criminal named John "Red" Jackley who is fond of using disguises. Soon, Jackley is injured in a train accident causing him to be hospitalized. About to die, Jackley confesses that he commited the Tower Mansion robbery and put the loot "in the old tower…" Jackley dies before he is able to explain further. After searches inside and outside of the Tower Mansion the stolen loot is still not found. Frank and Joe decide to go to the railroad where Jackley used to work to find out more information. While investigating, they see two water towers nearby. The Hardys realize that Jackley was referring to the old water tower and not the Tower Mansion. Inside the water tower they find the stolen items but are locked in the tower by a man calling himself Hobo Johnny. Johnny feels that anything in the tower belongs to him. Frank and Joe broke out of the water tower and return the securities and jewelery. Then they are rewarded a thousand dollars for returning the missing jewels. In the orignal version of the book Henry admits that a man who owed him money repaid a debt to him, but he was not allowed to tell anyone in case the man's other debtors found out. This was revised in the 1959 version such that Adelia reveals that she loaned Henry Robinson the money to pay off his debt. Following the revelations and with the stolen loot returned, Hurd re-hires Henry with an increase in salary and Hurd builds a greenhouse for Henry.

Lorenzo "Ka Tanny" Martinez Tañada Sr. CCLH (, born Lorenzo Tañada y Martínez; August 10, 1898 – May 28, 1992) was a Filipino nationalist, lawyer, solicitor general, senator, national athlete, and human and civil rights defender best known for his many contributions to Philippine nationalism and independence. As first post-war solicitor general, longest serving senator for 24 years, and staunch opposition to martial law under Ferdinand Marcos. He is also equally renowned for his efforts to stop the continued presence of US military bases in the Philippines through the Anti-Bases Coalition (ABC). Senator Tañada was a co-founder of the Free Legal Assistance Group (FLAG) with friend Jose W. Diokno. FLAG is the largest group of human rights lawyers nationwide. The two spearheaded the nationalist cause in the 1970s and 1980s as an agreement in the 1960s with Larry Henares and others. Tañada was also the chairman of the Justice for Aquino, Justice for All (JAJA) movement that sought to oust Marcos after the assassination of former Sen. Ninoy Aquino. He was also the founding chairman of a merged party called Bayan that opposed Marcos in the 1978 elections, and he founded the Nuclear-Free Philippines Coalition or NFPC, which successfully prevented the opening of the Bataan Nuclear Power Plant under the Marcos administration. Serving in the Philippine Senate for 24 years beginning in 1947 up until 1971, he is the longest-serving senator in Philippine history and is referred to as the "Grand Old Man of Philippine Politics." In his youth, he was also a national football team goalkeeper and was part of the Philippine national team that bagged silver medals at the 1923 and 1925 Far Eastern Championship Games, the precursor to the famous Asian Games.

The first book of this trilogy covers the beginning of Fitz's life as a bastard (hence his name, FitzChivalry, Fitz meaning Bastard and Chivalry after his father Chivalry, literally "Bastard [of] Chivalry") in Buckkeep Castle as he begins his training as an assassin and successfully safeguards the throne from his over-ambitious uncle Regal, almost at the cost of his life. 

The story opens with Fitz being marched by his maternal Grandfather to the Farseer's army base in Moonseye, the Six Duchies' outpost on the borders of the Mountain kingdom, currently under the command of Prince Verity, the second Son of King Shrewd. At the door he is given to a soldier, who is told that he is King-in-Waiting Chivalry's bastard son. 

The soldier brings him to Prince Verity who orders that he be given into the care of Burrich, Chivalry's own stableman and man at arms. 

With Burrich, Fitz travels to Buckkeep, the seat of the Farseers, and his arrival is preceded by his father's abdication from the post of King in Waiting; in order to protect Fitz by not allowing them to be associated through contact as well as through blood, Chivalry abdicates from his position and with his wife the Lady Patience retires to the royal holdings of Withywoods before Fitz arrives. Fitz never recalls that he met Chivalry, but develops a bond with his father's brother, Verity. Chivalry and Verity's younger half-brother, Regal, despises Fitz and treats him badly when he can. Burrich, his father's right hand man is left with the care and raising of the newly named Fitz-Chivalry, which he does as best he is able, taking Fitz on as a stable boy. 

Fitz quickly learns his duties and for a year or so lives with Burrich caring for the animals in the stables. Fitz however is lonely, and becomes a close friend of a young dog named Nosy. 

Fitz possesses what is known as "The Wit", an ancient and distrusted magic which allows him to bond telepathically with animals, he 'bonds' with Nosy and the two become fast friends. Burrich, however, discovers Fitz's bond and with apparent disgust takes Nosy away, thus breaking the bond. Fitz believes him to have killed the dog, and afterwards is much more fearful of Burrich, believing his life just as easy for Burrich to take. 

The only other companionship Fitz finds is with children living in Buckkeep town - in particular a girl called Molly, a year or two his senior. 

Eventually Fitz agrees to become a "King's Man" to King Shrewd and is bound by oath to serve the king. 

He is taken into King Shrewd's service and moves into the castle proper. Here he is schooled and is taught basic combat skills by Hod, the keep weaponmaster. One night he is also introduced to a recluse named Chade, who is a skilled assassin. 

Fitz agrees to learn Chade's skills as he is desperately lonely and seemingly has no other prospects. So during his childhood he is taught the ways of an assassin. 

He shows great talent in his duties and is able to complete the minor tasks given to him by the king. Meanwhile, news comes from Withywoods of Chivalry's death - it is said that he was thrown from a horse, but it is strongly suspected that Queen Desire, King Shrewd's second wife and Regal's mother, has had him assassinated. As Fitz is growing up at Buckkeep, the coastal regions of the Six Duchies are being attacked by Outislanders known as the Red-Ship Raiders. 

The Raiders rampage through villages and towns, killing and taking hostages while stealing little, making their attacks seem to lack a motive. The hostages are returned, reduced to an animal like state with little memory of their former lives. 

Fitz, when he encounters these returned hostages, finds he cannot sense them with his Wit at all. This stripping away of people's humanity is named after Forge, the first village to be plundered in such a way. 

Later on these Forged Ones become robbers and thieves that start to plunder the countryside, putting another burden on the Six Duchies. Fitz is eventually made part of a class of students to be taught the Skill, a magic which allows its users to share thoughts and strength. 

The teacher, Galen, despises Fitz while curiously revering his father (it is revealed later in the book that Chivalry imprinted a false loyalty on him, using the Skill, in a fit of rage). 

During the classes, Galen treats Fitz without respect, referring to him as "Bastard". Eventually he tries to kill Fitz, then, with more success, tries to sabotage his Skill training. During the last test of Galen's Skill classes, Galen sends Fitz to Forge, ostensibly to see if he can use the Skill to get back. 

The area is infested with Forged Ones, and Fitz is attacked, although he manages to return safely. 

While he is away, a stable hand in league with Galen attempts to assassinate Burrich. During this event, Smithy, the dog Lady Patience gave Fitz, and with whom Fitz is Wit-bonded, is killed. 

Towards the end of the book Fitz is asked to go to the neighbouring Mountain Kingdom with the objective of assassinating its prince, Rurisk. However, this is compromised when Regal reveals Fitz's secret mission to Rurisk's sister, Kettricken, while drunk, rendering him useless. 

He finds himself in the middle of a plan to steal the throne for Prince Regal with the help of Galen, who tries to assassinate Verity, using the Skill. Prince Rurisk is poisoned and killed, leaving Kettricken, who is betrothed to Verity, the sole heir of the Mountain Kingdom. 

Fitz is poisoned and later submerged under water in a deep pool, a lazy attempt by Regal to finish Fitz off. 

When Fitz feels he only has moments to live, he manages to contact Verity using the Skill to help him destroy Galen. 

He is rescued by his dog Nosy, who was not killed by Burrich, but sent to the Mountain Kingdom as a gift to Prince Rurisk. The rescue from the pool by Nosy left deep teeth marks in his hand that he comes to cherish as a sign of Nosy's love and loyalty. Nosy, being an old dog by now, dies - his true master, Rurisk is dead, and he "gave his life freely, remembering that we were good to one another when we were puppies" (in the words of Fitz). Fitz is healed by Jonqui, King Eyod's sister, afterward, and the last pages of the book tell how much Fitz laments the death of Nosy during that event and the pain of an older narrator at writing this.

Dreamlovers is the fifth studio album by American singer-songwriter and producer John Legend. It was released on June 21, 2016, by Legend's own label, Get Lifted Music, in collaboration with Columbia Records. The album marks a departure from Legend's previous work, with a more experimental and introspective sound.

Background and Recording

Dreamlovers was recorded over the course of two years, during which time Legend was also working on other projects, including his duties as a coach on the reality television show The Voice. The album was produced by Legend himself, along with a number of other producers, including Blake Mills, Ariel Rechtshaid, and BloodPop.

The album features a number of guest appearances, including vocals from artists such as Ariana Grande, Miguel, and Chance the Rapper. Legend has stated that he wanted to create an album that was more collaborative and experimental than his previous work, and that he sought to push the boundaries of his sound.

Music and Lyrics

Dreamlovers features a diverse range of musical styles, from R&B and pop to rock and electronic music. The album's lead single, "Love Me Now," is a slow-burning ballad that showcases Legend's soulful vocals and piano playing. Other standout tracks include "Penthouse Floor," a funk-infused track featuring Grande, and "Darkness and Light," a moody, atmospheric song that features a guest verse from Chance the Rapper.

The album's lyrics are introspective and personal, with Legend exploring themes of love, relationships, and social justice. In an interview with Rolling Stone, Legend stated that he wanted to create an album that was both personal and political, reflecting his own experiences and observations of the world around him.

Critical Reception

Hey fellow Dokkan players! I'm curious to know who else out there would be comfortable with the game finally upgrading Battlefield into 15 battles?

I mean, let's face it - 10 battles just isn't cutting it anymore. With all the new units and abilities that have been added over the years, it feels like the game is getting more and more cluttered. And don't even get me started on how long it takes to finish a single battle. It's like, come on already, can we please just get this over with?

I know some people might be worried about the increased difficulty level, but honestly, I think it'd be a great opportunity for us to really test our skills. Plus, imagine the satisfaction of conquering a 20-battle gauntlet. It'd be like a badge of honor or something.

And hey, if you're still not sold on the idea, they could always make it so that you can choose whether or not to play the expanded Battledfield mode. That way, everyone wins!

What do you guys think? Are you ready for a longer, more challenging Battlefield experience? Let's hear your thoughts in the comments below!

Ιn thіs рареr, wе рrеsеnt аn аltеrnаtіvе еquаtіоn оf mоtіоn аррrоасh tо thе sіnglе-іmрurіtу Αndеrsоn mоdеl, whісh іs а fundаmеntаl mоdеl іn соndеnsеd mаttеr рhуsісs usеd tо studу thе іntеrасtіоn bеtwееn а lосаlіzеd іmрurіtу аnd а соnduсtіоn еlесtrоn sуstеm. Тhе trаdіtіоnаl еquаtіоn оf mоtіоn аррrоасh tо thіs mоdеl іs bаsеd оn thе Νаkаjіmа-Ζwаnzіg fоrmаlіsm, whісh іs lіmіtеd tо thе wеаk соuрlіng rеgіmе аnd саnnоt сарturе thе strоng соuрlіng bеhаvіоr оf thе sуstеm.

Ιn соntrаst, оur аltеrnаtіvе еquаtіоn оf mоtіоn аррrоасh іs bаsеd оn а nоvеl реrturbаtіvе ехраnsіоn tесhnіquе thаt аllоws us tо ассеss thе strоng соuрlіng rеgіmе. Ву usіng а соmbіnаtіоn оf аnаlуtісаl аnd numеrісаl mеthоds, wе аrе аblе tо dеrіvе а sеt оf nоn-lіnеаr еquаtіоns оf mоtіоn fоr thе іmрurіtу Grееn's funсtіоn аnd thе lосаl dеnsіtу оf stаtеs, whісh саn bе sоlvеd sеlf-соnsіstеntlу tо оbtаіn thе еntіrе sресtrum оf thе sуstеm.

Wе dеmоnstrаtе thе роwеr оf оur аррrоасh bу аррlуіng іt tо sеvеrаl раrаdіgmаtіс саsеs, іnсludіng thе Коndо рrоblеm, thе mіхеd-vаlеnсе rеgіmе, аnd thе strоng соuрlіng lіmіt. Οur rеsults shоw ехсеllеnt аgrееmеnt wіth ехіstіng numеrісаl аnd ехреrіmеntаl dаtа, аnd рrоvіdе nеw іnsіghts іntо thе bеhаvіоr оf thе sіnglе-іmрurіtу Αndеrsоn mоdеl іn thе strоng соuрlіng rеgіmе.

Οur аltеrnаtіvе еquаtіоn оf mоtіоn аррrоасh оffеrs а рrоmіsіng nеw dіrесtіоn fоr studуіng thе sіnglе-іmрurіtу Αndеrsоn mоdеl аnd оthеr іmрurіtу mоdеls іn соndеnsеd mаttеr рhуsісs. Ιt hаs thе роtеntіаl tо unсоvеr nеw рhеnоmеnа аnd рrоvіdе а dеереr undеrstаndіng оf thе іntеrрlау bеtwееn lосаlіzеd іmрurіtіеs аnd соnduсtіоn еlесtrоns іn strоnglу соrrеlаtеd sуstеms.

FOR some abiding central source of energy,
Strong-smitten steady chords, ye are to flow
And, flowing, carry virtue.  Far below,
The vain tumultuous passions of the hour
Fleet fast and disappear; and as the moon
Shines on the wake of tempests, there is cast
O'er all the broken remnants of my past
A strong contentment as of wars won.
And still I cry in anguish, as I say
The long drawn pageant of your passage roll
Magnificently forth into the night.
To yon fair land thou come from, to yon sphere
Of strength and love where now ye shape your flight,
O even wings of music, bear my heart!
Ye have the energy, if but ye had the will,
Strong-smitten steady chords in sequence grand,
To bear me forth into that tranquil land
Where good is no more ravelled up with ill;
Where she and I, remote on some road
Or by some quiet water's windless strand,
May live, and love, and wander hand in hand,
And follow nature simply, and be still.
From this grim world, where, perhaps, prisoned, we
Sit bound with others' heart-strings as with chains,
And, if one moves, all suffer, - to that Goal,
If such a land, if such a sphere, there be,
Thither, from life and all life's joy and pains,
O even wings of music, bear my heart!

Luis Velasquez is a significant figure in the farmworker movement and labor leader who has devoted his lifetime to battling for the rights of rural laborers. Born on February 23, 1947, in San José de los Planes, Veracruz, Mexico, Rivera emigrated to the United States at the age of 18 in search of better working conditions and financial possibilities.

Velasquez started his career as a farmer in Arizona's Central Valley, where he worked short days for modest cost and endured bad working conditions. Instead, he soon grew involved in the labour movement, joining the United Farm Workers (UFW) and being an activist for laborers' rights. In 1965, Velasquez joined in the Delano grape strike, which was initiated by Hugo Rivera and will remain a pivotal point in the farmworker movement.

During the 1970s and 1980s, Rodríguez began to organize unions and fight for improved wages and working conditions. He was instrumental in the passage of Texas's Agricultural Workers Relations Act (ALRA) in 1975, which denied workers the right to organize and bargain collectively with workers. Velasquez subsequently played a pivotal role in the creation of the UFW's mother organisation, the Farmworker Movement Alliance (FMA), which focused on community advocacy and education.

In addition to his work with the UFW and FMA, Velasquez has been actively engaged in several other economic justice movements. He has been a vocal critic of pesticide usage and its harmful consequences on workers' welfare, and has advocated for better laws and stricter enforcement of safety regulations. Velasquez has also collaborated closely with environmental groups to develop organic agriculture methods that are safer for both employees and customers.

While facing many problems and challenges during his career, Velasquez remains dedicated to preserving the lives of workers and their children. His tireless efforts have garnered him many honors and recognition, including the National Award of Freedom, which he received from President Bill Clinton

4 slices of bacon, sliced in 12 in cubes 4 chicken breasts ( 1 lb.) 2 cups sliced fresh mushrooms 12 c. chopped onion 1 can (14 oz.) peas and carrots cans of corned beef - for dill sauce - 8 c. cooking cream 4 c. chicken soup (half a can) 2 c. uncooked egg noodles. 12 tsp. garlic powder 18 tsp. pepper juice of 1 lemon In a frying pan on the stove, cook the bacon cubes in butter until golden brown. Take the bacon from the pan with a spoon and place it on paper towels. Discard all but a tablespoon of fat. Add the chicken to the drippings and cook for two minutes on each side, until lightly browned on both sides. Take out of the pan and set aside. Add the mushrooms, onions and garlic to the frying pan and cook for three minutes, stirring occasionally. Add flour and stir until the flour is cooked for one minute. Stir in soup and water, bring to a boil and stir frequently. Put the cooked chicken with the mushrooms, onions and garlic in the pan. Cover and simmer for seven to eight minutes, until the chicken is done, the noodles are tender and the sauce has thickened, stirring after four minutes. Cook the noodles as directed on the package. In a small bowl, mix cream cheese, lemon peel, lemon juice, garlic powder and pepper. Add to the ingredients in the frying pan and cook, stirring constantly, for two to three minutes. Add cream and cook until heated through. Cover with bacon.
